pattern: S + have the qualifications for + N
simple: He doesn't have the skills required for the job.

example: I have no regrets about the decisions I made, even though things didn't go perfectly.
pattern: S + have no regrets about + N/V-ing
simple: She has some regrets about leaving school early.

type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: We should have deep sympathy for those who lost their homes in the devastating earthquake.
simple: I have great sympathy for the victims.

example: He usually has a quick shower before going to work in the morning to stay refreshed.
simple: She is having a bath right now.

example: After taking the medicine for months, he has developed a tolerance to it and needs a higher dose.
pattern: S + have a tolerance for/to + N
simple: Teachers must have a tolerance for mistakes.

example: Do you have time to grab a cup of coffee with me after the meeting?
simple: I don't have time to watch TV.

example: The counselor gave the student some useful advice on choosing a college major.
pattern: S + give + O + some advice on + N/V-ing
simple: My father gave me good advice.

example: Can you give an example to illustrate your point more clearly?
pattern: S + give an example to + V
simple: The teacher gave an example to explain the rule.

example: She gave birth to a healthy baby boy early this morning.
simple: The new policy gave birth to many problems.

example: Please give me a second chance to prove my ability to the team.
pattern: S + give + O + a chance to + V
simple: Everyone deserves to be given a chance.

example: We should give credit to the team members who worked hard behind the scenes.
simple: He took all the credit for the success.

example: The witness was asked to give evidence in court regarding the car accident.
pattern: S + be asked to give evidence
simple: The scientist gave evidence to support his theory.

example: The manager gave a detailed explanation for the unexpected delay of the project.
pattern: S + give an explanation for + N
simple: Can you give an explanation for your absence?

example: Could you please give me a hand with these heavy boxes?
pattern: S + give + O + a hand with + N
simple: Let me give you a hand.

example: The successful trial of the new medical treatment gives hope to patients suffering from the rare disease.
simple: His words gave us hope.

example: Before leaving for the airport, she gave her mother a warm hug.
simple: He gave his son a kiss goodnight.

example: His confident speech gave the audience the impression that he was well-prepared.
pattern: S + give + O + the impression that + clause
simple: You give the impression of being strict.

example: Modern education gives great importance to the development of critical thinking skills.
simple: They give equal importance to sports and academics.

example: The nurse will give you an injection to help relieve the severe pain in your back.
simple: The doctor gave him an injection.

example: The severe car crash gave him a harsh lesson about the importance of driving safely.
simple: Failure gives us a valuable lesson.

example: Employees are required to give two weeks' notice before they quit their jobs.
pattern: S + must give notice before + V-ing
simple: You have to give notice to the landlord.

example: Since it was raining heavily, my coworker kindly offered to give me a ride home.
pattern: S + give + O + a ride/lift to + 地方
simple: Can you give me a lift to the station?

example: Everyone in the meeting is encouraged to give an opinion on the new marketing strategy.
pattern: S + give an opinion on + N
simple: He gave his honest opinion.

example: The scholarship gives poor students an opportunity to pursue higher education.
pattern: N + gives + O + an opportunity to + V
simple: This job gives me an opportunity to learn.

example: The famous band will give a live performance at the stadium tonight.
pattern: S + give a performance at + N
simple: The actors gave a wonderful performance.

example: The teacher gave the students permission to leave the classroom early.
pattern: S + give + O + permission to + V
simple: My parents gave me permission to go.

example: The company gives preference to applicants with relevant work experience.
simple: They give preference to local candidates.

example: The government must give priority to protecting the environment over economic development.
simple: Safety should be given priority.

example: Please give me a reply as soon as possible so I can finalize the schedule.
pattern: Please give me a reply by + 時間
simple: He refused to give an answer.

example: The unfair policy gave rise to widespread protests across the country.
simple: The new rules gave rise to confusion.

phrase: give a talk / a speech / a lecture
example: The professor was invited to give a speech on global warming at the international conference.
pattern: S + give a speech on + 題目
simple: The president gave a speech on TV.

example: The actors returned to the stage to take a bow after the successful performance.
pattern: S + return to the stage to take a bow
simple: The singer took a bow as the audience clapped.

chinese: 受到打擊 / 遭受重創
example: The local tourism industry took a severe blow during the global pandemic.
pattern: S + took a severe blow + 時間/原因
simple: His confidence took a beating after the failure.

example: You have been studying for three hours; it's time to take a short break.
pattern: S + need to take a break/holiday
simple: We are planning to take a holiday in Japan.

example: Please excuse me for a moment; I need to take an urgent phone call.
pattern: S + need to take a phone call
simple: He stepped outside to take a call.

example: Teenagers should be educated about the terrible consequences of taking illegal drugs.
pattern: S + warn O + against taking drugs
simple: Taking drugs will ruin your health and life.

example: It is generally advised to take regular exercise to maintain your physical health.
simple: Taking exercise helps reduce stress.

example: I take strong exception to your unfair comments about my hardworking team.
simple: Many people took exception to the controversial policy.

chinese: 懂暗示 / 開得起玩笑
example: He couldn't take a joke and got really angry when we teased him about his haircut.
simple: She didn't take the hint and stayed for another hour.

example: The new traffic regulations will take effect at the beginning of next month.
pattern: The rule/law + will take effect + 時間
simple: The painkiller took effect quickly.

example: Could you take a look at my essay and check for any grammar mistakes?
pattern: Please take a look at + N
simple: The mechanic took a look at my car.

example: The doctor told me to take this medicine three times a day after meals.
simple: Don't forget to take your medicine.

example: Students are encouraged to take detailed notes during the lecture to remember key points.
simple: He wasn't there, so I took a message for him.

example: Tourists love to take pictures of the beautiful sunset at the beach.
pattern: S + take a photo of + N
simple: Can you take a picture for us?

example: The international film festival will take place in Taipei next year.
pattern: The event + takes place + 地方/時間
simple: The meeting took place yesterday.

example: Starting your own business means you have to be willing to take a risk.
pattern: S + be willing to take a risk
simple: Sometimes you need to take risks to succeed.

example: Please take a seat in the waiting room, and the doctor will be with you shortly.
simple: Everyone took their seats before the show started.

chinese: 搭計程車 / 火車 / 公車
example: Because it was pouring rain, we decided to take a taxi home instead of walking.
pattern: S + take a taxi to + 地方
simple: I usually take the train to work.

example: Every freshman is required to take a basic English course before graduation.
simple: She took a driving test last week.

example: The school nurse took the sick boy's temperature and found he had a high fever.
simple: The doctor will take your temperature first.

chinese: 散步 / 採取步驟 (或走一步)
example: After dinner, my grandparents usually take a walk in the park to digest their food.
pattern: S + take a walk + 地方
simple: We must take a step forward to solve this.

example: This swimming course is specifically designed for absolute beginners.
pattern: S + be designed for absolute beginners
simple: Even an absolute beginner can learn to use this app.

example: Please arrange the files in alphabetical order so they are easier to find.
simple: The names are listed in alphabetical order.

example: Eating a balanced diet is essential for maintaining good physical health.
simple: A balanced diet provides necessary nutrients.

example: When backpacking, you should only pack the bare essentials to keep your bag light.
simple: The refugee camp lacked even the bare essentials.

chinese: 巨大的失望、令人大失所望的事
example: The new superhero movie turned out to be a big disappointment to its fans.
pattern: N + turned out to be a big disappointment
simple: Losing the game was a big disappointment.

example: It would be a big mistake to ignore the doctor's advice regarding your health.
pattern: It would be a big mistake to + V
simple: Making that investment was a big mistake.

example: Everyone knew he was telling a blatant lie, but nobody dared to expose him.
simple: Her excuse was nothing but a blatant lie.

example: Children from broken homes often need extra emotional support from teachers.
pattern: S + come from a broken home
simple: He grew up in a broken home.

example: The actress won the hearts of the audience with her captivating smile.
pattern: S + charm O + with a captivating smile
simple: She welcomed us with a captivating smile.

chinese: 冷淡的接待、冷淡的反應
example: The politician's controversial proposal met with a chilly reception from the public.
pattern: S + met with a chilly reception
simple: His joke got a chilly reception.

example: The new law sends a clear message that drunk driving will not be tolerated.
pattern: S + send a clear message to O
simple: We must give a clear message to the team.

example: Before signing the contract, make sure you have a clear understanding of the terms.
pattern: S + have a clear understanding of + N
simple: We need a clear understanding of the rules.

example: I usually share my deepest secrets only with my close friends.
pattern: S + be a close friend of mine
simple: We have been close friends for ten years.

example: It is common knowledge that water boils at 100 degrees Celsius.
pattern: It is common knowledge that + S + V
simple: The company's financial trouble is common knowledge.

example: After hours of debate, the two sides finally reached a complete agreement.
pattern: S + be in complete agreement on + N
simple: I am in complete agreement with you.

example: Take a deep breath and try to calm down before stepping onto the stage.
pattern: Take a deep breath and + V
simple: She took a deep breath to steady her nerves.

example: The low price was the deciding factor when we chose to buy this car.
pattern: N + is the deciding factor in + N
simple: Weather will be the deciding factor for our trip.

example: The Internet has an enormous influence on how teenagers view the world.
pattern: S + have an enormous influence on + N
simple: Parents have an enormous influence on their kids.

chinese: 明確的目的、特意的目的
example: He traveled to Paris for the express purpose of learning authentic French baking.
pattern: S + V + for the express purpose of + V-ing
simple: She came here for the express purpose of seeing you.

chinese: 公平的交易、公平的待遇
example: The union negotiated with the company to ensure all workers got a fair deal.
simple: I don't think I'm getting a fair deal here.

example: The craftsman takes great pride in his handmade furniture.
pattern: S + take great pride in + N
simple: She feels great pride in her son's success.

example: I missed the beginning of the movie because I was stuck in heavy traffic.
pattern: S + be delayed due to heavy traffic
simple: Heavy traffic is common during rush hour.

example: This brand is famous for producing high quality leather goods.
simple: We demand high quality materials.

example: Even after twenty years, the antique car was still in immaculate condition.
pattern: S + be kept in immaculate condition
simple: The rented house was left in immaculate condition.

example: Many innocent victims lost their lives during the sudden terrorist attack.
pattern: S + claimed the lives of innocent victims
simple: He was just an innocent victim of circumstances.

example: When I am in a hurry in the morning, I just make a cup of instant coffee.
simple: Instant coffee is very convenient.

example: Climate change is considered a key issue that affects global security.
pattern: N + is a key issue in + N
simple: Education is a key issue in the election.

example: Based on all the evidence gathered, this seems to be the only logical conclusion.
simple: We must draw a logical conclusion from the facts.

example: Air pollution has become a major problem in many developing countries.
pattern: N + poses a major problem for + N
simple: Funding is a major problem for the project.

example: I had mixed feelings when I graduated: happy to finish, but sad to leave my friends.
pattern: S + have mixed feelings about + N
simple: She has mixed feelings about the job offer.

example: Choosing which university to attend is a momentous decision for a teenager.
simple: Moving abroad was a momentous decision.

example: Biting your fingernails when you are nervous is a nasty habit to break.
pattern: S + have a nasty habit of + V-ing
simple: He has a nasty habit of interrupting people.

example: Winning the gold medal in the Olympics is a notable achievement for any athlete.
simple: Publishing a book is a notable achievement.

example: There is a noticeable difference in temperature between the city and the mountains.
pattern: There is a noticeable difference between A and B
simple: The new software shows a noticeable difference in speed.

example: The actor received an award for his outstanding performance in the drama.
simple: Congratulations on your outstanding performance!

example: Please make sure to take all your personal belongings with you when leaving the train.
pattern: Keep an eye on your personal belongings.
simple: Do not leave your personal belongings unattended.

chinese: 突出的角色、重要的作用
example: Social media plays a prominent role in shaping teenagers' political views.
pattern: N + plays a prominent role in + N
simple: Education plays a prominent role in society.

example: The government changed its policy due to the strong pressure of public opinion.
pattern: S + be influenced by public opinion
simple: Public opinion is against the new law.

example: Can you give me a rough estimate of how much the repair will cost?
pattern: give a rough estimate of + N
simple: Here is a rough estimate of the timeline.

example: All of your classmates signed this card to wish you a speedy recovery.
simple: He made a speedy recovery from the surgery.

example: After years of freelancing, he finally found a steady job with a regular income.
pattern: S + look for/find a steady job
simple: It's hard to get a steady job nowadays.

example: The tourist spoke English with a strong accent, making it difficult for locals to understand him.
pattern: S + speak with a strong accent
simple: He has a strong accent but speaks fluently.

example: The national park is renowned worldwide for its stunning beauty and rich wildlife.
pattern: S + be struck by the stunning beauty of N
simple: The painting captures the stunning beauty of nature.

example: Keep a tight grip on the railing when you walk down the steep stairs.
pattern: S + keep a tight grip on + N
simple: He held my hand with a tight grip.

chinese: 徹底的瘋狂、極度愚蠢的行為
example: Driving a motorcycle in this terrible typhoon is total madness.
pattern: It is total madness to + V
simple: Spending all your money on clothes is total madness.

example: I only have a vague memory of my early childhood before we moved to Taipei.
pattern: S + have a vague memory of + N
simple: She has a vague memory of meeting him once.

example: Students must provide a valid reason if they want to be absent from the exam.
pattern: S + have a valid reason to + V
simple: You don't have a valid reason to complain.

example: Although he is good at speaking, grammar has always been his weak point.
pattern: S + need to improve one's weak point
simple: Every argument has a weak point.

example: The residents are very angry about the new tax policy.
simple: He was angry about the delay.

example: Many students are anxious about their future after graduation.
simple: She is anxious about the exam results.

example: My younger brother is completely crazy about baseball and watches every game.
simple: He has been crazy about cars since childhood.

example: The manager remained doubtful about the success of the new marketing plan.
simple: I am doubtful about his promises.

example: The volunteers were highly enthusiastic about helping the local community.
simple: She is enthusiastic about learning languages.

example: We are all very excited about the upcoming field trip to the museum.
simple: Are you excited about your vacation?

example: Customers were furious about the sudden increase in ticket prices.
simple: He was furious about being left behind.

example: It is normal to feel nervous about speaking in front of a large audience.
simple: I am nervous about the job interview.

example: Despite the current difficulties, the CEO remains optimistic about the company's future.
simple: We are optimistic about finding a cure.

example: Many young people are pessimistic about their chances of buying a house.
simple: He is pessimistic about the economy.

example: We are all very sad about the news of his sudden passing.
simple: She was sad about losing her pet.

example: If you are serious about losing weight, you must change your diet.
pattern: If S + be serious about + V-ing, S + must + V
simple: I am serious about learning piano.

chinese: 對...感到心煩意亂 / 生氣
example: She is still upset about the harsh comments her boss made yesterday.
simple: Don't be upset about small mistakes.

example: Parents are constantly worried about the safety of their children.
simple: I am worried about the coming typhoon.

chinese: 因...受責備 / 承擔責任
example: The driver was blamed for the accident because he was using his phone.
simple: He was blamed for losing the document.

example: Only full-time employees are eligible for the company's health insurance plan.
simple: You are eligible for a free upgrade.

example: Taiwan is famous for its delicious street food and friendly people.
simple: The city is famous for its historical sites.

example: I am truly grateful for all the support my family has given me.
simple: We are grateful for your help.

example: That intersection is notorious for frequent traffic accidents.
simple: The politician is notorious for corruption.

example: The students have studied hard and are well prepared for the final exams.
pattern: S + be well prepared for + N
simple: Are you prepared for the challenge?

example: He was punished for breaking the school rules and had to clean the classroom.
simple: She was punished for being late.

example: With a master's degree and three years of experience, she is highly qualified for the job.
simple: He is well qualified for the position.

example: After weeks of training, the team is finally ready for the big game.
simple: I am ready for a new start.

example: The region is renowned for its breathtaking natural scenery and hot springs.
simple: Paris is renowned for its art.

example: Human activities are largely responsible for the rapid increase in global temperatures.
simple: Who is responsible for this mess?

example: The principal is widely respected for his dedication to education.
simple: She is respected for her honesty.

example: This movie contains violence and is not suitable for young children.
simple: This soil is suitable for growing crops.

example: We should be thankful for the peace and freedom we enjoy today.
simple: I am thankful for your advice.

example: The politician was accused of taking bribes from several local businessmen.
simple: He was accused of stealing the car.

example: Don't be afraid of making mistakes; they are part of the learning process.
simple: She is afraid of spiders.

example: He was deeply ashamed of his rude behavior at the dinner party.
simple: You have nothing to be ashamed of.

example: Most smokers are fully aware of the dangers of smoking, yet they cannot quit.
simple: I am well aware of the problem.

example: With proper training, this new AI system is capable of performing complex tasks.
simple: She is capable of passing the exam.

example: People are becoming more conscious of the environmental impact of plastic bags.
simple: He is very conscious of his appearance.

example: I am slightly envious of her ability to learn foreign languages so quickly.
simple: They were envious of his success.

example: The judiciary must remain strictly independent of the government to ensure fairness.
simple: The decision was made independent of his advice.

example: The law applies to everyone equally, irrespective of their race or social status.
simple: Everyone is welcome, irrespective of age.

example: He was jealous of his coworker who received a promotion instead of him.
simple: She is jealous of her sister's beauty.

example: His parents are very proud of his outstanding academic achievements.
simple: I am proud of what we have done.

example: The little girl is scared of sleeping in the dark without a nightlight.
simple: He is scared of making mistakes.

example: The police became suspicious of the man when he couldn't answer simple questions.
simple: They are suspicious of his motives.

example: It is typical of him to arrive late and make excuses.
pattern: It is typical of O to + V
simple: This weather is typical of Taiwan in summer.

example: Like moths drawn to a flame, many young people are attracted to city life.
simple: I have always been attracted to science.

example: She quickly became accustomed to the fast pace of life in New York.
simple: I am accustomed to getting up early.

example: Many teenagers are addicted to playing online games on their smartphones.
simple: He is addicted to caffeine.

example: My brother cannot eat seafood because he is highly allergic to shrimp.
simple: Are you allergic to peanuts?

example: It is sad that some people remain completely indifferent to the suffering of others.
simple: He seems indifferent to my problems.

example: This cheap product is clearly inferior to the original brand in terms of quality.
simple: I feel inferior to my successful brother.

example: She has been married to a famous artist for over ten years.
simple: He is married to a doctor.

type: 形容詞/分詞 + 介系詞
example: Many environmentalists are strongly opposed to the construction of the new power plant.
simple: I am opposed to changing the rules.

example: You should always be polite to your elders and treat them with respect.
pattern: S + should be polite to + N
simple: The staff is very polite to customers.

example: The new manager is very open-minded and receptive to new ideas from the team.
simple: Children are often more receptive to change.

example: It is unacceptable to be rude to the waiters who are serving you.
pattern: It is adj to be rude to + N
simple: He was very rude to his teacher.

example: Your coat is very similar to the one I bought last year.
simple: His ideas are similar to mine.

example: This new model is far superior to the older version in battery life.
simple: I consider his work superior to hers.

example: High stress levels are often associated with an increased risk of heart disease.
simple: Dark clouds are associated with rain.

example: The children quickly became bored with their new toys and wanted something else.
simple: I am getting bored with this repetitive job.

example: She is blessed with a beautiful singing voice and a supportive family.
simple: Taiwan is blessed with rich natural resources.

example: Make sure this software is compatible with your computer's operating system.
simple: We are simply not compatible with each other.

example: When confronted with difficulties, you must stay calm and find a solution.
pattern: When confronted with N, S + V
simple: The company was confronted with a financial crisis.

example: He lives a simple life in the country and is content with what he has.
simple: Not everyone is content with a quiet life.

example: The night market is always crowded with tourists during the weekend.
pattern: The place is crowded with + N
simple: The street was crowded with people.

example: My parents were absolutely delighted with the gift I bought for them.
simple: She was delighted with her test scores.

example: Before starting the job, you need to become familiar with the company's rules.
simple: I am not familiar with this neighborhood.

type: 形容詞片語 + 介系詞
example: I am completely fed up with his constant complaining and negative attitude.
pattern: S + be fed up with + N
simple: Citizens are fed up with the terrible traffic.

example: It is always good to be friendly with your neighbors in case of an emergency.
simple: She is friendly with everyone in the office.

example: The coach was very pleased with the team's outstanding performance today.
simple: Are you pleased with the final result?

example: The new cafe near the school is very popular with college students.
simple: This song is popular with young people.

example: If you are not satisfied with our service, you can get a full refund.
simple: I am satisfied with my current job.

example: My parents were absolutely delighted to hear that I passed the entrance exam.
pattern: S + be absolutely delighted to + V
simple: She was absolutely delighted with her gift.

example: It is absolutely necessary to wear a seatbelt when driving on the highway.
pattern: It is absolutely necessary to + V
simple: Sleep is absolutely necessary for good health.

example: To expect a child to study for ten hours a day is absolutely ridiculous.
simple: The price of this ticket is absolutely ridiculous.

example: It is absolutely wrong to judge someone solely by their appearance.
pattern: It is absolutely wrong to + V
simple: You are absolutely wrong about him.

example: He has been actively involved in environmental protection campaigns since college.
pattern: S + be actively involved in + N
simple: She is actively involved in local politics.

example: Two passengers were badly injured in the car crash and taken to the hospital.
simple: The player was badly injured during the match.

example: We decided to stay indoors because it was bitterly cold outside.
simple: A bitterly cold wind blew across the field.

example: The fans were bitterly disappointed when their favorite team lost the final game.
simple: I am bitterly disappointed by his attitude.

chinese: 幸福地一無所知的、毫不知情的
example: The children played in the yard, blissfully unaware of the approaching storm.
pattern: S + be blissfully unaware of + N
simple: He remained blissfully unaware of the danger.

example: My sister and I have completely different personalities; she is outgoing while I am shy.
pattern: A and B are completely different
simple: Their opinions are completely different from ours.

chinese: 位置優越的、交通便利的
example: The hotel is conveniently located near the subway station, making it easy to travel.
pattern: N + is conveniently located near + N
simple: Our new office is conveniently located in the city center.

chinese: 極度認真的、非常嚴肅的
example: I thought he was joking, but the expression on his face was deadly serious.
pattern: S + be deadly serious about + N
simple: He is deadly serious about quitting his job.

example: He felt deeply ashamed of having lied to his best friend.
pattern: S + be deeply ashamed of + N
simple: I am deeply ashamed of my past mistakes.

chinese: 深表關切的、非常擔憂的
example: Environmental groups are deeply concerned about the destruction of the rainforest.
pattern: S + be deeply concerned about + N
simple: Parents are deeply concerned about school safety.

example: The country remains deeply divided over the issue of nuclear power.
pattern: The group is deeply divided over + N
simple: Public opinion is deeply divided.

chinese: 深度參與的、牽涉極深的
example: The mayor was deeply involved in the corruption scandal and eventually resigned.
pattern: S + be deeply involved in + N
simple: He is deeply involved in local charity work.

example: Many viewers were deeply offended by the insensitive jokes in the comedy show.
pattern: S + feel deeply offended by + N
simple: I was deeply offended by his rude remarks.

example: After walking in the desert for hours, he was desperately eager for a glass of water.
pattern: S + be desperately eager to + V
simple: She is desperately eager to prove herself.

type: 副詞 + 現在分詞(作形容詞)
example: The suspect's excuse for being at the crime scene was not entirely convincing.
pattern: His argument is not entirely convincing.
simple: The new theory is entirely convincing.

example: The results of the recent medical tests were entirely satisfactory to the doctor.
simple: The customer service was entirely satisfactory.

example: I will be eternally grateful to the brave firefighter who saved my dog's life.
pattern: S + will be eternally grateful for + N
simple: She is eternally grateful for your help.

example: The smartphone market has become fiercely competitive over the past decade.
simple: Getting into that school is fiercely competitive.

chinese: 充分了解的、完全意識到的
example: You should be fully aware of the potential risks before signing the contract.
pattern: S + be fully aware of + N
simple: I am fully aware of the problem.

example: They have been happily married for over fifty years and still hold hands.
pattern: S + have been happily married for + 時間
simple: My grandparents are happily married.

example: The bank was surrounded by heavily armed police officers during the robbery.
simple: Heavily armed guards stood at the gate.

example: The victim's face was hideously disfigured in the terrible chemical explosion.
simple: He survived but was hideously disfigured.

example: Her efforts in organizing the charity event were highly commended by the mayor.
pattern: S + be highly commended by + N
simple: The artwork was highly commended by the judges.

example: The new virus is highly contagious and can spread quickly through the air.
simple: Keep away from him; his cold is highly contagious.

example: The interviewers were highly impressed by her fluent English and confidence.
pattern: S + be highly impressed with + N
simple: I am highly impressed by your work.

example: Investing in renewable energy has proven to be a highly profitable business.
pattern: N + is a highly profitable business.
simple: The tech industry is highly profitable.

example: It is highly unlikely that it will snow in southern Taiwan during winter.
pattern: It is highly unlikely to + V
simple: His success is highly unlikely without your help.

example: It is highly unusual for the punctual professor to be late for class.
pattern: It is highly unusual that + S + V
simple: This kind of weather is highly unusual here.

example: She was mildly amused by his clumsy attempts to impress her.
simple: He watched the kids play, mildly amused.

chinese: 極其明顯的（明顯到令人難受的地步）
example: It was painfully obvious that he hadn't prepared for the presentation at all.
simple: It is painfully obvious that she is lying.

example: As a child, he was so painfully shy that he rarely spoke to strangers.
pattern: S + used to be painfully shy
simple: She is painfully shy around new people.

example: It is patently obvious that the company needs to change its marketing strategy.
pattern: It is patently obvious that + S + V
simple: His dislike for the plan is patently obvious.

example: Let me make it perfectly clear: I will not accept any more late assignments.
simple: I hope I've made myself perfectly clear.

example: It is perfectly normal to feel a bit anxious before a big job interview.
pattern: It is perfectly normal for N to V

example: He has held the world record for the 100-meter dash for over five years.
pattern: S + hold the record for + N
simple: She holds several Olympic records.

example: The university held a grand reception to welcome the international exchange students.
simple: A reception was held after the wedding.

example: He incurred a massive debt due to his gambling addiction and lost his house.
simple: The company incurred heavy debts.

example: If you return the rental car late, you will incur an additional late fee.
simple: Canceling the ticket may incur a fee.

example: Using your credit card overseas may incur a foreign transaction charge.
simple: Extra luggage will incur a charge.

example: The building incurred severe structural damage during the magnitude 7.0 earthquake.
simple: The car incurred little damage in the crash.

example: The politician's racist remarks quickly incurred the anger of the public.
pattern: S + incur the anger of + N
simple: His actions incurred his boss's anger.

example: It is wise to invest money in real estate to build long-term wealth.
simple: They invested money in the stock market.

example: If you want to master a language, you must invest time in practicing every day.
simple: Parents should invest time in their children.

example: The young entrepreneur invested all his energy into making the startup a success.
simple: She invested much energy in the project.

example: A reliable person is someone who always keeps a promise, no matter what happens.
simple: It is hard for him to keep a promise.

example: Can I trust you to keep a secret? Please don't tell anyone about this.
simple: She is very bad at keeping secrets.

example: Even when things got difficult, he kept his word and stayed to help us.
simple: A gentleman always keeps his word.

example: After failing the exam three times, he began to lose confidence in himself.
simple: Investors are losing confidence in the market.

example: The driver lost control of the car on the icy road and hit a tree.
simple: She lost control of her temper.

example: The teacher eventually lost patience with the boys who wouldn't stop talking.
simple: I am starting to lose patience.

example: During the economic recession, many small businesses lost money and had to close.
simple: He lost a lot of money in stocks.

example: To lose weight effectively, you need to combine a healthy diet with regular exercise.
simple: She lost weight by eating less sugar.

example: Despite the bad weather, the construction team is making steady headway on the new bridge.
simple: We are finally making some headway.

example: Many teenagers take part-time jobs during the summer vacation to make money.
simple: He started a business to make money.

example: Volunteering at the local shelter is a great way to make a difference in your community.
pattern: S + make a difference in + N
simple: One person can make a difference.

example: It is hard to make a living as a freelance writer without a stable income.
pattern: S + make a living by + V-ing
simple: He makes a living by selling paintings.

example: To succeed in the modern world, you must be ready to meet new challenges every day.
pattern: S + be ready to meet a challenge
simple: She is brave enough to meet the challenge.

example: Unfortunately, your application was rejected because it did not meet our requirements.
pattern: S + meet the requirements for + N
simple: This computer meets the minimum requirements.

chinese: 精心策劃一場活動/運動
example: The marketing team orchestrated a brilliant campaign that boosted sales by 30%.
pattern: S + orchestrate a campaign to + V
simple: They orchestrated a campaign to save the forest.

example: The general secretly orchestrated an attack on the enemy's headquarters at dawn.
pattern: S + orchestrate an attack on + N
simple: Hackers orchestrated a cyber attack.

example: The criminal mastermind orchestrated a hold-up at the national bank without leaving a trace.
simple: They orchestrated a hold-up last night.

example: It takes excellent leadership skills to orchestrate an international event like the Olympics.
simple: She orchestrated the whole charity event.

example: He paid her a nice compliment on her beautiful dress, which made her smile.
simple: I must pay you a compliment on your cooking.

example: It takes a lot of time, money, and patience to raise a child properly.
simple: She raised three children by herself.

example: The farmer raises animals like cows and sheep on his vast piece of land.
simple: My grandfather raised animals on the farm.

example: The movie's fantastic trailer raised the audience's expectations significantly.
simple: The success raised our expectations.

example: During the meeting, an employee raised an important issue regarding workplace safety.
simple: I would like to raise an issue here.

example: After evaluating all the evidence, the detective finally reached a logical conclusion.
simple: The jury reached a conclusion quickly.

example: It took us five hours of driving to finally reach our holiday destination.
simple: We reached our destination at noon.

example: If you stay focused and work hard, you will definitely reach your goal.
simple: He reached his goal of losing 10 kilos.

example: The jury took three days to deliberate before reaching a verdict of 'not guilty'.
simple: They finally reached a verdict.

example: I am trying to save money so that I can buy a new laptop next year.
simple: Taking the bus saves money.

example: Buying multi-functional furniture is a great way to save space in a small apartment.
simple: This folding bed saves a lot of space.

example: Donating blood is a simple act, but it can literally save a life.
simple: The doctor saved his life.

example: His quick thinking and calm reaction managed to save the situation from becoming a disaster.
simple: Her apology saved the situation.

example: The suspect will stand trial next month for the crimes he allegedly committed.
simple: He has to stand trial for murder.

example: Without proper preparation, you don't stand a chance of passing the difficult exam.
pattern: S + stand a chance of + V-ing
simple: Do we stand a chance of winning?

example: This new cheap smartphone simply cannot stand comparison with the leading brands.
pattern: A cannot stand comparison with B
simple: Her writing can stand comparison with professionals.

example: Even when everyone else disagreed with him, he stood his ground and defended his ideas.
simple: You must stand your ground.

example: The soldiers immediately stood to attention when the general walked into the room.
simple: The guards stood to attention.

example: The new mayor promised to tackle the traffic problem within his first year in office.
simple: We must tackle this problem together.

example: World leaders gathered at the summit to tackle the global issue of climate change.
simple: The book tackles the issue of poverty.

example: She drank a large cup of coffee before beginning to tackle the difficult task ahead.
simple: I have a big task to tackle today.

example: The government formed a special committee to effectively tackle the economic crisis.
simple: They acted fast to tackle the crisis.

example: After the founder passed away, his eldest son took control of the family business.
simple: The pilot took control of the plane.

example: It is important for public figures to take a firm stand against racial discrimination.
pattern: S + take a stand against + N
simple: We must take a stand on this issue.

example: The school has taken steps to ensure the safety of all students during fire drills.
simple: We are taking steps to improve quality.

chinese: 引起懷疑、使人對...產生懷疑
example: The new video evidence throws doubt on the suspect's alibi.
simple: His actions threw doubt on his words.

example: This new historical discovery might throw light on how ancient Egyptians built the pyramids.
simple: The book throws light on his early life.

example: They decided to throw a huge party to celebrate their 10th wedding anniversary.
pattern: S + throw a party for + N
simple: Let's throw a party for her birthday!

example: The little boy threw a tantrum in the toy store because his mother wouldn't buy him the robot.
simple: She threw a tantrum when she didn't get her way.

chinese: 四處走動、(消息)傳開、克服
example: It is very easy to get around the city by taking the MRT.
simple: Bad news gets around quickly.

example: He gets angry easily when people interrupt him while he is speaking.
simple: Don't get angry over small things.

example: We are hoping to get away for a few days to relax at the beach.
simple: The thief managed to get away.

chinese: (口語) 趕快開始、馬上行動
example: We have a lot of work to do before the deadline, so let's get cracking!
simple: If we want to finish, we'd better get cracking.

example: I heard you were sick. I hope you get better soon.
simple: The weather is getting better.

example: He was going to ask her out, but he got cold feet at the last minute.
simple: The groom got cold feet before the wedding.

example: We should head back home before it gets dark.
simple: It gets dark very early in winter.

example: They decided to get divorced after realizing they were no longer happy together.
simple: My parents got divorced last year.

example: It is illegal and extremely dangerous to get drunk and drive.
pattern: S + get drunk on + 飲料
simple: He got completely drunk at the party.

example: The children got very excited when they saw the presents under the Christmas tree.
simple: Don't get too excited yet.

example: I suddenly got a great idea for our final science project.
simple: Where did you get that idea?

example: After months of searching, he finally got a job as a software engineer.
pattern: S + get a job at + 公司
simple: She got a job in a bank.

example: They plan to get married next spring in a beautiful church.
simple: When are you getting married?

example: You must get permission from your parents before going on the camping trip.
simple: He got permission to leave early.

example: Please get ready quickly, or we will miss our flight.
simple: I am getting ready for the party.

example: We should get rid of these old clothes to make room for new ones.
simple: It's hard to get rid of a bad habit.

example: I ignored his calls completely, hoping he would eventually get the message and leave me alone.
simple: He glared at me, and I got the message.

example: We need to get a move on, or we are going to be late for the movie.
pattern: Come on, get a move on!
simple: If you don't get a move on, we'll miss the train.

example: The project is already behind schedule, so we really need to get moving.
simple: Let's get moving before it rains.

example: Arguing with an unreasonable person will get you nowhere.
simple: We are getting nowhere in this discussion.

example: After hours of trying different solutions, we are finally getting somewhere.
simple: Keep practicing, and you'll get somewhere.

chinese: 繼續進行... / 與...和睦相處
example: Please stop complaining and get on with your homework.
simple: How do you get on with your new roommate?

example: He pretended to be sick to get out of doing the household chores.
simple: You can't get out of helping us today.

example: We have a lot to cover today, so let's get started right away.
simple: I need to get started on my essay.

example: She didn't explain the plan in detail, but I got the drift of what she wanted.
simple: He spoke quickly, but I got the drift.

example: He got the sack because he was consistently late for work.
pattern: S + get the sack for + V-ing
simple: If you don't work hard, you'll get the sack.

example: Learning English takes time, but if you keep practicing, you will get there eventually.
simple: It's a long journey, but we will get there.

example: Our extended family tries to get together at least once a year for Chinese New Year.
simple: Let's get together for a drink soon.

example: Please don't get upset over such a small mistake; everyone makes them.
simple: She got upset when she heard the news.

example: It took him a few months to get used to driving on the left side of the road in the UK.
simple: I am getting used to the hot weather here.

example: Everyone makes a mistake from time to time, so don't be too hard on yourself.
simple: I made a terrible mistake on the test.

example: His explanation for being late didn't make any sense to me at all.
simple: It makes sense to buy tickets in advance.

example: Regardless of the final outcome, you should just do your best in the competition.
pattern: S + do one's best to + V
simple: I will do my best to help you.

example: Students are expected to do their homework immediately after getting home from school.
simple: She is doing her homework in her room.

example: Could you do me a favor and translate this document into English?
pattern: Could you do me a favor?
simple: He did me a huge favor yesterday.

example: She is doing research on renewable energy for her master's degree.
simple: We need to do more research before deciding.

example: Sometimes you just have to take a chance if you really want to achieve your dreams.
pattern: S + take a chance on + N
simple: I decided to take a chance and invest in the business.

example: The judge took the boy's young age into account when deciding his punishment.
simple: You must take the weather into account.

type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: The CEO resigned to take full responsibility for the company's massive financial loss.
simple: He took responsibility for the mistake.

example: I have to give a presentation on climate change in my science class tomorrow.
pattern: S + give a presentation on + N
simple: She gave a great presentation yesterday.

type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: The invention of the smartphone has had a profound impact on modern communication.
pattern: S + have an impact on + N
simple: Pollution has a huge impact on our health.

example: I need to have a serious talk with my son about his recent poor academic performance.
pattern: S + have a talk with + O
simple: Let's have a talk after dinner.

type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: Good teachers usually have a lasting influence on their students' personal development.
pattern: S + have an influence on + N
simple: Media has a great influence on teens.

type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: You must have confidence in yourself if you want other people to believe in you.
simple: I have full confidence in her ability.

type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: Drinking too much coffee before bed will definitely have a negative effect on your sleep.
pattern: S + have an effect on + N
simple: The medicine had no effect on him.

example: Even after graduating from high school, the classmates promised to keep in touch with each other.
pattern: S + keep in touch with + O
simple: Let's keep in touch!

example: We got lost in the forest because we forgot to bring a map and our phones died.
pattern: S + get lost in + 地方
simple: Follow the signs so you don't get lost.

example: Reading extensively is one of the most effective ways to gain knowledge about the world.
pattern: V-ing is a way to gain knowledge.
simple: Traveling helps you gain knowledge.

example: The team worked together to find a creative way to solve the complex problem.
pattern: S + try to solve a problem
simple: Math helps us learn how to solve problems.

example: The beautiful red dress in the shop window immediately caught her eye.
simple: The strange painting caught my eye.

example: You must bear in mind that safety should always be your top priority.
pattern: Bear in mind that + S + V.
simple: Bear in mind that the deadline is tomorrow.

example: The powerful earthquake caused severe damage to the city's infrastructure.
simple: The storm caused a lot of damage.

example: The young firefighter showed great courage when saving the child from the burning house.
pattern: S + show great courage in + V-ing
simple: It takes time to show courage.

example: The whole room fell silent the moment the strict principal walked in.
pattern: S + fall silent when + S + V
simple: The crowd fell silent as he began to speak.

example: Many high school graduates choose to go abroad for further studies to broaden their horizons.
simple: He went abroad on business.

chinese: 離開、外出(度假)、(症狀)消失
example: If you take this medicine, your headache should go away in a few hours.
simple: The bad smell eventually went away.

example: Despite the bad weather, the organizers decided to go ahead with the outdoor concert.
simple: Please go ahead and eat without me.

example: Without proper guidance, teenagers can easily go astray and get involved in crimes.
simple: The letter seems to have gone astray in the mail.

example: The little boy went missing in the crowded amusement park, causing his parents to panic.
simple: My keys have gone missing again.

example: After a long and exhausting day at work, all I want to do is go home and rest.
simple: It's getting late; we should go home.

chinese: 直走 / 左轉 / 右轉
example: Go straight on this road for two blocks, and then go left at the traffic light.
pattern: Go straight / left / right at + N
simple: Go right when you see the bank.

chinese: 往北 / 南 / 東 / 西走
example: Many birds go south during the winter to find warmer climates and more food.
simple: We are going north for our vacation.

chinese: 去購物 / 釣魚 / 航海
example: On weekends, my father often goes fishing by the lake to relax and enjoy nature.
simple: Let's go shopping this afternoon.

example: He started to go bald in his late twenties, which made him feel a bit insecure.
simple: Many men go bald as they age.

example: The old dog is gradually going blind and deaf due to its advanced age.
simple: Listening to loud music can make you go deaf.

example: The constant noise from the construction site next door is making me go crazy.
simple: He went mad with anger.

example: After the power outage, the computer system went haywire and started deleting files randomly.
simple: Everything went haywire at the last minute.

example: The crowd went wild with excitement when the singer finally appeared on stage.
simple: Kids tend to go wild at parties.

example: Due to poor management and the economic recession, the company eventually went bankrupt.
simple: Many small businesses went bankrupt last year.

example: Nowadays, you can easily go online to check your bank account balance or pay bills.
simple: I went online to buy a ticket.

example: After graduating from high school, she decided to go to college to study art.
simple: He goes to school by bus every day.

example: The thief was caught by the police and will go to jail for his crimes.
pattern: S + go to prison/jail for + N
simple: He went to prison for robbery.

example: My grandparents are very religious and go to church every Sunday morning.
simple: They go to church to pray.

example: He wakes up at 6 a.m. every day and takes the MRT to go to work.
simple: I have to go to work early tomorrow.

example: You look absolutely exhausted; you should take a hot shower and go to bed early tonight.
simple: I went to sleep right after the movie.

example: To lose weight quickly, some people go to extremes by starving themselves, which is very unhealthy.
pattern: S + go to extremes to + V
simple: He goes to extremes to prove his point.

example: We must eat all the leftovers in the fridge so that good food doesn't go to waste.
simple: All my hard work went to waste.

example: Since the irresponsible new manager took over, the whole department has gone to hell.
simple: The project went to hell after he left.

phrase: go on a trip / a journey / a tour
chinese: 去旅行 / 踏上旅程 / 去觀光
example: Our family plans to go on a trip to Europe next summer to experience different cultures.
pattern: S + go on a trip to + 地方
simple: He went on a business trip.

phrase: go on a holiday / a vacation
example: They decided to go on a vacation to Hawaii to relax and get away from work.
simple: We are going on a holiday next week.

example: For their 20th wedding anniversary, the couple went on a luxury cruise across the Mediterranean.
simple: My grandparents love going on cruises.

example: It has always been my dream to go on a safari in Africa to see lions and elephants in the wild.
simple: They went on a safari in Kenya.

example: The two best friends packed their bags and went on an adventure around the world.
simple: Are you ready to go on an adventure?

example: Since the weather is so lovely today, let's go on a picnic in the park.
simple: We went on a picnic by the river.

example: He was very nervous because he was going on a blind date with someone he met online.
pattern: S + go on a date with + O
simple: They went on their first date yesterday.

example: The doctor strongly advised him to go on a strict diet to improve his health.
pattern: S + need to go on a diet
simple: I am going on a diet before the wedding.

example: After getting her first paycheck, she went on a shopping spree and bought a lot of clothes.
pattern: S + go on a shopping spree
simple: He went on a spending spree.

example: The young actor took a deep breath before he went on stage to perform.
simple: She felt nervous before going on stage.

example: The factory workers decided to go on strike to demand higher wages and better working conditions.
pattern: S + go on strike for + N
simple: The bus drivers went on strike yesterday.

example: The patient will need to go on a specific medical treatment to completely cure the rare disease.
pattern: S + go on a treatment for + N
simple: He went on a new treatment plan.

example: Winning the international championship is a great accomplishment for the young team.
simple: Graduating with honors is a great accomplishment.

example: I have great admiration for people who dedicate their lives to helping others.
pattern: S + have great admiration for + N
simple: He looked at her with great admiration.

type: 形容詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: The project requires a great amount of time and resources to complete.
pattern: S + V + a great deal of + 不可數名詞
simple: He spent a great deal of money on the house.

example: I bought this designer coat at a 50% discount; it was a great bargain.
simple: These shoes are a great bargain.

example: The lost climbers were in great danger of freezing to death in the snowstorm.
simple: The rare animals are in great danger.

type: 介系詞 + 形容詞 + 名詞
example: The professor explained the complex theory in great detail so everyone could understand.
pattern: S + describe/explain O + in great detail
simple: She planned the event in great detail.

example: The foreign student had great difficulty understanding the local slang at first.
simple: He walked with great difficulty.

example: To his great embarrassment, he forgot his lines during the school play.
pattern: To one's great embarrassment, S + V
simple: She realized her mistake with great embarrassment.

example: The announcement of the free concert caused great excitement among the fans.
simple: He opened the gift with great excitement.

example: We went camping in the mountains last weekend, and it was great fun.
simple: Playing board games with friends is great fun.

example: Seeing her children grow up healthy and strong brings her great happiness.
simple: The news filled them with great happiness.

example: The old man wept with great joy when he finally reunited with his family.
simple: It is a great joy to see you again.

example: Going to the beach on such a sunny day is a great idea.
pattern: That sounds like a great idea.
simple: He came up with a great idea.

type: 介系詞 + 形容詞 + 名詞
example: Learning to communicate effectively is of great importance in today's society.
simple: This document is of great importance.

example: You got accepted into your dream university? That is absolutely great news!
simple: I have some great news for you.

example: Studying abroad is a great opportunity to explore different cultures and meet new people.
pattern: This is a great opportunity for + N
simple: Don't miss this great opportunity.

example: The young athlete shows great potential to become a world champion in the future.
pattern: S + show great potential in + N
simple: This market has great potential for growth.

example: The experienced surgeon performed the complicated operation with great skill.
pattern: S + do something with great skill
simple: He plays the piano with great skill.

chinese: 巨大的力量、極大的優勢
example: It takes great strength and courage to overcome such a terrible tragedy.
pattern: It takes great strength to + V
simple: Her ability to stay calm is a great strength.

example: As the famous saying goes, with great power comes great responsibility.
pattern: S + have great power over + N
simple: The media wields great power.

example: The school charity fair was a great success, raising thousands of dollars for the orphanage.
simple: His first novel was a great success.

example: She gets great satisfaction from helping stray animals find loving homes.
pattern: S + get great satisfaction from + V-ing
simple: He looked at his work with great satisfaction.

chinese: 深刻的理解、極大的體諒
example: The principal showed great understanding when the student explained his family problems.
pattern: S + have a great understanding of + N
simple: She has a great understanding of music.

example: The businessman accumulated great wealth through his successful investments in real estate.
simple: He inherited great wealth from his family.

example: The village elder shared words of great wisdom with the younger generation.
simple: She leads the team with great wisdom.

example: The lawyer presented a strong argument to prove his client's innocence.
simple: There is a strong argument for raising taxes.

example: She has a strong belief that hard work will eventually pay off.
pattern: S + have a strong belief that...
simple: He holds a strong conviction about human rights.

chinese: 充分的理由、有力的立論
example: There is a strong case for banning smartphones in the classroom to improve focus.
pattern: There is a strong case for/against + N
simple: He makes a strong case for the new policy.

example: If we leave now, there is a strong chance that we can catch the last train.
pattern: There is a strong chance that + S + V
simple: There's a strong chance of rain tomorrow.

example: I need a cup of strong coffee to keep myself awake during the morning meeting.
simple: He prefers strong tea in the afternoon.

chinese: 堅定的承諾、強烈的使命感
example: The new principal has shown a strong commitment to improving student welfare.
pattern: S + show a strong commitment to + N
simple: The company made a strong commitment to safety.

chinese: 強烈的關聯、緊密的聯繫
example: Studies have shown a strong connection between lack of sleep and depression.
pattern: There is a strong connection between A and B
simple: We share a strong connection.

example: The bright red door forms a strong contrast with the white walls of the house.
pattern: A forms a strong contrast with B
simple: His quiet nature is in strong contrast to her outgoing personality.

example: The controversial policy drew strong criticism from the general public.
simple: The decision met with strong criticism.

example: After graduating, she had a strong desire to travel around the world and experience different cultures.
pattern: S + have a strong desire to + V
simple: He felt a strong desire to help them.

example: Our school places a strong emphasis on both academic achievement and moral education.
pattern: S + put a strong emphasis on + N
simple: The course puts a strong emphasis on practical skills.

example: Without strong evidence, the police cannot arrest the suspect.
simple: There is strong evidence linking him to the crime.

example: I have a strong feeling that everything will turn out fine in the end.
pattern: S + have a strong feeling that...
simple: She has strong feelings about animal rights.

example: Parents exert a strong influence on their children's personality development.
pattern: S + have a strong influence on + N
simple: The teacher had a strong influence on his life.

example: The movie is rated R because it contains violence and strong language.
simple: He was criticized for using strong language.

example: The government's plan to cut workers' salaries met with strong opposition from the labor union.
simple: There is strong resistance to the new law.

example: Durian is a tropical fruit known for its creamy texture and very strong smell.
simple: Garlic has a very strong taste.

example: The charity project would not be possible without the strong support of the local community.
pattern: S + show strong support for + N
simple: The mayor voiced his strong support for the plan.

example: Young children have a strong tendency to imitate their parents' behavior.
pattern: S + have a strong tendency to + V
simple: There is a strong tendency for prices to rise.

chinese: 優良的傳統、深厚的傳統
example: This historic school has a strong tradition of academic excellence and sportsmanship.
pattern: S + have a strong tradition of + N
simple: The village maintains a strong tradition of woodworking.

chinese: 堅定的看法、強烈的觀點
example: The author expressed strong views on the current political situation in his new book.
pattern: S + hold strong views on + N
simple: She has strong views on animal welfare.

example: The flight was delayed for several hours due to the strong wind and heavy rain.
simple: The strong wind blew down many trees.

example: The lawyer presented a strong argument to prove his client's innocence.
simple: There is a strong argument for raising taxes.

example: She has a strong belief that hard work will eventually pay off.
pattern: S + have a strong belief that...
simple: He holds a strong conviction about human rights.

chinese: 充分的理由、有力的立論
example: There is a strong case for banning smartphones in the classroom to improve focus.
pattern: There is a strong case for/against + N
simple: He makes a strong case for the new policy.

example: If we leave now, there is a strong chance that we can catch the last train.
pattern: There is a strong chance that + S + V
simple: There's a strong chance of rain tomorrow.

example: I need a cup of strong coffee to keep myself awake during the morning meeting.
simple: He prefers strong tea in the afternoon.

chinese: 堅定的承諾、強烈的使命感
example: The new principal has shown a strong commitment to improving student welfare.
pattern: S + show a strong commitment to + N
simple: The company made a strong commitment to safety.

chinese: 強烈的關聯、緊密的聯繫
example: Studies have shown a strong connection between lack of sleep and depression.
pattern: There is a strong connection between A and B
simple: We share a strong connection.

example: The bright red door forms a strong contrast with the white walls of the house.
pattern: A forms a strong contrast with B
simple: His quiet nature is in strong contrast to her outgoing personality.

example: The controversial policy drew strong criticism from the general public.
simple: The decision met with strong criticism.

example: After graduating, she had a strong desire to travel around the world and experience different cultures.
pattern: S + have a strong desire to + V
simple: He felt a strong desire to help them.

example: Our school places a strong emphasis on both academic achievement and moral education.
pattern: S + put a strong emphasis on + N
simple: The course puts a strong emphasis on practical skills.

example: Without strong evidence, the police cannot arrest the suspect.
simple: There is strong evidence linking him to the crime.

example: I have a strong feeling that everything will turn out fine in the end.
pattern: S + have a strong feeling that...

example: I can't drink this soup right now because it is boiling hot.

type: 副詞 + 形容詞(過去分詞)

chinese: 特別突出的、尤其顯著的

chinese: 被顯著報導的、放在醒目位置的

chinese: 玻璃琴 (用裝水的高腳杯演奏的樂器)

chinese: 身體上的挑戰 (生理障礙)

example: Burning fossil fuels, such as coal and oil, releases huge amounts of carbon dioxide.

example: The city boasts several world-class concert halls designed with perfect acoustics.

example: Drivers who violate traffic regulations will be heavily fined or lose their licenses.

example: The prize was awarded jointly to three scientists who contributed to the discovery.

type: 名詞 + 介系詞 (或動詞搭配)

simple: My first encounter with him was unpleasant.

example: We saw a beautiful eagle perching on the highest branch of the old oak tree.
simple: The cat perched on the fence.

example: I was completely struck by the sheer beauty of the canyon when I first saw it.

example: Hearing the police sirens coming after them, the robbers abandoned their car and ran.

example: As you climb higher up the mountain, the oxygen in the air begins to thin quickly.

example: The magician threw a smoke bomb and seemed to disappear within seconds.
simple: The symptoms should disappear within a week.

example: The new student tried hard to fit into the class and make new friends.

chinese: 進展順利 / 計算出 / 鍛鍊

type: 副詞 + 形容詞(過去分詞)

type: 副詞 + 形容詞(過去分詞)

simple: Dress codes are strictly enforced here.

example: Many elderly people are fearful of leaving their homes during the flu season.

example: The manager is highly critical of employees who arrive late to meetings.

example: Investors remain doubtful about the company's ability to recover from the scandal.

example: The Amazon rainforest is home to thousands of unique plant and animal species.

example: He spent the whole afternoon browsing in antiquarian bookshops looking for rare editions.

example: The conference focused on promoting sustainable development in developing countries.

example: The invention of the jet engine revolutionized commercial air travel.

example: The earthquake severely damaged the building's structural integrity, making it unsafe.

type: 名詞作形容詞 + 名詞

example: The artist demonstrated masterful skills in his latest oil painting.

example: Stress and lack of sleep are common problems associated with the modern lifestyle.

example: The Mona Lisa is perhaps the most recognizable piece of art in the world.

type: 過去分詞作形容詞 + 名詞

example: The fearless tribesmen bravely defended their land against foreign invaders.

example: This semi-nomadic group moves their herds between the mountains and the valleys depending on the season.

type: 現在分詞作形容詞 + 名詞

example: The human body has an amazing self-healing power when given enough rest and nutrition.

example: The international charity provides food and medical supplies to children in the war-torn country.

type: 形容詞 + 形容詞 + 名詞

type: 過去分詞作形容詞 + 名詞

example: We were too tired to cook, so we decided to order pizza for dinner.

example: He worked part-time delivering pizza to save money for his college tuition.

chinese: 管理辦公室、維持辦公室運作

example: The famous comedian was invited to host a late-night talk show.

example: Don't forget to tag me in the photos you post on Instagram.

example: The eventual cost of repairing the damaged bridge was much higher than expected.

example: Fans waited in line for hours to buy the band's latest releases.

example: For farmers, a sudden drop in temperature can spell disaster for their crops.

example: The arch design of the bridge helps to distribute stress evenly across its structure.

example: Wearing a seatbelt can significantly reduce the likelihood of serious injury in a car crash.

example: Robots are now programmed to perform complex actions that were once impossible for machines.

example: The delayed response to the natural disaster ultimately cost many lives.

example: Through hard work and dedication, she achieved the status of a senior partner in the law firm.

example: In many indigenous cultures, completing a difficult challenge signifies the coming of age for young men.

example: The company changed its packaging materials to reduce its negative impact on the environment.

example: Some amphibians, like salamanders, have the remarkable ability to regenerate lost organs.

example: He quit his high-paying job to move to Paris and pursue his dreams of becoming an artist.

example: The young actress made a successful debut in the award-winning independent film.

example: They both work in the tech industry, but the similarities end there.

type: 名詞 + 動詞(現在分詞)

simple: The blossoms are blooming beautifully this year.

example: We were driving on the highway when suddenly the car engine stopped.
simple: The boat's engine stopped in the middle of the lake.

example: If a boat has a hole in the bottom, it will eventually fill with water and sink.

chinese: 新書發表會、讀書會演講

chinese: 如雨後春筍般出現、湧現

example: Outdoor events are always subject to changes depending on the weather conditions.

example: We spent the whole afternoon wandering in the museum, admiring the ancient artifacts.

example: Other than a few minor spelling mistakes, your essay is exceptionally well-written.

chinese: 被歸功於... / 被認為是...的發明者
type: 過去分詞(作形容詞) + 介系詞

type: 形容詞(最高級) + 名詞

chinese: 節後不滿情緒、節後症候群

type: 過去分詞(作形容詞) + 名詞

simple: Baldness can be an inherited feature.

type: 形容詞 + 名詞 + 名詞

type: 過去分詞(作形容詞) + 名詞

type: 形容詞 + 名詞 + 名詞

pattern: Ships travel through deep water channels.

type: 現在分詞(作形容詞) + 名詞

example: Her brilliant presentation and deep knowledge never failed to amaze her teacher.

chinese: 發展出方程式 / 推導出等式

chinese: 引發質疑 / 惹起問題

chinese: 樂隊花車、跟風 / 潮流

example: A dedicated research team spent three years investigating the side effects of the new drug.

example: Maintaining a healthy body weight is crucial for preventing heart diseases.

simple: Rice straw can be used to feed animals.

example: The experienced marine pilot safely guided the huge cargo ship into the narrow port.

chinese: 酸度率 / 酸鹼值比例

example: Adding artificial preservatives can significantly extend the shelf life of packaged foods.

example: It is generally recommended to store leftover food in an airtight plastic container in the fridge.

example: The transport ministry recently announced new safety regulations for all commercial flights.

type: 動詞 + 副詞 + 介系詞

type: 動詞 + 副詞 + 介系詞

type: 過去分詞(作形容詞) + 介系詞

type: 過去分詞(作形容詞) + 介系詞

example: You shouldn't jump into conclusions before hearing all the facts from both sides.

example: Please feel free to reach out to me if you have any further questions about the project.

example: When the boss offered him a position in New York, he immediately jumped on the opportunity.

type: 過去分詞(作形容詞) + 介系詞

simple: This museum is devoted to modern art.

example: The company designed the new smartphone to appeal to younger consumers.
simple: The idea doesn't appeal to me at all.

type: 過去分詞(作形容詞) + 介系詞

example: The missing painting is assumed to be stolen by an organized crime group.

example: The company's new recycling policy is entirely in line with its core environmental values.

type: 動名詞/現在分詞 + 名詞

type: 過去分詞/形容詞 + 介系詞

type: 被動動詞/過去分詞 + 介系詞

type: 過去分詞(作形容詞) + 介系詞

type: 動詞(過去式) + 介系詞

example: Although they are different brands, the design of the two phones is somewhat similar.

chinese: 極有成就的、造詣極高的

example: I ran into my former manager at the supermarket yesterday and we had a nice chat.

example: Most banks and post offices are closed on a national holiday.

example: The view of the snow-capped mountain reflecting in the crystal-clear lake was breathtaking.

example: Building the ancient pyramids required an incredible amount of manual labor.

example: Looking at these old photos always reminds me of the light-hearted days of my childhood.

chinese: 不明的起源 / 來源不明

example: The avocado has become a highly fashionable fruit, often featured on trendy cafe menus.

example: Many clothing brands are switching to eco-friendly fibers like organic cotton and bamboo.

example: Taking public transportation instead of driving is a great way to reduce your carbon footprint.

example: Compost made from kitchen waste serves as an excellent natural fertilizer for the garden.

example: The school held a grand anniversary celebration to mark its 50th year of founding.

example: She prefers to keep her private matters separate from her professional life.
pattern: I don't discuss private matters at work.

type: 被動動詞/過去分詞 + 介系詞

example: Researchers are conducting studies to explore a link between stress and heart disease.

chinese: 激發洞察力 / 引發啟發

example: Nature's beauty has always been a powerful force to inspire creation among artists.

example: The government agency will issue official documents for all new immigrants next month.

example: The TV series was canceled because the pilot episode failed to attract enough viewers.
simple: The pilot episode introduces the main characters.

chinese: 試點計畫 / 實驗性專案
example: The city implemented a pilot project to test the efficiency of electric buses.
simple: The pilot project was a success, so they expanded it.

chinese: 試點方案 / 實驗方案
example: The government launched a pilot scheme to provide free internet to rural communities.
simple: The results of the pilot scheme were encouraging.

chinese: 初步研究 / 前期研究
example: We conducted a pilot study with twenty volunteers before starting the full-scale experiment.
simple: A pilot study helps identify potential problems.

example: We were too tired to cook, so we ordered a pizza delivery for dinner.
simple: He worked as a pizza delivery driver.

chinese: 建築許可 / 規劃許可
example: You must obtain planning permission from the local council before building an extension.
simple: They were refused planning permission for the garage.

example: Some people undergo plastic surgery to improve their appearance or correct physical defects.
simple: Plastic surgery is becoming more common.

chinese: 警察學校 / 警官學校
example: Recruits undergo rigorous physical and mental training at the police academy.
pattern: S + graduate from the police academy
simple: He entered the police academy last year.

example: A police car with flashing lights chased the speeding vehicle down the highway.
pattern: S + be spotted in a police car
simple: Three police cars arrived at the scene.

example: The local police department is offering a reward for any information about the missing child.
simple: He works for the city's police department.

example: The government plans to increase the size of the police force to combat rising crime.
simple: She is the first woman in our family to join the police force.

example: The police officer asked the driver to pull over and show his driver's license.
pattern: S + work as a police officer
simple: Every police officer must wear a uniform.

chinese: 警察國家 (指政府嚴密監控人民的國家)
example: Citizens in a police state often lack basic freedoms and live under constant surveillance.
pattern: N + is becoming a police state
simple: Dissent is not allowed in a police state.

example: He went to the police station to report that his wallet had been stolen.
simple: The nearest police station is two blocks away.

example: The minister relies on her policy adviser to provide expert analysis of social issues.
pattern: S + work as a policy adviser
simple: He is a senior policy adviser to the president.

example: The recent policy change regarding travel restrictions has caused confusion at the airport.
simple: The company announced a major policy change.

example: The government's policy decision to raise taxes was met with strong public opposition.
simple: We are waiting for a final policy decision.

chinese: 決策者 / 政策制定者
example: Environmental policy makers are meeting to discuss ways to reduce carbon emissions.
simple: The report is intended for policy makers.

example: The company issued a clear policy statement regarding its commitment to diversity.
simple: The policy statement outlines the school's goals.

example: Some countries have implemented strict population control measures to slow down the growth rate.
simple: The government discussed the ethics of population control.

example: High population density in urban areas often leads to traffic congestion and high housing costs.
simple: Taipei has a very high population density.

chinese: 人口爆炸 (指人口急遽增加)
example: The population explosion in the 20th century put a massive strain on the Earth's resources.
simple: Improved health care caused a population explosion.

example: Slow population growth in developed nations has led to concerns about an aging workforce.
simple: Population growth is stabilizing in many regions.

example: He commissioned a famous portrait painter to create a painting of his daughter.
pattern: S + work as a portrait painter
simple: The portrait painter captured her expression perfectly.

example: In the past, people often used a postal order to send money safely through the mail.
simple: The magazine accepts payment by postal order.

example: You need to put a postage stamp on the top right corner of the envelope.
simple: The first postage stamp was issued in 1840.

example: It took her a long time to come to terms with the loss of her beloved pet.
pattern: S + finally come to terms with + N
simple: He is learning to come to terms with his past.

example: The manager has to deal with several customer complaints every single day.
pattern: S + know how to deal with + N
simple: We need a plan to deal with the crisis.

example: Whether we go for a picnic or not will depend on the weather tomorrow.
simple: The success of the project depends on teamwork.

chinese: 跟上...的最新進度 / 了解最新消息
example: As a technology expert, he reads daily news to keep abreast of the latest developments.
simple: It is important to keep abreast of current events.

chinese: 阻止...靠近 / 阻止(惡化)
example: Eating a healthy diet and exercising regularly can help keep diseases at bay.
simple: The soldiers fought hard to keep the enemy at bay.

example: Heavy smoking can lead to severe lung diseases and other health problems.
simple: Hard work often leads to success.

example: With his thick makeup and wig, the actor could easily pass for a woman.
simple: His English is so good that he could pass for a native speaker.

chinese: 為...付錢 / 為...付出代價
example: If you make a serious mistake at work, you may have to pay for it with your job.
pattern: S + must pay for the + N
simple: He used his savings to pay for the new car.

example: The older boys used to pick on him because he was smaller and quieter than them.
simple: The teacher noticed that some kids were picking on the new student.

example: She spent nearly an hour trying to pick out the perfect dress for the wedding.
pattern: S + pick out + N + from + 來源
simple: Can you pick out your brother in this old photo?

chinese: 增加 / 堆疊 (壓力或重量)
example: As the deadline approached, the manager began to pile on the pressure for the team.
simple: He has piled on the pounds since he stopped exercising.

example: It is considered rude in some cultures to point at people with your finger.
simple: The child pointed at the colorful bird in the tree.

example: All the evidence points to the fact that the fire was started deliberately.
simple: The compass needle points to the North.

example: The detective spent the whole night poring over the case files to find a clue.
simple: She pored over the map for hours.

chinese: 投入 (資源、精力) / 湧入
example: The company decided to pour more resources into its research and development department.
simple: Thousands of fans poured into the stadium.

example: He was intent on winning the competition, no matter how hard he had to train.
simple: They were intent on finishing the project before sunset.

chinese: 對...疑神疑鬼 / 偏執的
example: After his computer was hacked, he became very paranoid about his online security.
simple: She is paranoid about people talking behind her back.

chinese: 熱衷於... / 對...充滿熱情
example: Our school's environmental club is passionate about protecting the local ecosystem.
