simple: He is passionate about classical music.

example: Teachers need to be very patient with students who learn at a slower pace.
pattern: S + should be patient with + 人
simple: Please be patient with me while I learn this new system.

example: Many economists are pessimistic about the chances of a quick global recovery.
pattern: S + be pessimistic about the + N
simple: Don't be so pessimistic about your future.

example: The manager was very pleased with the team's performance during the busy season.
simple: She was pleased with her exam results.

example: Small children can be very possessive about their toys and don't like sharing.
simple: He is very possessive about his personal space.

chinese: 對...著迷 / 強迫性地關注
example: She is obsessive about cleanliness and washes her hands dozens of times a day.
simple: He is obsessive about details.

example: Tropical rainforests are famous for having exotic plants and animals in abundance.
simple: Natural resources are found in abundance in this region.

example: The company's accounting practices are in accordance with international standards.
pattern: S + be in accordance with + N
simple: The prize was awarded in accordance with the rules.

example: If you want to get a good seat, you should book your tickets in advance.
simple: Thanks for telling me in advance.

example: In all likelihood, the new highway project will be completed by next spring.
simple: In all likelihood, we will win the game.

example: The medicine works best when taken in conjunction with a healthy diet and exercise.
simple: The police worked in conjunction with local residents.

example: The northern part of the country is mountainous; in contrast, the south is very flat.
pattern: S + V; in contrast, S + V
simple: In contrast to her sister, she is very shy.

example: If we don't take action now, many rare species will be in danger of extinction.
simple: The building is in danger of collapsing.

example: Data scientists are currently in high demand because companies want to analyze user behavior.
simple: Fresh organic vegetables are always in demand.

example: The professor explained the complicated chemical reaction in great detail.
simple: We discussed the plan in detail.

example: The ownership of the small island is still in dispute between the two neighboring nations.
simple: The facts of the case are in dispute.

example: In essence, his proposal is just a simpler version of the old plan.
simple: The two ideas are, in essence, the same.

example: This is the oldest manuscript in existence that describes the ancient ritual.
simple: There are only a few copies in existence.

example: He looks very young; in fact, he is already over fifty years old.
pattern: S + V; in fact, S + V
simple: The test was quite easy; in fact, everyone passed.

example: Most of the board members voted in favor of the new marketing strategy.
simple: I am in favor of stopping the project.

example: The new safety regulations have been in force since the beginning of this year.
simple: The law is still in force.

chinese: 全力進行中 / 達到高潮
example: When we arrived at the party, the music was loud and the dancing was already in full swing.
simple: The construction is now in full swing.

example: Both countries entered the negotiations in good faith, hoping to achieve lasting peace.
pattern: S + act / negotiate in good faith
simple: The offer was made in good faith.

example: In hindsight, I should have listened to my mother's advice about saving money.
pattern: In hindsight, S + should have + p.p.
simple: It's easy to be right in hindsight.

chinese: 處於危險 / 受到威脅
example: If the company fails to get the loan, thousands of jobs will be in jeopardy.
simple: The future of the forest is in jeopardy.

example: The principal decided to cancel the outdoor event in light of the recent storm warnings.
pattern: in light of + N, S + V
simple: In light of the evidence, he was found not guilty.

chinese: 處於不確定狀態 / 懸而未決
example: His visa application is still in limbo while the authorities check his background.
simple: The project has been in limbo for months.

example: The statue was built in memory of the brave soldiers who died in the war.
pattern: S + be built in memory of + 人
simple: They held a concert in memory of the singer.

example: Chocolate is fine as long as you eat it in moderation as part of a balanced diet.
simple: You should drink coffee in moderation.

chinese: 整齊的 / 妥當的 / 依序地
example: Is your passport in order for the international trip next week?
simple: Keep your room in order.

example: He's a vegetarian; in other words, he doesn't eat any meat or fish.
pattern: S + V; in other words, S + V
simple: She is busy; in other words, she can't come.

example: The project's success was due in part to the team's strong communication skills.
simple: The problem was caused in part by bad luck.

example: I love Italian food, and I am fond of pasta in particular.
simple: Is there anything in particular you want to do?

example: Many coastal communities are in peril due to rising sea levels and erosion.
simple: The ship was in peril during the storm.

example: You must apply for the passport in person so that they can verify your identity.
simple: I have never met the author in person.

chinese: 就緒 / 準備妥當 / 在正確位置
example: All the security measures are now in place for the president's visit tomorrow.
simple: The pieces of the puzzle are all in place.

example: The new policy sounds good in theory, but it remains to be seen how it will work in practice.
pattern: S + V; however, in practice, S + V
simple: In practice, the rules are often ignored.

example: I agree with your proposal in principle, but we need to discuss the specific details later.
pattern: S + agree in principle with + N
simple: In principle, the plan is acceptable.

example: The manager asked to speak with the employee in private to discuss the recent incident.
pattern: S + speak/talk to sb in private
simple: Can we talk in private for a moment?

example: The construction of the new library is currently in progress and will be finished next year.
simple: Quiet, please! Examination in progress.

example: Many young people leave their hometowns and move to big cities in pursuit of better career opportunities.
pattern: S + V + in pursuit of + N
simple: He spent his life in pursuit of the truth.

chinese: 以實際價值計算 / 扣除通膨後
example: Although salaries have risen, they have actually decreased in real terms because of high inflation.
simple: The budget was cut in real terms.

example: He received a gold watch in recognition of his forty years of loyal service to the company.
pattern: S + receive N in recognition of + N
simple: She won an award in recognition of her research.

example: The company's performance has improved significantly in relation to the previous year.
pattern: S + increase/change in relation to + N
simple: We discussed issues in relation to safety.

chinese: 回顧起來 / 事後看來
example: In retrospect, I should have spent more time with my family instead of working so late every night.
pattern: In retrospect, S + should have + p.p.
simple: In retrospect, the decision was a mistake.

example: The explorers traveled deep into the jungle in search of the legendary lost city.
pattern: S + travel/go + in search of + N
simple: They headed west in search of gold.

example: Strawberries are much cheaper and sweeter when they are in season.
simple: Which fruits are in season right now?

example: In spite of the heavy traffic, we managed to arrive at the airport on time.
pattern: In spite of + N, S + V
simple: In spite of his age, he still plays soccer.

example: I'm sorry, but that model of smartphone is currently not in stock; we will have more next week.
simple: We have plenty of batteries in stock.

example: The team won three championships in succession, setting a new national record.
simple: Two accidents happened in succession.

example: The two departments worked in tandem to ensure the project was completed before the deadline.
pattern: A work in tandem with B
simple: The software works in tandem with the hardware.

example: In terms of cost, the train is much cheaper than flying to London.
pattern: In terms of + N, S + V
simple: In terms of popularity, basketball is number one.

chinese: 在缺乏...的情況下 / 在...不在時
example: In the absence of a clear plan, the team struggled to coordinate their efforts effectively.
pattern: In the absence of + N, S + V
simple: In the absence of proof, we cannot blame him.

chinese: 在(災難等)發生之後 / 在...的餘波中
example: In the aftermath of the earthquake, many families were left without electricity or water.
pattern: In the aftermath of + N, S + V
simple: The town was rebuilt in the aftermath of the war.

chinese: 在...背景下 / 就...的前後文來看
example: You need to understand the historical background to interpret the data in the context of that era.
pattern: view N in the context of + N
simple: Consider his comments in the context of the meeting.

example: The manager kept the employees in the dark about the company's financial problems.
pattern: S + keep sb in the dark about + N
simple: I was completely in the dark about the surprise party.

chinese: 萬一發生... / 如果發生...
example: In the event of a fire, please use the stairs instead of the elevator.
pattern: In the event of + N, S + V
simple: In the event of rain, the game will be canceled.

example: She showed great courage and determination in the face of extreme adversity.
pattern: S + remain + adj + in the face of + N
simple: He stayed calm in the face of danger.

example: Investing in renewable energy might be expensive now, but it will save money in the long run.
pattern: S + will be better/worth it in the long run
simple: Exercise is beneficial in the long run.

example: The mechanic will fix your car tomorrow; in the meantime, you can borrow mine.
pattern: S + V; in the meantime, S + V
simple: Dinner is cooking; in the meantime, let's set the table.

chinese: 在籌劃中 / 在進行中
example: The tech company has several innovative new products in the pipeline for next year.
pattern: S + have + N + in the pipeline
simple: More tax cuts are in the pipeline.

example: There are several historical landmarks located in the vicinity of the train station.
pattern: located in the vicinity of + N
simple: The crowd was in the vicinity of 5,000 people.

chinese: 繼...之後 / 在...的影響下
example: New safety laws were introduced in the wake of the ferry disaster to prevent future accidents.
pattern: N + happen in the wake of + N
simple: Unemployment rose in the wake of the crisis.

example: In theory, anyone can become a billionaire if they work hard enough, but it's rare in reality.
simple: In theory, the system should be perfectly safe.

chinese: 保持聯繫 / 了解現狀
example: Even though we live in different countries, we stay in touch through social media every day.
pattern: S + keep/stay in touch with + 人
simple: Are you still in touch with your old friends?

example: The package was damaged in transit, so I requested a refund from the seller.
simple: The goods are still in transit.

chinese: 輪流地 / 進而 / 接著
example: Increased stress leads to poor sleep, which in turn affects your productivity at work.
pattern: A leads to B, which in turn leads to C
simple: The students answered the questions in turn.

chinese: 徒勞無功地 / 白費力氣地
example: He tried to fix the broken computer himself, but his efforts were in vain.
pattern: S + try to V + in vain
simple: All our plans were in vain.

example: In view of the recent budget cuts, we have to cancel the year-end party.
pattern: In view of + N, S + V
simple: In view of the evidence, the suspect was released.

chinese: 禁止進入的 / 超出範圍的
example: The construction site is out of bounds to the public for safety reasons.
pattern: N + be out of bounds to + 人
simple: The forest is out of bounds after dark.

chinese: 上氣不接下氣 / 喘不過氣
example: He ran all the way to the station and arrived there completely out of breath.
simple: The climb left me out of breath.

chinese: 不符合某人的個性 / 反常
example: It was very out of character for the quiet student to start a fight in school.
pattern: It be out of character for sb to + V
simple: His rude comment was out of character.

example: The car hit a patch of ice and spun out of control into the ditch.
pattern: N + be / get out of control
simple: The fire quickly got out of control.

example: The doctors say that the patient is finally out of danger after the successful surgery.
simple: The child is still weak but out of danger.

example: The information in this guidebook is out of date; you should buy a newer version.
simple: Those clothes are completely out of date.

example: Most of the children in this village spend their entire summer holidays out of doors.
pattern: S + spend time out of doors
simple: It is healthy to live out of doors.

example: The photograph was out of focus because my hands were shaking when I took it.
simple: The edges of the image are out of focus.

chinese: 失去控制 / 立即 (拒絕)
example: The argument between the two neighbors quickly got out of hand and the police were called.
pattern: The situation / The party + got out of hand
simple: He rejected the proposal out of hand.

chinese: 在安全的地方 / 避開危險
example: The residents were evacuated to ensure that they were out of harm's way before the storm hit.
pattern: Keep N out of harm's way
simple: Parents try to keep their children out of harm's way.

chinese: 言行越軌 / 不恰當 / 不一致
example: His rude comments to the manager were completely out of line, and he was fired immediately.
pattern: Someone's behavior + be out of line
simple: Your price is out of line with the market.

chinese: 運氣不好 / 沒機會了
example: If you were looking for tickets for tonight's concert, I'm afraid you are out of luck.
simple: I was out of luck and missed the last bus.

chinese: 故障 / 秩序混亂 / (言行)不當
example: The elevator in our building is out of order again, so we have to use the stairs.
simple: The public telephone is out of order.

chinese: 不在正確位置 / 不得體 / 格格不入
example: I felt a bit out of place at the formal party because I was wearing jeans.
simple: The book was out of place on the shelf.

chinese: 自掏腰包 / 賠錢 / 缺錢
example: I am fifty dollars out of pocket after paying for my friend's lunch today.
pattern: S + be + 金額 + out of pocket
simple: He was left out of pocket after the trip.

example: I used to play the piano well, but I am out of practice now that I have a job.
pattern: S + be out of practice (at N/V-ing)
simple: I am out of practice at playing tennis.

example: That specific novel has been out of print for decades and is very hard to find.
simple: The dictionary is currently out of print.

chinese: 不成比例 / 誇大其詞
example: The media blew the minor incident out of proportion to attract more viewers.
simple: The reaction was out of proportion to the mistake.

chinese: 拿不到 / 達不到 (目標)
example: Keep all medicines on the top shelf, out of reach of small children.
pattern: be out of reach for/of sb
simple: Success seemed out of reach for him.

example: Fresh cherries are very expensive in winter because they are out of season.
simple: You shouldn't hunt deer out of season.

chinese: 體位不佳 / 身體走樣 / 變形
example: I get tired easily after climbing stairs because I have been out of shape lately.
pattern: S + be / get out of shape
simple: The heavy box was squashed out of shape.

example: Out of sight, out of mind; people often forget about friends they don't see often.
simple: The plane flew out of sight.

chinese: 身體不適 / 心情不佳
example: She has been feeling a bit out of sorts lately, so she decided to take a day off.
simple: He's out of sorts today, so leave him alone.

example: The politician was out of step with the majority of voters on the issue of taxes.
pattern: S + be out of step with + N
simple: He was out of step with the music.

example: I am sorry, but the laptop you are looking for is currently out of stock.
simple: The book is out of stock in all stores.

example: The audio and video in the movie were out of sync, which was very annoying.
pattern: A and B + be out of sync
simple: The lights were out of sync with the music.

chinese: 不可能的 / 不予考慮的
example: Buying a new car right now is out of the question because we need to save money for the house.
pattern: N + be out of the question
simple: Traveling during the exam period is out of the question.

chinese: 偏僻的 / 不礙事的 / 解決了
example: Now that the final exams are out of the way, I can finally enjoy my summer holiday.
pattern: get N out of the way
simple: He lives in a house that is a bit out of the way.

example: I'm sorry, but we are out of time, so we have to end the meeting here.
simple: The drummer was slightly out of time with the band.

chinese: 失去聯繫 / 不了解現狀
example: The elderly politician was criticized for being out of touch with the needs of young people.
pattern: be out of touch with + N
simple: I've been out of touch with him for years.

example: The piano is slightly out of tune, so it needs to be adjusted by a professional.
simple: He sang loudly but completely out of tune.

example: He has been out of work for six months and is struggling to pay his rent.
simple: The factory closure left many people out of work.

example: You should clean your contact lenses on a daily basis to prevent eye infections.
pattern: S + V + on a daily basis
simple: He exercises on a daily basis.

chinese: 與...同等 / 與...不相上下
example: The quality of this local wine is on a par with the best French brands.
pattern: A + be on a par with + B
simple: His latest novel is on a par with his early masterpieces.

example: The machines are inspected on a regular basis to ensure they are working safely.
pattern: S + V + on a regular basis
simple: We meet for coffee on a regular basis.

chinese: 預算吃緊 / 預算有限
example: As a student living abroad, she had to learn how to cook on a tight budget.
pattern: S + be/live on a tight budget
simple: The movie was filmed on a tight budget.

example: The outdoor concert was canceled on account of the heavy rain and thunder.
pattern: S + be V-ed on account of + N
simple: He retired early on account of ill health.

example: On average, women in this country live longer than men.
simple: I work 40 hours a week on average.

chinese: 總的來說 / 權衡之下
example: There are pros and cons to the plan, but on balance, it seems like a good idea.
simple: On balance, I think we made the right choice.

example: The lawyer made a statement on behalf of his client regarding the lawsuit.
pattern: S + V + on behalf of + 人
simple: I am writing on behalf of my mother.

example: All passengers are requested to be on board the plane at least 20 minutes before departure.
simple: We need to get more investors on board for this project.

example: My father is currently in Singapore on business, but he will return this Friday.
simple: He travels to London once a month on business.

example: As a surgeon, he has to be on call during the weekends in case of emergencies.
simple: The plumber is on call 24 hours a day.

example: You can borrow my laptop on condition that you return it before tomorrow evening.
pattern: S + V + on condition that + S + V
simple: He agreed to help on condition that he was paid.

chinese: 按計畫進行 / 朝著正確目標
example: Despite the early delays, the project is now on course to be finished by December.
pattern: N + be on course to + V
simple: The company is on course for record profits.

example: Many ancient artifacts are on display in the national museum for the public to see.
pattern: N + be on display at + 地方
simple: The latest fashion trends are on display here.

example: There is always at least one nurse on duty in the intensive care unit at all times.
simple: The guard is on duty until 8 a.m.

chinese: 存檔中 / 有記錄在案
example: We will keep your resume on file and contact you if a suitable position opens up.
simple: The police have his fingerprints on file.

chinese: 著火中 / (引申) 表現極佳
example: Call the fire department immediately! The abandoned building across the street is on fire.
simple: The forest was on fire for three days.

example: It's only a ten-minute walk from here, so we decided to go there on foot.
pattern: S + go to + 地方 + on foot
simple: Explorers crossed the desert on foot.

chinese: 提防 / 站崗 / 警惕
example: Citizens should be on guard against suspicious emails that ask for personal information.
pattern: S + be on guard against + N
simple: The soldiers were on guard at the gate.

chinese: 在場 / 在手邊 / 可供使用
example: Medical staff were on hand at the marathon to treat any runners who felt unwell.
pattern: S + be on hand to + V
simple: I have some extra cash on hand.

chinese: 等待中 / 暫停 / (電話)掛起
example: The construction project was put on hold due to a lack of government funding.
simple: I was on hold for ten minutes before I talked to a real person.

example: She bought the expensive designer bag on impulse and regretted it the next day.
simple: He joined the army on impulse.

example: The manager is currently on leave, so please contact his assistant for urgent matters.
simple: He is on sick leave today.

example: The famous painting is currently on loan to a museum in New York.
pattern: N + be on loan from/to + 單位
simple: I have several books on loan from the library.

chinese: 提供的 / 正在出售的
example: There is a wide variety of fresh seafood on offer at the local night market.
simple: Special discounts are on offer this week.

example: The book you requested is currently on order and should arrive within three days.
simple: We have more supplies on order.

example: The plan looks great on paper, but we need to see how it works in reality.
simple: The team is strong on paper but weak on the field.

example: Police officers are on patrol in the city center to ensure public safety during the festival.
simple: The guards are on patrol 24 hours a day.

example: I am sure he broke the vase on purpose because he was angry with me.
simple: I didn't do it on purpose.

chinese: 有紀錄的 / 公開紀錄的
example: This has been the hottest summer on record, according to the weather bureau.
simple: He is on record as opposing the plan.

example: Everything in the department store will be on sale starting from next Friday.
simple: Are these shoes on sale?

example: Despite the heavy rain, the construction project is still proceeding on schedule.
simple: The train arrived exactly on schedule.

example: A collection of rare paintings by the local artist is currently on show at the museum.
simple: New car models are on show at the exhibition.

example: The company provides on site childcare for employees to make their lives easier.
simple: There were no doctors on site when the accident happened.

example: Bus drivers have been on strike for three days, causing massive traffic problems.
simple: The factory workers are on strike for higher wages.

chinese: 隨時可用的 / 桶裝的 (飲料)
example: At the university, expert advice is always on tap for students who need guidance.
simple: They have cold beer on tap.

example: The soldiers remained on the alert for any signs of enemy movement in the forest.
pattern: S + be on the alert for + N
simple: Parents must be on the alert for potential hazards.

example: I'm not tired at all; on the contrary, I feel full of energy after the walk.
pattern: Not ..., on the contrary, S + V
simple: He isn't lazy; on the contrary, he is very hardworking.

chinese: 處於防禦地位 / 懷有戒心
example: The politician was forced on the defensive by the reporter's sharp questions.
pattern: S + be / get on the defensive
simple: Don't be so on the defensive; I'm just trying to help.

chinese: 準時地 / 一秒不差地
example: The meeting started at ten o'clock on the dot, and no latecomers were allowed.
simple: He arrived at 7 p.m. on the dot.

example: The students were on the edge of their seats while watching the exciting climax of the movie.
simple: He has been on edge all day waiting for the results.

example: On the face of it, the offer seems very generous, but you should read the fine print.
pattern: On the face of it, S + V
simple: On the face of it, it's a simple problem.

chinese: 匆忙地 / 隨機應變地 / 忙碌中
example: I didn't have time to prepare a speech, so I had to make it up on the fly.
simple: He makes decisions on the fly.

example: As a busy mother of three, she is always on the go from morning until night.
pattern: S + be always on the go
simple: I like to eat my breakfast on the go.

example: The application was rejected on the grounds of insufficient evidence.
pattern: rejected/accepted on the grounds of + N
simple: She retired on the grounds of ill health.

example: With new technology, significant changes in the medical field are on the horizon.
simple: A major economic crisis is on the horizon.

example: Because it was the restaurant's opening day, the dessert was on the house.
simple: Have another drink; it's on the house.

example: Police reports suggest that property crimes in the city are on the increase.
simple: Online shopping is on the increase.

chinese: 在工作中 / 在職期間
example: New employees usually receive on the job training to learn how to operate the machinery.
pattern: S + learn sth on the job
simple: He got injured while on the job.

example: The police warned residents to stay indoors because a dangerous criminal is on the loose.
simple: There is a tiger on the loose from the zoo.

example: This brand-new smartphone will be on the market early next month.
simple: Is your house on the market yet?

example: In modern society, many people are constantly on the move and rely on mobile technology.
pattern: S + be always on the move
simple: The army is on the move.

chinese: 精確地 / 一點也不差
example: The concert started at exactly eight o'clock on the nose.
simple: The cost was five hundred dollars on the nose.

example: Nuclear energy is very clean; on the other hand, the disposal of radioactive waste is a huge problem.
pattern: S + V; on the other hand, S + V
simple: I want to go; on the other hand, I should save money.

example: The hungry lion was on the prowl in the tall grass, searching for a zebra.
simple: Criminals are on the prowl in this area at night.

example: He decided to start his own business on the quiet without telling his boss.
simple: They were seeing each other on the quiet.

chinese: 面臨破裂 / 處於困境 / 加冰塊
example: Their marriage has been on the rocks for months, and they are now considering a divorce.
pattern: A relationship + be on the rocks
simple: I'll have a whiskey on the rocks.

chinese: 在逃 / 奔波 / 忙碌
example: The suspect has been on the run from the police for over a week.
pattern: S + be on the run (from + 人)
simple: He's always on the run between two jobs.

example: The project was put on the shelf due to a lack of funding from the government.
pattern: N + be put on the shelf
simple: Old equipment is just sitting on the shelf.

chinese: 兼職 / 秘密地 / (食物)另外放
example: He works as a waiter, but he also earns some extra money as a musician on the side.
pattern: S + do sth on the side
simple: I'll have the dressing on the side, please.

example: The manager offered her the job on the spot because he was so impressed by her skills.
simple: He had to make a decision on the spot.

example: On the surface, he seems very friendly, but deep down he is actually quite selfish.
simple: The lake looks calm on the surface.

chinese: 提交討論的 / 考慮中的
example: The management's proposal is still on the table, waiting for the union's response.
simple: Withdraw your offer; it's no longer on the table.

example: The company was on the verge of bankruptcy before the new investor saved it.
pattern: S + be on the verge of + N/V-ing
simple: She was on the verge of tears.

example: His popularity among young voters seems to be on the wane lately.
simple: The storm is on the wane.

chinese: 憤怒準備開戰 / 怒氣沖沖
example: Watch out! The teacher is on the warpath because someone cheated on the test.
simple: The boss is on the warpath again.

example: I am already on the way to the station; I should be there in ten minutes.
simple: Winter is on the way.

example: There were a few minor mistakes, but on the whole, your essay was excellent.
simple: On the whole, the weather was good.

example: If you want to make a good impression, you must arrive at the interview on time.
simple: The flight departed exactly on time.

chinese: 在...之上 / 除...之外 / 掌控中
example: On top of her regular job, she also works as a volunteer at the hospital on weekends.
pattern: On top of N, S + V
simple: I have so much work on top of my exams.

example: The suspect is currently on trial for the theft of the jewelry from the museum.
simple: We're using the new software on trial.

chinese: 憑信用 / 賒帳 / 基於信任
example: In this small village, you can buy groceries on trust and pay for them at the end of the month.
simple: Don't take everything he says on trust.

example: The sculptures will be on view to the public for the entire month of October.
simple: His latest work is on view at the gallery.

chinese: 成藥 / 非處方藥 / 櫃台交易
example: You can buy simple painkillers over the counter without a doctor's prescription.
simple: OTC drugs are widely available.

chinese: 隨著時間過去 / 逐漸
example: Learning a new language is difficult at first, but it gets easier over time with practice.
simple: The problem resolved itself over time.

chinese: 跟上...的步調 / 並駕齊驅
type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: It is hard for local businesses to keep pace with the rapid changes in the global market.
pattern: S + struggle to keep pace with + N
simple: He ran fast to keep pace with the lead runner.

type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: You should make a habit of reviewing your notes every evening after school.
pattern: S + should make a habit of + V-ing
simple: I make a habit of drinking water before breakfast.

chinese: 得到回報 / 付清 / 成功
example: All his hard work finally paid off when he was accepted into his dream university.
simple: The long hours of practice really paid off.

example: The train slowly began to pick up speed as it left the station.
pattern: N + start(s) to pick up speed
simple: The project is picking up speed now.

chinese: 埋頭苦幹 / 堅持不懈地做
example: If you keep plugging away at your studies, you will surely see results.
pattern: S + keep plugging away at + N
simple: He's been plugging away at that novel for years.

chinese: 艱難地完成 / 費勁地閱讀
example: It took me all weekend to plod through that long and boring report.
simple: We ploded through the deep snow.

chinese: 依然有效 / 同樣適用
example: What was said in the past still holds true for the current situation.
simple: This rule holds true in all cases.

example: Please make certain that all the lights are turned off before you leave the building.
simple: I made certain that the door was locked.

example: Advances in technology have made possible things that were once considered science fiction.
pattern: S + make it possible for sb to + V
simple: The scholarship made it possible for him to study abroad.

example: The company will make public its quarterly earnings report next Monday.
pattern: S + refuse to make N public
simple: They made the secret files public.

example: The soldiers were ordered to make ready for the upcoming mission.
simple: The hotel staff is making the room ready for the guest.

example: The cruelty shown by the dictator was beyond belief.
simple: The beauty of the sunrise was beyond belief.

example: Tension between the two rival gangs finally reached a boiling point last night.
simple: His anger reached a boiling point.

example: I had a brief encounter with an old friend at the train station yesterday.
pattern: S + have a brief encounter with + 人
simple: It was a brief encounter but a meaningful one.

chinese: 如魚離水 / 在陌生環境感到不適應
example: Living in the big city for the first time, he felt like a fish out of water.
pattern: S + feel like a fish out of water
simple: I was a fish out of water at the tech conference.

example: Let's get the ball rolling by discussing the first item on our agenda.
simple: Who wants to get the ball rolling?

chinese: 加倍努力 / 多盡一份力
example: The hotel staff always go the extra mile to make sure their guests are comfortable.
pattern: S + be willing to go the extra mile
simple: She went the extra mile to finish the task.

chinese: 理所當然 / 是合乎邏輯的
example: It stands to reason that if you study harder, you will get better grades.
pattern: It stands to reason that + S + V
simple: It stands to reason that he'll be late.

chinese: 熟悉竅門 / 了解規則
example: It will take you a few weeks to know the ropes in this new office.
pattern: S + need time to know the ropes
simple: He has worked here long enough to know the ropes.

chinese: 最後手段 / 最後的訴求
example: War should always be the last resort after all diplomatic efforts have failed.
pattern: S + be used as a last resort
simple: We will only call the police as a last resort.

chinese: 竭盡全力 / 徹底搜查 / 不遺餘力
example: The police promised to leave no stone unturned in their search for the missing person.
pattern: S + leave no stone unturned to + V
simple: I will leave no stone unturned to find the truth.

example: Hey! Long time no see! How have you been since graduation?
simple: I saw my old neighbor today; long time no see!

chinese: 事實上 / 平心而論的 / 務實的
example: As a matter of fact, I have already finished the work you assigned me.
pattern: As a matter of fact, S + V
simple: He spoke in a cold, matter-of-fact tone.

example: For the rescue team, every second counts as it is a matter of life and death.
pattern: N + is a matter of life and death
simple: They are dealing with a matter of life and death.

example: Whether modern art is beautiful or not is often a matter of opinion.
pattern: N + is (just) a matter of opinion
simple: Which movie is best is a matter of opinion.

example: I refused to accept the money as a matter of principle, not because it wasn't enough.
pattern: S + V + as a matter of principle
simple: It is a matter of principle for him.

example: He is so talented that it is only a matter of time before he becomes famous.
pattern: It is (only) a matter of time.
simple: It's only a matter of time before we win.

chinese: 大熔爐 (指文化多樣的社會)
example: New York City is often described as a melting pot where people from all over the world live together.
pattern: N + is a cultural melting pot
simple: The university is a melting pot of ideas.

chinese: 錯失良機 / 錯過機會
example: You should have bought that house last year; now you've missed the boat.
pattern: S + don't want to miss the boat
simple: Hurry up, or you'll miss the boat.

example: I think you're missing the point of the story; it's about friendship, not money.
simple: He missed the point of my joke.

chinese: 排除萬難 / 竭盡全力 / 翻天覆地
example: The parents would move heaven and earth to find a cure for their child's illness.
pattern: S + will move heaven and earth to + V
simple: I'll move heaven and earth to be there on time.

chinese: 沒什麼大不了 / 小事一樁
example: Don't worry about spilling the water; it's no big deal.
simple: Losing the game was no big deal.

example: There is no doubt that climate change is one of the biggest challenges we face today.
pattern: There is no doubt that + S + V
simple: No doubt, he is the best player on the team.

chinese: 別放在心上 / 沒惡意 / 不計較
example: Even though you won the game, I hope there are no hard feelings between us.
pattern: I hope there are no hard feelings.
simple: We broke up but there are no hard feelings.

chinese: 毫無好感 / 互相嫌惡
example: There is no love lost between the two politicians, who have been rivals for years.
pattern: There is no love lost between A and B.
simple: There was no love lost between the two companies.

chinese: 無人是孤島 (指人與人需互相依存)
example: We all need support from others sometimes; as the saying goes, 'no man is an island.'
pattern: Remember that no man is an island.
simple: No man is an island, so we must cooperate.

chinese: 了不起的成就 / 非同尋常的壯舉
example: Climbing Mount Everest is no mean feat, even for experienced climbers.
simple: Winning the Nobel Prize is no mean feat.

example: We haven't heard from the rescue team yet, but let's hope no news is good news.
pattern: Remember, no news is good news.
simple: They haven't called yet, but no news is good news.

chinese: 無附加條件 / 沒限制
example: The generous donor gave a million dollars to the charity with no strings attached.
pattern: S + offer N with no strings attached
simple: It's a free gift with no strings attached.

example: Both choices were bad, leaving me in a no-win situation.
pattern: S + be in a no-win situation
simple: The manager was caught in a no-win situation.

example: Do you think he'll lend you money? Not a chance.
simple: Go back to work? Not a chance on a Sunday!

example: The game isn't over yet, not by a long shot.
pattern: Not ..., not by a long shot.
simple: He is not the best player, not by a long shot.

example: I wasn't offended in the least by his remarks.
simple: It doesn't matter in the least.

phrase: not the end of the world
example: Failing the test is bad, but it is not the end of the world.
pattern: N + be not the end of the world
simple: Making a mistake is not the end of the world.

chinese: 簡直就是 / 毫無疑問是
example: His performance was nothing short of a miracle.
pattern: N + be nothing short of + N
simple: The recovery was nothing short of amazing.

chinese: 不值得一提 / 平淡無奇
example: The hotel was okay, but the food was nothing to write home about.
pattern: N + be nothing to write home about
simple: His latest movie is nothing to write home about.

example: She spent the afternoon clearing up various odds and ends in the house.
pattern: S + V + various odds and ends
simple: I have a few odds and ends to finish before I go.

chinese: 人煙稀少的 / 偏僻的
example: We love to visit places that are off the beaten track when we travel.
pattern: N + be off the beaten track
simple: The farmhouse is a bit off the beaten track.

chinese: 即興地 / 未經準備地
example: I didn't prepare a speech, so I spoke off the cuff.
simple: She gave a brilliant speech off the cuff.

chinese: 脫離困境 / 擺脫責任
example: He was supposed to do the dishes, but his sister let him off the hook.
pattern: S + be / get off the hook
simple: The lawyer got his client off the hook.

example: The report's conclusions were completely off the mark.
simple: Your guess was way off the mark.

chinese: 不列入紀錄 / 私下底說
example: Strictly off the record, the company is planning to close this office.
pattern: Tell sb sth off the record
simple: She spoke to the journalist off the record.

chinese: 現成的 / 現貨供應的
example: We can buy this software off the shelf instead of designing it ourselves.
simple: Off-the-shelf products are often cheaper.

phrase: off the top of your head
chinese: 憑記憶 / 不假思索地
example: I can't remember the number off the top of my head.
pattern: S + cannot V + off the top of one's head
simple: Just tell me what you know off the top of your head.

chinese: 預算極少地 / 拮据地
example: The students traveled around Europe on a shoestring.
simple: The project was started on a shoestring budget.

chinese: 輕而易舉地獲得 / 拱手相送
example: He expected to have success handed to him on a silver platter.
pattern: hand N to sb on a silver platter
simple: She was given everything on a silver platter.

example: The television is on the blink again; we should call a technician.
simple: The fridge has been on the blink all week.

chinese: 準確 / 正中要害 / 準時
example: Her guess was right on the button.
pattern: S + be right on the button
simple: The delivery arrived at noon on the button.

example: A change of government seems to be on the cards.
simple: Promotion is on the cards for her.

example: The meeting started at 10 o'clock on the dot.
simple: He arrived at seven on the dot.

chinese: 猶豫不決 / 持觀望態度
example: I'm still on the fence about which car to buy.
pattern: S + be (still) on the fence about + N
simple: Voters are on the fence regarding the new tax.

chinese: 隨機應變地 / 在忙碌中
example: I didn't have time to prepare, so I made the decision on the fly.
simple: He learned how to use the software on the fly.

example: He paid for the car on the nail.
pattern: pay for N on the nail
simple: Contractors want their money on the nail.

chinese: 以防萬一 / 抱著一線希望
example: I applied for the job on the off chance that they might hire me.
pattern: S + V + on the off chance that + S + V
simple: I called him on the off chance that he was home.

phrase: on the spur of the moment
chinese: 一時衝動地 / 臨時起意
example: We decided to go to the beach on the spur of the moment.
pattern: do sth on the spur of the moment
simple: He joined the party on the spur of the moment.

example: He has won five games on the trot.
pattern: S + V + N + on the trot
simple: I've been working for ten hours on the trot.

example: No thanks, I'm on the wagon now.
simple: He has been on the wagon for six months.

chinese: 一勞永逸地 / 徹底地
example: We need to settle this matter once and for all.
pattern: S + V + once and for all
simple: He quit smoking once and for all.

chinese: 一朝被蛇咬，十年怕草繩
example: He is very careful about lending money now; once bitten twice shy.
simple: I won't try that again; once bitten twice shy.

chinese: 罕見地 / 千載難逢地
example: My brother lives in Canada, so I only get to see him once in a blue moon.
pattern: S + V + once in a blue moon
simple: He cleans his room only once in a blue moon.

chinese: 一生一次的 / 千載難逢的
example: Traveling to Antarctica is a once in a lifetime opportunity that most people dream of.
pattern: a once in a lifetime + N
simple: Winning the lottery is a once in a lifetime event.

example: Once upon a time, there was a brave knight who lived in a castle.
pattern: Once upon a time, there was/were...
simple: Once upon a time, animals could talk.

example: Her handmade jewelry is truly one of a kind; you won't find it anywhere else.
pattern: N + be one of a kind
simple: My grandfather was one of a kind.

example: Don't try to solve the entire problem today; just take it one step at a time.
pattern: Take/Do it one step at a time.
simple: Recovery takes time, so take it one step at a time.

chinese: 顯而易見的案件 / 容易處理的事
example: With so much evidence against him, it seemed like an open and shut case.
pattern: N + be an open and shut case
simple: The detective finished the open and shut case quickly.

chinese: 坦誠的人 / 易懂的事物
example: My sister's face is an open book; you can always tell what she is thinking.
simple: The teacher's personal life was an open book.

example: It is an open secret that the two companies are planning to merge next year.
pattern: It is an open secret that + 子句
simple: His drinking problem was an open secret.

chinese: 在戶外活動 / 痊癒走動
example: After a week in the hospital, he is finally out and about again.
simple: On sunny days, everyone is out and about.

chinese: 處於孤立或危險的境地 / 冒險發言
example: He went out on a limb to support his friend when everyone else was criticizing him.
pattern: go out on a limb for sb
simple: Don't go out on a limb if you aren't sure.

example: Over and above his regular salary, he receives a significant bonus every year.
pattern: Over and above N, S + V
simple: He has expenses over and above his rent.

example: I'm glad the difficult exams are finally over and done with.
pattern: N + be (finally) over and done with
simple: The argument is over and done with.

chinese: 走下坡 / 老了 / 巔峰已過
example: He feels like he is over the hill now that he has turned forty.
simple: She proves she's not over the hill by winning.

chinese: 非常高興 / 興高采烈
example: She was over the moon when she heard that she had won the scholarship.
pattern: S + be over the moon about + N
simple: He's over the moon about his new baby.

chinese: 太過火 / 誇張 / 過分
example: Her reaction to the small spider was a bit over the top, don't you think?
pattern: S + be a bit over the top
simple: The decorations were way over the top.

chinese: 出外狂歡 / 痛飲慶祝
example: After the final exams, the students went out to paint the town red.
pattern: S + go out and paint the town red
simple: We painted the town red after our graduation.

example: Instead of admitting his mistake, he tried to pass the buck to his subordinates.
simple: The buck stops here (責任由我負).

example: The boss gave everyone a pat on the back for completing the project on time.
pattern: deserve a pat on the back
simple: Give yourself a pat on the back for a job well done.

chinese: 花大錢 / 付極高的代價
example: We had to pay through the nose for a hotel room during the holiday season.
pattern: S + have to pay through the nose for + N
simple: I paid through the nose for these tickets.

chinese: 小事一樁 / 輕而易舉的事
example: The math test was a piece of cake; I finished it in less than twenty minutes.
pattern: N + be a piece of cake
simple: Fixing this is a piece of cake.

chinese: 渺茫的希望 / 空頭支票 / 畫大餅
example: His plan to build a lunar colony is just pie in the sky for now.
pattern: N + be just pie in the sky
simple: Promises of lower taxes were pie in the sky.

chinese: 半信半疑 / 語帶保留
example: You should take everything he says with a pinch of salt because he likes to exaggerate.
pattern: take N with a pinch of salt
simple: I took the news with a pinch of salt.

example: His idea of becoming a famous movie star without any training is a pipe dream.
pattern: N + is nothing but a pipe dream
simple: Universal peace is still a pipe dream.

example: Dealing with those criminals is playing with fire; you should stay away from them.
simple: If you keep cheating, you're playing with fire.

example: He finally plucked up courage to ask her out on a date.
pattern: S + pluck up courage to + V
simple: She plucked up courage to speak to the boss.

chinese: 潑冷水 / 打擊...的熱情
example: I was excited about my new business idea, but he poured cold water on it immediately.
pattern: S + pour cold water on one's plan
simple: Don't pour cold water on her dreams.

chinese: 使...出名 / 使...受到關注
example: The success of the local music festival really put this small town on the map.
pattern: N + put + 地點 + on the map
simple: The discovery of oil put the region on the map.

example: The suspect was ordered to hand over his passport to the authorities immediately.
pattern: S + hand over + N + to + 人
simple: He handed over the keys to the new owner.

example: The volunteers began to hand out fliers about environmental protection to the passers-by.
simple: The teacher handed out the test papers.

chinese: 遞交 / 轉手 / (美式足球)遞球
example: After the meeting, the project manager will hand off the tasks to the design team.
pattern: S + hand off + N + to + 單位
simple: The relay runner handed off the baton.

example: Please remember to hand back the borrowed library books by the end of the week.
pattern: S + hand back + N + to + 人
simple: The teacher graded the essays and handed them back.

example: Students must hand in their assignments before the deadline or they will lose points.
simple: He handed in his resignation yesterday.

example: Could you please hand around these cookies to everyone in the room?
simple: She handed around a photo of her new baby.

chinese: 大量地 / 迅速地 (通常指賺錢或賠錢)
example: Since the new app was launched, the company has been making money hand over fist.
simple: Business is booming and we are making profits hand over fist.

example: The detective suspected that the bank manager was working hand in glove with the robbers.
pattern: A + work hand in glove with + B
simple: They have been working hand in glove on this project.

example: You have to hand it to her; she managed to finish the marathon despite her injury.
pattern: You have to hand it to + 人
simple: I have to hand it to him, he is a great salesman.

chinese: 無疑地 / 輕而易舉地
example: This is hands down the best chocolate cake I have ever eaten.
pattern: S + be hands down the best...
simple: She won the race hands down.

example: The manager took a hands off approach, letting the employees make their own decisions.
simple: Hands off! That's my personal computer.

chinese: 實際操作的 / 親身實踐的
example: The science museum offers many hands on exhibits that children really enjoy.
pattern: S + have hands on experience in + 領域
simple: He is a hands on manager who knows every detail.

example: With four young children and a full-time job, she certainly has her hands full.
simple: The police have their hands full with the investigation.

chinese: 束手無策 / 受限於規定而無法行動
example: I would like to help you, but my hands are tied by the strict company regulations.
simple: The judge said his hands were tied by the law.

example: By a show of hands, the majority of the students voted to go to the beach for the field trip.
pattern: By a show of hands, S + V
simple: Let's see a show of hands from those who agree.

chinese: 一鳥在手 (勝過二鳥在林) / 把握現有
example: I decided to accept the job offer now because a bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
pattern: Remember, a bird in the hand...
simple: He chose the safe option; a bird in the hand.

chinese: 畢恭畢敬地 / 請求原諒或幫助
example: He went back to his old boss hat in hand, asking if he could have his job back.
pattern: S + go hat in hand to + 人
simple: They had to go hat in hand to the bank for a loan.

example: After the long negotiation, the workers finally gained the upper hand in the dispute.
simple: The champion has the upper hand in this match.

chinese: 穩定的手 / 沉穩的控制
example: A surgeon must have a steady hand when performing such a delicate brain surgery.
simple: You need a steady hand to paint small details.

example: We should always be ready to lend a helping hand to those who are in need.
pattern: S + lend a helping hand to + 人
simple: Thank you for lending me a helping hand yesterday.

example: The soldiers were trained in hand-to-hand combat to defend themselves without weapons.
simple: The battle turned into a hand-to-hand struggle.

chinese: 僅夠糊口的 / 勉強度日的
example: Many families in the developing world still live a hand-to-mouth existence.
simple: They lived hand-to-mouth during the war.

chinese: 乾洗手 / 手部消毒液
example: Using hand sanitizer is an effective way to kill germs when soap and water are not available.
pattern: S + use hand sanitizer to + V
simple: I always keep a bottle of hand sanitizer in my bag.

example: The soldier threw a hand grenade toward the enemy bunker to clear the way.
simple: The explosion was caused by a hand grenade.

example: The delivery man used a hand truck to move the heavy boxes from the truck to the warehouse.
pattern: S + use a hand truck to + V
simple: A hand truck makes moving heavy items easier.

chinese: 神魂顛倒 / 深陷愛河
example: He fell head over heels in love with her the moment they first met at the library.
pattern: S + fall head over heels in love with + 人
simple: They are head over heels for each other.

example: The hikers were covered in mud from head to toe after walking through the swamp.
simple: She was dressed in black from head to toe.

example: The two vehicles were involved in a serious head on collision on the narrow bridge.
pattern: a head on collision / face N head on
simple: We decided to deal with the issue head on.

example: He dived head first into the swimming pool without checking the depth of the water.
pattern: S + dive/jump head first into + N
simple: She rushed head first into the project.

example: She was chosen to head up the new marketing department because of her extensive experience.
pattern: S + head up + 部門/計畫
simple: Who is heading up the investigation?

chinese: 攔截 / 阻止 / 出發
example: The government took immediate measures to head off an economic crisis.
pattern: S + take measures to head off + N
simple: They headed off the protesters at the gate.

example: It is getting dark, so we should start to head back to the campsite now.
pattern: S + head back to + 地方
simple: We headed back home after the movie.

example: We are planning to head out early tomorrow morning to avoid the heavy traffic.
pattern: S + head out + 時間/地點
simple: What time are you heading out?

example: As soon as the bell rang, the students all headed for the cafeteria for lunch.
simple: The ship is heading for the harbor.

example: The tour guide did a quick head count to ensure that no one was missing from the group.
simple: We need an accurate head count for the dinner.

example: The company's head office is located in London, but it has branches all over the world.
pattern: S + be located in + 地方
simple: I have to send these files to the head office.

example: The head coach was blamed for the team's poor performance during the season.
pattern: N + is the head coach of + 球隊
simple: The head coach signed a new contract.

chinese: 古怪的人 / 瘋子 (非正式)
example: Some people think he is a bit of a head case because he talks to his plants every day.
pattern: S + be a bit of a head case
simple: Don't listen to him, he's a total head case.

chinese: 頭部受傷 / 腦部創傷
example: The athlete suffered a serious head injury during the match and was taken to the hospital.
simple: Wearing a helmet reduces the risk of head trauma.

chinese: 國家元首 / 政府首腦
example: The head of state arrived at the summit to discuss international trade agreements.
pattern: N + serves as the head of state
simple: The President is both the head of state and head of government.

example: Please submit your research proposal to the head of department by next Friday.
simple: He was promoted to head of department.

example: In many tax documents, you need to specify who is considered the head of household.
pattern: N + is the head of household
simple: She is the sole breadwinner and head of household.

chinese: 班上的尖子 / 成績最優者
example: She has always been at the head of the class thanks to her dedication to her studies.
pattern: S + be (at) the head of the class
simple: He worked hard to stay at the head of the class.

chinese: 勉力維持 / 不陷於困境
example: The small bookstore is struggling to keep its head above water during the economic recession.
pattern: S + struggle to keep one's head above water
simple: I have so many bills that I can barely keep my head above water.

example: You need to face reality; you can't go through life with your head in the clouds all the time.
pattern: S + has one's head in the clouds
simple: He's a dreamer with his head in the clouds.

chinese: 保持警覺 / 環顧四周
example: When you are walking alone at night, it is important to keep your head on a swivel.
pattern: Keep your head on a swivel.
simple: The guards kept their heads on a swivel during the event.

chinese: 領先起步 / 有利地位
example: Attending a prestigious preschool can give children a head start in life.
pattern: give sb a head start in + N
simple: This training course gave me a head start in my career.

example: It takes a lot of courage to speak up against injustice when no one else will.
simple: She didn't have the courage to tell him the truth.

example: She found the courage and strength to rebuild her life after the devastating fire.
pattern: find the courage and strength to + V
simple: He showed great courage and determination during the crisis.

chinese: 戰火中的勇氣 / 壓力下的膽識
example: The soldier was awarded a medal for his courage under fire during the dangerous rescue mission.
simple: The CEO demonstrated courage under fire when the scandal broke.

example: It is rare to find a politician who has the courage of his convictions and refuses to compromise.
pattern: have the courage of one's convictions
simple: You must have the courage of your convictions if you want to lead.

example: He had to summon all his courage to ask his boss for a promotion during the meeting.
simple: She finally gathered enough courage to walk onto the stage.

chinese: 獲取勇氣 / 感到振奮
example: We can take courage from the fact that many others have successfully overcome similar obstacles.
simple: Take courage, my friend; things will get better.

example: The young boy demonstrated remarkable courage by jumping into the river to save his dog.
simple: The firefighters showed incredible courage.

example: Many people fail to achieve their goals simply because they lack the courage to start.
pattern: S + lack(s) the courage to + V
simple: He lacked the courage to admit his mistake.

example: Starting a new career in a foreign country requires immense courage and adaptability.
simple: It needs a lot of courage to do the right thing.

example: He drank a few glasses of whiskey to give himself some Dutch courage before the performance.
simple: A little Dutch courage helped him talk to her.

chinese: 道德勇氣 / 體能勇氣
example: Standing up for a classmate being bullied requires strong moral courage.
pattern: S + has the moral courage to + V
simple: Athletes need physical courage to play risky sports.

chinese: 極大的勇氣 / 非凡的勇氣
example: The scientist showed extraordinary courage in pursuing his research against public opinion.
simple: She faced her illness with immense courage.

chinese: 沈穩的勇氣 / 內心的勇氣
example: Real heroism often involves the quiet courage to keep going in spite of failure.
simple: She possessed an inner courage that surprised everyone.

chinese: 勇敢的行為 / 決定 / 努力
example: Saving the passengers from the burning bus was a truly courageous act.
simple: It was a courageous effort to finish the race while injured.

example: The company needs a courageous leader to navigate through these uncertain economic times.
simple: He is known as a courageous person.

chinese: 勇敢的立場 / 戰鬥 / 奮戰
example: The small group took a courageous stand against the giant corporation.
simple: They put up a courageous fight against the invaders.

example: I spent the afternoon thumbing through some old fashion magazines while waiting for my friend.
simple: He thumbed through the report quickly.

example: The committee gave a thumbs down to the proposed plan to build a new shopping mall.
pattern: S + give a thumbs down to + N
simple: They thumbed down my suggestion immediately.

chinese: 贊成 / 支持 / 成功
example: The audience gave the performers a big thumbs up for their outstanding show.
simple: The project finally got the thumbs up from the boss.

example: Don't forget to save your presentation on a thumb drive as a backup.
pattern: save N on a thumb drive
simple: I lost my thumb drive containing important files.

example: This large dictionary has a thumb index to help users find words more quickly.
simple: Check the thumb index to find the letter G.

example: The ancient warrior wore a heavy thumb ring to protect his hand while using a bow.
simple: She bought a silver thumb ring at the craft market.

chinese: 拇指螺絲 / (引申) 酷刑
example: A thumb screw allows you to tighten the bolt using only your fingers without any tools.
pattern: S + use a thumb screw to + V
simple: The light stand is secured with a thumb screw.

example: As a general rule of thumb, you should drink at least eight glasses of water a day.
pattern: As a rule of thumb, S + V
simple: A good rule of thumb is to save 10% of your income.

chinese: 園藝天賦 / 擅長種植
example: My grandmother has a green thumb; every plant she touches seems to grow perfectly.
simple: If you have a green thumb, your garden will be beautiful.

chinese: 受某人控制 / 唯命是從
example: The weak leader was completely under the thumb of his powerful advisors.
pattern: S + be under the thumb of + 人
simple: He's been under his wife's thumb for years.

example: I'm all thumbs today; I've already dropped three glasses while washing the dishes.
pattern: S + be all thumbs when it comes to + N
simple: When it comes to sewing, I'm all thumbs.

example: His car broke down on the highway, so he had to thumb a ride to the nearest gas station.
pattern: S + thumb a ride to + 地方
simple: We thumbed a ride across the country.

chinese: 嗤之以鼻 / 藐視 / 嘲笑
example: The rebellious teenager thumbed his nose at the school rules and wore whatever he liked.
pattern: S + thumb one's nose at + N
simple: They thumbed their noses at the traditional way of doing things.

example: The two children were bored, so they decided to have a thumb wrestle to see who was stronger.
simple: Do you want to thumb wrestle?

example: The musician played a soothing melody on the thumb piano during the acoustic session.
simple: Learning to play the thumb piano is relatively easy.

phrase: put a stop to / put an end to
example: The government is determined to put a stop to illegal logging in the rainforest.
pattern: S + must put a stop to + N
simple: We need to put an end to this argument.

example: The teachers put pressure on students to perform well in the final exams.
simple: Environmental groups put pressure on the company.

example: This modern school puts a lot of emphasis on creative thinking and art.
simple: The report puts emphasis on economic growth.

example: The sudden influx of tourists has put a strain on the local water supply.
pattern: N + put a strain on + N
simple: Financial problems put a strain on their marriage.

example: The manager had to put a limit on the number of people allowed in the hall.
pattern: S + put a limit on + N
simple: We should put a limit on our spending.

phrase: put a smile on one's face
chinese: 使某人微笑 / 讓某人開心
example: Receiving the handwritten card really put a smile on her face after a long day.
pattern: N + put a smile on one's face
simple: The good news put a smile on his face.

example: In the fairy tale, the evil witch put a spell on the prince and turned him into a frog.
pattern: S + put a spell on + 人
simple: Her beauty put a spell on everyone in the room.

chinese: 強加之詞 / 斷章取義
example: I never said that I hated the plan; don't put words into my mouth.
pattern: Don't put words into my mouth!
simple: Stop putting words into her mouth.

phrase: put effort / time / energy / money into
chinese: 投入努力 / 時間 / 精力 / 金錢於...
type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: If you want to master a language, you must put time and effort into practicing it every day.
simple: The company put billions of dollars into research.

chinese: 信任 / 相信 / 寄予希望於...
type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: You should only put your trust in people you have known for a long time.
pattern: S + put one's trust/faith in + 人
simple: We put our hope in the new policy.

phrase: put value / a price / a premium on
chinese: 重視 / 定價 / 對...給予高度重視
type: 動詞 + 名詞 + 介系詞
example: Our company puts a premium on honesty and integrity in all business dealings.
pattern: S + put a premium on + N
simple: You can't put a price on friendship.

chinese: 撥出 / 留存 / 撇開 (不理會)
example: He tries to put aside a portion of his salary every month for his future house.
simple: Let's put aside our differences and work together.

example: The children were told to put away their toys before dinner started.
simple: Put your books away and listen to me.

chinese: 放回原處 / 推遲 / 撥回 (時鐘)
example: Please put the dictionary back on the shelf when you are finished with it.
simple: The meeting has been put back until next week.

chinese: 放下 / 寫下 / 鎮壓 / 安樂死
example: The soldiers were sent to the capital to put down the rebellion.
simple: He put down his thoughts in a diary.

example: The committee has put forward a new proposal to reduce carbon emissions.
simple: He put forth a brilliant idea.

chinese: 進行 (抵抗) / 建造 / 提供住宿
example: The small team put up a brave fight, but they eventually lost the match.
pattern: S + put up a fight against + N
simple: Can you put me up for the night?

chinese: 撲滅 (火、菸) / 出版 / 趕走
example: Firefighters worked through the night to put out the forest fire.
simple: The company puts out a new catalog every year.

chinese: 接通 (電話) / 使經歷 / 完成 (交易)
example: Could you please put me through to the marketing department?
pattern: put sb through to + 人/部門
simple: His parents put him through college.

chinese: 推遲 / 延期 / 使人反感
example: We decided to put off the final decision until we have more information.
simple: Don't be put off by his rude manner.

chinese: 穿上 / 舉辦 / 增加 (體重) / 假裝
example: The school is planning to put on a musical performance at the end of the term.
simple: She put on her coat and left.

example: I don't know how she puts up with his constant complaining every day.
pattern: S + can no longer put up with + N
simple: We had to put up with the loud noise all night.

example: The team's success was put down to their excellent cooperation and hard work.
pattern: S + put + A + down to + B
simple: He put his mistake down to tiredness.

chinese: 感到不悅 / 受到干擾
example: She was quite put out by his late arrival and lack of an apology.
pattern: S + be (quite) put out by + N
simple: He felt put out when they didn't invite him.

chinese: 欺騙某人 / 瞞過某人
example: You can't put anything over on her; she is far too clever to be fooled.
pattern: S + try to put it over on + 人
simple: He tried to put a fast one over on the manager.

example: She is too kind and often feels put upon by her lazy colleagues.
pattern: S + feel put upon by + N
simple: Don't let yourself be put upon like that.

chinese: 優先考慮 / 放在...之前
example: A good leader always puts the needs of the team before their own interests.
simple: She puts her family before her career.

chinese: 解釋清楚 / 表達(觀點)
example: He is a great speaker and knows how to put his ideas across effectively.
pattern: S + fail to put the message across
simple: The teacher put the difficult concept across well.

example: Wait a moment while I put you through to the manager's office.
pattern: Could you put me through to + 人?
simple: I'll put you through now.

example: After three years of hard work, he decided to put in for a promotion.
pattern: S + put in for a transfer/raise
simple: She put in for a week off.

example: You should put your documents in order before the interview starts.
pattern: It is time to put your life in order.
simple: He put his financial affairs in order.

chinese: 暫停 / 擱置 / (電話)掛起
example: The expansion plan was put on hold until the economic situation improved.
simple: I was put on hold for ten minutes by the bank.

example: Nobody likes him because he always puts on airs as if he were better than everyone.
simple: She started to put on airs after getting the promotion.

example: He has put on a lot of weight since he stopped exercising last year.
pattern: S + put on + 數字 + kilos
simple: I tend to put on weight during winter.

chinese: 表演 / 演戲 / 裝模作樣
example: The kids put on a wonderful show for their parents on Christmas Eve.
pattern: S + put on a show to + V
simple: He's not really sick; he's just putting on a show.

chinese: 裝作勇敢 / 臉上強裝鎮定
example: Although she was deeply disappointed, she put on a brave face and congratulated the winner.
pattern: S + try to put on a brave face
simple: He put on a brave face despite the bad news.

example: The house was put on the market last week and was sold within three days.
pattern: N + be (now) put on the market
simple: The company is putting its new drug on the market.

example: He put his entire career on the line to defend his professional principles.
pattern: S + put one's life/reputation on the line
simple: Firefighters put their lives on the line every day.

example: The success of the local festival really put this small village on the map.
pattern: N + put + 地方 + on the map
simple: The discovery of oil put the region on the map.

chinese: 使人處於尷尬地位 / 逼某人當場表態
example: The reporter really put the politician on the spot with that difficult question.
pattern: S + be put on the spot
simple: I hate being put on the spot in front of my friends.

example: The science fair provides a great opportunity for students to put their ideas into practice.
pattern: It is time to put the theory into practice.
simple: They finally put the new plan into practice.

chinese: 強加之詞 / 曲解原意
example: I never said I wanted to quit; you are putting words into my mouth.
pattern: Don't put words into my mouth!
simple: The lawyer was accused of putting words into the witness's mouth.

chinese: 使某人放鬆 / 使人不拘束
example: The doctor's friendly manner immediately put the nervous patient at ease.
pattern: S + try to put + 人 + at ease
simple: A smile can put people at ease.

example: My advisor put me in touch with several experts in the field of renewable energy.
pattern: Can you put me in touch with + 人?
simple: I can put you in touch with a good lawyer.

chinese: 支持 / 後退 / 備份
example: The driver had to back up the car to let the ambulance pass through the narrow lane.
simple: Don't forget to back up your computer files.

chinese: 故障 / 崩潰 / 拆解
example: Our car broke down on the highway in the middle of the night, so we had to call for help.
simple: The teacher broke down the complex lesson into simple parts.

example: Burglars tried to break in through the back window while the family was away on vacation.
simple: As she was talking, he broke in to say he disagreed.

example: War broke out in the region after years of political tension and failed negotiations.
simple: He broke out of prison last week.

example: The new technology has brought about significant changes in the way we communicate.
simple: What brought about the sudden change in policy?

example: He was brought up by his grandparents in a small coastal village.
pattern: S + be brought up by + 人
simple: Don't bring up that incident again.

example: The manager is currently in a meeting, but he will call you back as soon as he is free.
simple: I'll call you back in ten minutes.

example: The outdoor concert was called off due to the approaching tropical storm.
simple: They decided to call off the wedding.

example: It took him a few minutes to calm down after the frightening encounter with the bear.
simple: Please calm down and tell me what happened.

example: Despite the constant interruptions, the teacher told the class to carry on with their work.
simple: He carried on working despite the noise.

example: Scientists are carrying out several experiments to test the safety of the new drug.
simple: They carried out the orders without question.

chinese: 跟上 / 追上 / 敘舊
example: He worked very hard during the weekend to catch up with the rest of the class.
simple: We went for coffee to catch up.

chinese: 登記 / 退房 / 查看
example: We need to check in at the hotel before four o'clock to confirm our reservation.
simple: Check out this cool website I found.

example: The volunteers spent the morning cleaning up the park after the music festival.
simple: Please clean your room up before guests arrive.

chinese: 偶然遇見 / 給人的印象
example: I came across some old family photographs while I was cleaning the attic.
simple: He comes across as a very confident person.

example: When do you plan to come back from your trip to the United States?
simple: Wait for me, I'll come back soon.

chinese: 進入 / 流行 / 起作用
example: Please come in and have a seat while you wait for the doctor.
simple: Long skirts are coming in again this season.

chinese: 快點 / 加油 / 進展 / 開始
example: Come on, we only have ten minutes before the train leaves!
simple: The light came on automatically.

example: The doctor advised him to cut down on fatty foods to improve his health.
simple: The city cut down several old trees.

chinese: 切斷 / 中斷 / 隔絕
example: Our water supply was cut off this morning because of the construction work nearby.
simple: We were cut off in the middle of our phone conversation.

chinese: 處理 / 應付 / 關於
example: The new manager has many difficult problems to deal with in her first month.
simple: His latest book deals with historical events.

example: The success of the outdoor wedding will depend on the weather tomorrow.
simple: You can always depend on her for help.

example: We took the wrong turn and ended up in a completely different town.
pattern: S + end up + V-ing/介系詞片語
simple: He ended up becoming a famous artist.

chinese: 跌倒 / 倒塌 / 失敗
example: The little boy fell down and scraped his knee while playing in the yard.
simple: The old building finally fell down during the storm.

example: The two best friends fell out over a silly argument and haven't spoken since.
simple: Her hair started to fall out due to stress.

chinese: 弄清楚 / 想出 / 計算
example: It took the engineers a long time to figure out the cause of the technical failure.
simple: Can you figure out how to open this?

example: You need to fill out the application form completely and sign your name at the bottom.
simple: She filled in her name and address.

chinese: 發現 / 查出 / 得知
example: We need to find out what time the last train leaves tonight.
simple: I found out that he was lying.

chinese: 相處融洽 / 進展 / 過活
example: I get along very well with my new classmates and we often study together.
simple: How are you getting along with your project?

chinese: 逃離 / 離開 / 度假
example: The thief managed to get away from the police after a long chase through the city.
simple: We need to get away for a few days.

example: She was very happy to finally get her borrowed camera back from her cousin.
simple: What time did you get back last night?

chinese: 進入 / 下車 / 上車 / 出去
example: Please make sure to get off the bus at the correct stop near the library.
pattern: S + get on/off + 大眾運輸
simple: Get in the car, we're leaving.

chinese: 克服 / 康復 / 忘懷
example: It took her several months to get over the flu and return to her normal routine.
simple: He is still trying to get over his breakup.

chinese: 通過 / 完成 / 接通電話
example: I have so much work to do that I don't think I can get through it all by Friday.
simple: I couldn't get through to him because the line was busy.

example: He usually gets up at six o'clock every morning to go for a run before work.
simple: Get up and give the lady your seat.

example: The charity asked people to give away their old clothes to help the homeless.
simple: She accidentally gave away the surprise party.

example: The company encourages its employees to give back to the community through volunteer work.
simple: Please give back the pen when you're finished.

chinese: 屈服 / 讓步 / 繳交
example: After hours of negotiation, the management finally gave in to the workers' demands.
simple: The government refused to give in to the terrorists.

chinese: 分發 / 用盡 / (機器)停止運作
example: After walking for ten hours without a break, her legs finally gave out.
simple: They were giving out free samples at the mall.

example: He decided to give up smoking for the sake of his long-term health.
simple: Never give up on your dreams.

example: The city council has given the project the green light to go ahead.
simple: Go ahead, I'm listening.

example: If the headache doesn't go away soon, you should probably see a doctor.
simple: I told him to go away.

example: I need to go back to the office because I forgot my house keys on the desk.
pattern: S + go back to + 地方
simple: Our friendship goes back to high school.

example: The show must go on regardless of the technical difficulties we are facing.
simple: Tell me what's going on.

chinese: 出去 / 熄滅 / 過時
example: The lights suddenly went out during the storm, leaving us in total darkness.
simple: The fire went out because there was no wood.

example: When I grow up, I want to be a professional photographer and travel the world.
pattern: S + grow up in + 地方
simple: Stop acting like a child and grow up!

example: The teacher reminded the students to hand in their final reports by the end of the day.
simple: He handed in his resignation letter.

example: The volunteers spent the afternoon handing out fliers about the upcoming charity event.
simple: The teacher handed out the test papers.

example: Teens often love to hang out at the mall with their friends on Saturday afternoons.

chinese: 力求 / 喜歡 / 選取

chinese: 經歷 / 檢查 / 耗盡 / 通過

chinese: 傳遞 / 轉交 / 去世

example: The company decided to pour more resources into its research and development department.

example: The students are working very hard to prepare for the upcoming national entrance exam.

example: The chairman will preside over the annual meeting and announce the new board members.

example: The workers' union continues to press for higher wages and better working conditions.

example: The new laws are designed to prevent companies from polluting the local rivers.

chinese: 吸引 / 停靠 / 賺取

chinese: 撤出 / 拔出 / 駛出

chinese: 表達清楚 / 解釋明白

chinese: 放下 / 寫下 / 鎮壓 / 安樂死
