chinese: 有資格參加 / 符合條件
example: You need to have at least three years of work experience to qualify for this scholarship.
simple: She didn't qualify for the final race.

example: It's a waste of time to quarrel with people who are not willing to listen to logic.
simple: Don't quarrel with your siblings.

example: Hundreds of fans queued up for hours to buy tickets for the famous singer's concert.
simple: People queued up to get into the store.

example: The teacher asked the excited children to quiet down so she could start the story.
simple: Wait for the crowd to quiet down.

example: The rescue team is racing against time to find the survivors after the earthquake.
simple: He will race against the champion.

example: The police officer radioed in the suspect's license plate number to headquarters.
simple: The pilot radioed in a Mayday signal.

chinese: 憤怒指責 / 強烈抨擊
example: The activists railed against the government's decision to cut funding for education.
simple: He railed against the unfair treatment.

example: Ash and smoke rained down on the city after the massive volcanic eruption.
simple: Gifts rained down from the sky.

chinese: 團結在...周圍 / 齊心支持
example: The local community rallied around the family after they lost their home in a fire.
simple: The team rallied around their injured captain.

example: The prices of the rooms in this hotel range from fifty to two hundred dollars per night.
pattern: N + range from A to B
simple: Temperatures range from 10 to 30 degrees.

example: This scientist ranks among the most influential researchers in the field of biology.
simple: He ranks among the greatest writers.

example: Most critics rate this movie as one of the best films of the decade.
simple: The hotel is rated as five-star.

example: During the long drought, the government had to ration out the water supply to the citizens.
simple: The food was rationed out among the soldiers.

example: Feel free to reach out to me if you have any questions about the project.
simple: The charity reaches out to homeless people.

example: I read about the new archaeological discovery in the science magazine yesterday.
simple: I read about the accident in the news.

example: The teacher read off the names of the winners to the entire class.
simple: He read off the numbers one by one.

chinese: 細讀 / 從頭到尾看一遍
example: You should read over your essay at least once to check for spelling errors.
simple: I need to read over the contract before signing.

chinese: 研讀資料 / 收集資訊
example: I need to read up on the history of Japan before I travel there next month.
simple: She spent the night reading up on the topic.

example: It's hard to reason with someone who is currently in a very angry state.
pattern: S + try to reason with + 人
simple: They tried to reason with the protesters.

example: The teenagers began to rebel against the strict rules of their boarding school.
simple: He rebelled against his parents' expectations.

chinese: 處理 / 考慮 / 面對
example: If we ignore this problem now, we will have to reckon with it later.
pattern: S + have to reckon with + N
simple: The company is a force to be reckoned with.

example: After months of not speaking, she finally decided to reconcile with her sister.
simple: It's hard to reconcile his words with his actions.

example: It took the economy several years to fully recover from the impact of the pandemic.
simple: He is still recovering from the surgery.

example: You should refer to the instruction manual if you are not sure how to use the machine.
simple: The teacher referred to the graph on the board.

example: At the end of the year, it is helpful to reflect on what you have achieved and what you can improve.
simple: He reflected on his past mistakes.

example: Please refrain from smoking in the hospital building for the comfort of other patients.
simple: They refrained from making comments.

example: Many teenagers find it difficult to relate to their parents because of the generation gap.
simple: The study relates to student behavior.

chinese: 對...放鬆心情 / 不必擔心
example: You should try to relax about the upcoming exam; you've already studied very hard.
simple: She's much more relaxed about her work now.

example: The athlete was released from his contract after a series of poor performances.
simple: He was released from hospital this morning.

example: Several guests remarked on the beautiful decorations at the wedding reception.
simple: Critics remarked on the actor's performance.

example: Please remember to lock the door and turn off the lights before you leave.
simple: I must remember to buy some milk.

example: This old song always reminds me of the happy summers I spent at the beach.
simple: You remind me of your father.

example: You should remove the old batteries from the flashlight to prevent leakage.
simple: He was removed from his post.

example: They decided to rent out their spare room to a university student for extra income.
simple: Are you going to rent out the house?

example: After the long meeting, the board members repaired to a local restaurant for dinner.
simple: The guests repaired to the garden.

example: The English teacher asked the students to repeat each sentence after her.
simple: Repeat after me: 'Hello'.

example: We need to replace the old wooden windows with modern aluminum ones.
simple: The factory replaced workers with robots.

example: I haven't had time to reply to your email yet, but I will do it tonight.
simple: Did she reply to your letter?

example: New employees should report to the human resources office on their first day.
simple: You will report directly to the manager.

example: The union represented its concerns to the management regarding safety issues.
simple: He represented the facts to the committee.

example: You can request an application form from the information desk in the lobby.
pattern: S + request + N + from + 人/單位
simple: They requested assistance from the police.

example: High concentration is required of students when they are performing experiments.
simple: This job requires a lot of patience of you.

example: The firefighters managed to rescue the cat from the burning building.
pattern: S + rescue + 人/物 + from + 來源
simple: The crew was rescued from the sinking ship.

type: 動詞 / 名詞 + 介系詞
example: The university is conducting detailed research into the causes of climate change.
simple: She is researching into cancer treatments.

example: He finally resigned himself to the fact that he wouldn't get the promotion this time.
simple: She resigned herself to a quiet life.

example: The blurry image slowly resolved into the shape of a person as we got closer.
simple: The argument resolved into a friendly discussion.

chinese: 訴諸於 / 採取 (最後手段)
example: He had to resort to borrowing money from his parents to pay off his debts.
simple: They resorted to violence to get what they wanted.

example: The patient is responding well to the new medical treatment.
simple: How did he respond to your suggestion?

chinese: 依賴 / 基於 / 停留在
example: The future of the company rests on the success of this innovative new product.
simple: All our hopes rest on you.

example: My father is planning to retire from his job as a high school teacher next year.
simple: She retired from professional tennis.

example: The stray cat eventually returned to the garden where it was first found.
simple: Life returned to normal after the war.

example: He finally revealed his true intentions to the rest of the team.
simple: The secret was revealed to the public.

example: The driver carefully reversed his car into the narrow parking space.
pattern: S + reverse + N + into + 地方
simple: He reversed into the garage.

example: After the short holiday, everything in the office reverted to its normal routine.
simple: The land reverted to the original owner.

chinese: 圍繞著...旋轉 / 以...為中心
example: In the past, many people believed that the sun revolved around the Earth.
simple: His life revolves around his children.

type: 動詞 / 名詞 + 介系詞
example: The company rewarded the employees for their hard work with a generous bonus.
simple: There is a reward for information about the missing dog.

example: We finally managed to get rid of the old furniture that was taking up too much space.
simple: How can I get rid of these ants?

example: The success of the entire project rides on the outcome of tomorrow's meeting.
simple: Much rides on your decision.

example: I'm busy right now; can I ring you back in ten minutes?
simple: He said he would ring back later.

example: She suddenly rang off before I could finish my sentence.
simple: I have to ring off now, someone is at the door.

example: The cashier rang up the items and told me the total cost.
simple: Ring me up when you get there.

example: Please rinse off the soap from your hands thoroughly before drying them.
simple: He rinsed the mud off his boots.

chinese: 洗乾淨 (容器內部) / 漂洗
example: After drinking the juice, make sure to rinse out the glass.
simple: Rinse out your mouth with water.

chinese: 敲竹槓 / 偷竊 / 撕掉
example: That tourist shop is known to rip off unsuspecting foreigners by overcharging them.
simple: I was ripped off by the taxi driver.

example: He was so angry that he ripped up the letter without even reading it.
simple: They ripped up the old contract.

chinese: 克服 / 超越 / 不受...影響
example: A true leader should be able to rise above personal conflicts for the good of the team.
simple: She rose above her humble beginnings.

example: The people decided to rise up against the oppressive dictator.
simple: The whole town rose up in protest.

type: 動詞 / 名詞 + 介系詞
example: He was willing to risk his life for the safety of his family.
simple: I wouldn't risk my reputation for that.

example: The sudden illness robbed her of the chance to compete in the Olympics.
simple: The thief robbed him of his wallet.

example: By the time the weekend rolls around, I am usually exhausted from work.
simple: Monday rolled around again too quickly.

example: The government decided to roll back some of the recent tax increases.
simple: They want to roll back environmental regulations.

chinese: 滾滾而來 / 大量湧入
example: Donations began to roll in after the news reported the disaster.
simple: The money is really rolling in now.

example: The software company is planning to roll out the new version next month.
simple: The government rolled out the vaccination program.

chinese: 翻身 / 延期 (貸款/點數)
example: I was so tired that I just rolled over and went back to sleep when the alarm rang.
simple: The unused minutes roll over to the next month.

example: Even though I'm not a big sports fan, I always root for our national team.
simple: Who are you rooting for in the final?

example: The management is determined to root out corruption within the company.
simple: We must root out the cause of the problem.

example: I managed to rope in a few friends to help me move house during the weekend.
simple: He roped me into helping with the project.

example: The abandoned wooden boat was left to rot away on the deserted beach.
simple: The leaves were left to rot away.

example: I'll rough out a basic plan now and we can discuss the details later.
simple: The architect roughed out the design on a napkin.

example: The gang tried to rough up the store owner to get him to pay protection money.
simple: He got roughed up in a street fight.

chinese: 圓滿結束 / 四捨五入
example: We decided to round off the evening with a delicious dessert at a local cafe.
pattern: S + round off + N + with + N
simple: Please round off the number to the nearest decimal.

chinese: 圍捕 / 召集 / 四捨五入 (進位)
example: The police managed to round up all the suspects within forty-eight hours.
simple: The cowboys rounded up the cattle.

chinese: 塗抹 / 觸及痛處 (反覆強調錯誤)
example: I know I made a mistake, so please don't rub it in!
simple: He kept rubbing in the fact that he won.

example: You can rub out any pencil marks with a good eraser.
simple: The name had been rubbed out.

example: The police have ruled out foul play in the investigation of the sudden death.
simple: Don't rule out the possibility of a mistake.

chinese: 偶然遇見 / 跑著穿過
example: I ran across an old friend from university while I was traveling in London.
simple: The boy ran across the street.

example: The small dog was running after the squirrel in the backyard.
simple: He had to run after the bus.

example: She is planning to run against the current mayor in the upcoming election.
simple: Luck was running against him.

example: Run along now, kids; your parents are waiting for you in the car.
simple: I must be running along now.

example: The kitten was so frightened by the loud noise that it ran away and hid under the bed.
simple: Don't run away from your problems.

chinese: 撞倒 / 耗盡 (電力) / 貶低 / 搜索
example: The battery in my laptop is running down; I need to find a charger.
simple: He's always running his boss down.

example: Many citizens are encouraging him to run for president in the next election.
simple: Run for your lives!

chinese: 順路進入 / 逮捕 / 磨合 (引擎)
example: I need to run in to the grocery store for a few minutes to buy some milk.
pattern: S + run in to + 地方
simple: You should run in the new engine carefully.

chinese: 跑掉 / 複印 / 流走
example: Could you please run off twenty copies of this handout for the class?
simple: The thief ran off when he saw the police.

chinese: 以...為動力 / 繼續下去
example: This new type of car runs on electricity instead of gasoline.
simple: The meeting ran on for three hours.

chinese: 撞到並碾過 / 超出 (時間) / 溢出
example: I'm sorry I'm late; the previous meeting ran over by fifteen minutes.
pattern: S + run over + N / S + run over
simple: The bus ran over a bicycle.

chinese: 快速過一遍 / 貫穿 / 耗盡
example: Let's run through the main points of the presentation one more time before the meeting.
simple: A sense of fear ran through the crowd.

chinese: 積欠 (債務) / 迅速跑向
example: He ran up a huge credit card debt while he was traveling around the world.
pattern: S + run up + N (債務/帳單)
simple: The child ran up to her mother.

example: You shouldn't rush into a major decision without thinking about it carefully first.
simple: They rushed into marriage after only a month.

chinese: 草率完成 / 快速通過
example: Don't rush through your homework; it's important to do it correctly and neatly.
simple: The bill was rushed through parliament.

example: We need to install security software to safeguard against hackers and viruses.
simple: The policy safeguards against future losses.

chinese: 輕快地通過 / 順利完成
example: She had studied so hard that she managed to sail through the difficult entrance exam.
simple: He sailed through the interview.

chinese: 為...儲蓄 / 除...之外 (介系詞)
example: He has been saving for a new car for two years and finally has enough money.
simple: The room was empty save for an old chair.

example: You can save on electricity costs by turning off the lights when you leave the room.
simple: Shopping online can help you save on fuel.

example: The young couple is saving up to buy their first apartment in the city.
pattern: S + save up (for + N / to + V)
simple: I'm saving up for a holiday.

example: The company had to scale down its operations due to the unexpected economic downturn.
simple: They scaled down the project to save costs.

example: The loud noise from the construction site scared away the birds in the garden.
simple: Rising prices might scare off potential buyers.

chinese: 勉強維持生活 / 勉強糊口
example: Even though his salary is very low, he manages to scrape by through careful spending.
pattern: S + manage to scrape by (on + N)
simple: We can just about scrape by on my earnings.

example: He didn't study much for the exam, but he managed to scrape through with a passing grade.
simple: She just managed to scrape through the interview.

example: I really screwed up the presentation by forgetting the most important data.
simple: Don't screw it up this time.

example: The police sealed off the entire street to investigate the scene of the accident.
simple: The building was sealed off after the gas leak.

example: Rescue teams are searching for survivors after the massive earthquake in the region.
simple: He is searching for a new job.

chinese: 處理 / 安排 / 考慮
example: I'll see about getting us some tickets for the concert next week.
simple: You should see about fixing that leak.

example: My parents came to the airport to see me off before I left for college in the US.
simple: They saw him off at the station.

chinese: 識破 / 看穿 / 貫徹始終
example: I could easily see through his lies because he looked very nervous.
simple: We must see the project through to the end.

chinese: 處理 / 照料 / 確保
example: Don't worry about the travel arrangements; my secretary will see to everything.
simple: Could you see to the leaking faucet?

chinese: 找出 / 尋找 (特別指費力尋找)
example: Investors are always seeking out new opportunities in the rapidly changing market.
simple: She sought out her old friend in the crowd.

chinese: 利用 / 抓住 (主意、機會)
example: The media seized on the scandal to increase their viewership during the slow news week.
simple: He seized on the chance to prove his worth.

example: The government decided to sell off some state-owned companies to reduce the national debt.
simple: They sold off the old equipment.

example: All the tickets for the famous singer's concert were sold out within ten minutes.
simple: He was accused of selling out his friends.

example: The noisy children were sent away so the adults could discuss the important matter.
simple: I sent away for a new catalog.

example: I had to send the steak back because it was overcooked and dry.
simple: If you don't like the color, you can send it back.

example: The teacher decided to send for the student's parents after his repeated bad behavior.
simple: They sent for a doctor immediately.

chinese: 呈報 / 寄送 (作品、申請)
example: You must send in your application form before the deadline if you want the job.
simple: The army was sent in to keep order.

example: I'll send off these letters to the post office as soon as I finish writing them.
simple: The player was sent off for a bad foul.

example: It is important to separate your personal life from your professional work.
simple: The island is separated from the mainland by a channel.

example: This old building used to serve as a warehouse, but now it's a modern art gallery.
simple: He served as the company's CEO for ten years.

example: The chef is ready to serve up the main course to the hungry guests.
simple: The media served up a lot of rumors.

example: After the storm, the residents set about cleaning up the fallen branches from the streets.
simple: We set about our task with enthusiasm.

example: The long-standing argument over the inheritance set the two brothers against each other.
simple: She set her savings against the cost of the car.

example: Her unique singing voice really sets her apart from all the other performers.
simple: We set apart some money for our holiday.

chinese: 撥出 (時間金錢) / 擱置 (爭論)
example: You should set aside at least thirty minutes every day to practice your English.
simple: Let's set aside our differences and cooperate.

chinese: 延誤 / 阻礙 / 花費
example: The heavy rains have set back the construction project by several weeks.
simple: The new car set him back thirty thousand dollars.

chinese: 記下 / 放下 / 規定
example: The company rules are clearly set down in the employee handbook.
simple: He set down his bag and rested.

example: The scientist set forth his new theory in a detailed article in the journal.
simple: They set out early in the morning.

chinese: (不快之事) 開始並可能持續
example: We should head back home before the winter darkness sets in.
simple: Despair began to set in after the failure.

example: The farmer was afraid that wolves might set on his sheep during the night.
simple: He was set on by a gang of youths.

example: If we all set to, we can finish painting the room before the sun goes down.
simple: They set to with enthusiasm.

example: He wanted a high salary, but he eventually had to settle for a lower offer.
simple: Never settle for second best.

chinese: 安頓下來 / 適應新環境
example: It took the exchange student a few weeks to settle in and make new friends.
simple: How are you settling in to your new job?

example: After looking at several houses, they finally settled on a small apartment near the park.
simple: We eventually settled on a date for the wedding.

example: If you pay for the taxi now, we can settle up when we get to the hotel.
simple: Let's settle up before we leave.

chinese: 縫補 / 成功完成 / 確保成功
example: She managed to sew up the hole in her favorite sweater so it looked like new.
simple: They've pretty much sewn up the deal.

example: It took me a long time to shake off the flu and return to work.
simple: He couldn't shake off the feeling that something was wrong.

example: The new manager plans to shake up the entire department to improve efficiency.
simple: The news of the accident really shook him up.

chinese: 表現進步 / 改善行為 / 發展
example: The coach told the player that he needs to shape up or he will be dropped from the team.
simple: Things are shaping up nicely.

example: The employees were happy to share in the company's profits at the end of the year.
simple: We all shared in her happiness.

example: You should learn to share your toys with other children at the park.
simple: I shared a room with my brother.

example: Even though he was nervous, his natural talent for acting really shone through.
simple: Her kindness shines through everything she does.

example: The soldiers were ordered not to shoot at the protesters during the demonstration.
simple: Someone shot at the moving car.

example: The manager quickly shot down my proposal because it was too expensive.
simple: The enemy plane was shot down.

example: A lizard's tongue can shoot out very fast to catch a fly.
simple: Flames shot out from the burning building.

chinese: 急速上升 / 猛長 (身高)
example: The price of vegetables has shot up recently due to the heavy rain.
simple: The boy has shot up since I last saw him.

example: You should shop around before buying a new computer to find the best deal.
simple: I'm shopping around for a new car insurance.

example: The government took measures to shore up the economy during the crisis.
simple: They needed to shore up the old walls.

example: My colleague offered to show me around the office on my first day of work.
pattern: S + show + 人 + around (+ 地方)
simple: We'll show you around the city.

example: The secretary will show the next candidate in for the interview.
simple: Please show the guests in.

example: He likes to show off his new sports car to all of his friends.
simple: Stop showing off!

chinese: 送客 / 帶領某人出去
example: After the meeting ended, I showed the visitors out to the elevator.
simple: The host showed us out after dinner.

example: His true feelings began to show through his cold appearance.
simple: The stain is showing through the paint.

example: He spent most of his life shut away in a remote mountain cabin.
simple: She shut herself away to finish the novel.

chinese: 關閉 / 停業 / 關機
example: The factory was forced to shut down after failing several safety inspections.
simple: Remember to shut down your computer.

example: The valley was shut in by high, snow-capped mountains on all sides.
simple: He felt shut in by the narrow room.

chinese: 切斷 / 關掉 (水、電、瓦斯)
example: Please make sure to shut off the gas before you leave the house.
simple: The water supply was shut off this morning.

chinese: 排除 / 擋住 / 把...拒之門外
example: These heavy curtains are designed to shut out the sunlight completely.
simple: She tried to shut out the painful memories.

example: He is a very brave person who never shies away from a difficult challenge.
simple: Some people shy away from commitment.

example: Most of the family sided against him when he made the controversial decision.
simple: They sided against the proposal.

example: In the argument, my father usually sides with my mother to avoid conflict.
simple: Which side are you siding with?

example: He foolishly signed away his rights to the invention without realizing its value.
simple: She signed away her inheritance.

example: I had to sign for the package when it arrived at my office this morning.
simple: The neighbor signed for my delivery.

example: All visitors are required to sign in at the front desk before entering the building.
simple: Don't forget to sign out before you leave.

chinese: 結束工作 / 簽署認可 / (廣播)結束
example: The radio host always signs off with the same catchphrase every night.
simple: The manager finally signed off on the budget.

chinese: 簽署雇用合約 / 註冊登入
example: The famous actor finally agreed to sign on for the sequel to the superhero movie.
simple: He signed on as a volunteer.

example: He decided to sign over his property to his children before he retired.
pattern: S + sign over + N + to + 人
simple: She signed over the rights to her book.

example: Many students signed up for the advanced coding workshop held by the university.
simple: I signed up for a yoga class.

example: The teacher singled out her essay as an excellent example for the whole class.
simple: He was singled out for criticism.

example: It took a few minutes for the shocking news to sink in.
simple: The gravity of the situation began to sink in.

chinese: 袖手旁觀 / 放鬆坐著
example: You can't just sit back and watch while everyone else is working hard.
simple: Sit back and enjoy the show.

example: The professor allowed the visitor to sit in on his lecture for one afternoon.
simple: I sat in on the management meeting.

chinese: 袖手旁觀 / 不參加 (直到結束)
example: Because of his ankle injury, he had to sit out the rest of the soccer match.
simple: I'll sit this dance out.

chinese: 坐著直到...結束 (常用於無聊的事)
example: It was difficult to sit through the three-hour presentation without falling asleep.
simple: We sat through two hours of boring speeches.

chinese: 評估 / 打量 (人或情況)
example: The boxer tried to size up his opponent's strength during the first round.
simple: He sized up the situation quickly.

example: Before the actual meeting, the manager sketched out the main points of the proposal.
simple: She sketched out a plan for the house.

example: I only had time to skim through the report before the interview started.
simple: He skimmed through the newspaper.

example: On Saturday mornings, I like to sleep in until ten o'clock.
simple: It's a holiday, so let's sleep in.

chinese: 透過睡眠消除 (頭痛、酒意)
example: If you have a headache, you should try to sleep it off instead of taking medicine.
simple: He's upstairs sleeping off a hangover.

example: That's a major decision; why don't you sleep on it and give me your answer tomorrow?
simple: I need to sleep on the job offer.

example: My daughter asked if her best friend could sleep over this Friday night.
simple: The kids are sleeping over at their grandma's.

example: The company slipped up by failing to check the quality of the raw materials.
simple: Someone slipped up and sent the wrong email.

example: You need to slow down when you are driving through a residential area.
simple: Slow down! You're going too fast.

chinese: 平息 (爭論) / 掩飾 (問題)
example: The manager tried to smooth over the disagreement between the two employees.
simple: They smoothed over the problem with a joke.

chinese: 從...中振作起來 (憂鬱、發呆)
example: You need to snap out of your depression and start thinking about the future.
simple: Snap out of it!

example: The best concert tickets were snapped up by fans within minutes of going on sale.
simple: Bargains were snapped up quickly.

chinese: 嗅出 / 發現 (秘密、隱藏物)
example: Specially trained dogs can sniff out illegal drugs hidden in luggage at the airport.
simple: The detective sniffed out a scandal.

example: I need some time to sort out the problems with my computer before I can work.
simple: Let's sort out these documents.

chinese: 探聽 (某人的) 口風 / 試探想法
example: I'll sound out my boss about the new project before I make a formal proposal.
simple: She sounded him out about the job offer.

chinese: 發呆 / 心不在焉 / 隔開
example: I'm sorry, I missed what you said because I was spacing out for a moment.
simple: The desks were spaced out evenly.

chinese: 引發 / 導致 (突然發生)
example: The new tax policy sparked off protests across the entire country.
simple: The incident sparked off a major row.

chinese: 代表...發言 / 表達願望
example: The lawyer will speak for his client during the court hearing tomorrow.
simple: Can you speak for the team?

chinese: 公開發表意見 / 敢於發言
example: It is important to speak out against injustice whenever you see it.
simple: She spoke out in favor of the new law.

chinese: 大聲點說 / 大膽表達
example: Could you please speak up? I can't hear you clearly from the back of the room.
simple: Speak up if you have any questions.

example: The car speeded up as it entered the highway.
simple: We need to speed up the process.

example: The instructions are very clear, so I don't need to spell out every single step for you.
simple: Let me spell out the terms of the contract.

example: Many families spend a large portion of their income on housing and transportation.
simple: She spent fifty dollars on a new book.

chinese: 分手 / 分開 / 分配
example: The team members decided to split up to search the entire area more quickly.
simple: They split up the profit among themselves.

example: The hikers spread out across the field to look for the lost trail.
simple: The city spreads out over a large area.

example: New coffee shops are springing up all over the city lately.
simple: Dandelions sprang up in the garden.

example: The neighbor was caught trying to spy on us through the fence.
simple: He was accused of spying on the government.

chinese: 堆積 / 相比 / 衡量
example: I have so much work stacking up on my desk that I'll have to stay late tonight.
simple: How does the new car stack up against the old one?

chinese: 撲滅 / 根除 (犯罪、疾病)
example: The government is trying to stamp out corruption in the civil service.
simple: They worked hard to stamp out the fire.

chinese: 支持 (某人) / 待命 / 遵守 (承諾)
example: Her friends promised to stand by her no matter what happened during the crisis.
simple: Emergency services are standing by.

chinese: 代表 / 象徵 / 容忍
example: In mathematics, the letter 'x' often stands for an unknown quantity.
simple: I won't stand for such rudeness.

example: Could you please stand in for me at the meeting while I see the doctor?
simple: A substitute teacher stood in for our regular teacher.

example: Her bright red dress made her stand out in the crowded room.
simple: He stands out from the rest of the team.

chinese: 勇敢面對 (強大對手) / 耐用
example: The small boy had the courage to stand up to the school bully.
pattern: S + stand up to + 人/物
simple: The car stands up to long trips well.

example: It is rude to stare at people in public, even if they look different.
simple: She stared at the wall in silence.

example: The computer crashed, so I had to start over from the beginning of the report.
simple: Let's start over and try again.

example: Children were warned to stay away from the deep water in the lake.
simple: Stay away from me!

example: The students stayed up all night to prepare for the final exam.
simple: We stayed up to watch the game.

example: The man was arrested after trying to steal from a jewelry store downtown.
pattern: S + steal + N + from + 人/地方
simple: He stole money from my wallet.

example: The elderly manager decided to step aside and let a younger person take over.
simple: Please step aside to let the doctor pass.

example: The politician was forced to step down following the scandal.
simple: He stepped down as chairman.

chinese: 介入 / 干預 / 幫忙
example: The police had to step in to prevent the argument from becoming violent.
simple: When the leader left, her deputy stepped in.

chinese: 加強 / 提高 / 挺身而出
example: The government needs to step up security measures at all international airports.
simple: He stepped up to accept the challenge.

chinese: 堅持 (計畫、決定) / 黏在...
example: If you want to lose weight, you must stick to your diet and exercise plan.
simple: Stick to the facts.

chinese: 團結一致 / 黏在一起
example: Family members should always stick together during difficult times.
simple: If we stick together, we can win.

chinese: 繼續做 / 待在某人身邊
example: He decided to stick with his old car for another year to save money.
simple: Stick with me, and you'll be safe.

example: His controversial comments stirred up a lot of anger among the local residents.
simple: The wind stirred up the dust.

chinese: 順道拜訪 / 中途停留
example: I'll stop by the grocery store on my way home to buy some milk.
simple: We stopped over in London for two days.

chinese: 弄清楚 / 改過自新 / 弄直
example: It took the accountant a long time to straighten out the company's complicated finances.
simple: He needs to straighten his life out.

chinese: 感到壓力重重 / 使極度緊張
example: The upcoming final exams are really stressing out many students in the class.
simple: Working long hours stresses me out.

chinese: 開始 (談話、交誼) / (樂隊)演奏
example: He is a very friendly person who can strike up a conversation with anyone.
simple: The band struck up a lively tune.

chinese: 欺騙某人 / 拖延 (給假希望)
example: She realized he was just stringing her along and had no intention of marrying her.
simple: Don't string him along if you're not interested.

chinese: 脫掉 (全部) 衣服 / 剝除
example: After the long hike, he stripped off his muddy clothes and took a hot shower.
simple: The workers stripped the old paint off the walls.

chinese: 與...奮鬥 / 在...方面有困難
example: Many international students struggle with the local dialect when they first arrive.
simple: She is struggling with her math homework.

example: While researching at the library, I stumbled across a rare book about local history.
simple: We stumbled upon a hidden beach.

chinese: 使遭受 / 取決於 / 易受...的
type: 片語動詞 / 形容詞片語
example: The new airport expansion plan is still subject to government approval.
simple: Flights are subject to delay due to weather.

chinese: 服從 / 屈服於 / 提交給
example: The rebels eventually had to submit to the power of the government forces.
simple: You must submit your report to the manager.

example: I decided to subscribe to the online magazine to stay updated on technology news.
simple: Do you subscribe to his theory?

example: You can substitute honey for sugar if you want a healthier alternative.
simple: He substituted for the injured player.

example: Please subtract the cost of shipping from the total amount.
simple: The messy yard subtracts from the value of the house.

example: She finally succeeded in passing the difficult exam after three attempts.
simple: He succeeded in business.

example: Nobody likes him because he is always trying to suck up to the boss.
simple: Stop sucking up!

example: Many elderly people suffer from insomnia during the summer heat.
simple: The company is suffering from a lack of funds.

example: To sum up, the project was a huge success despite the early challenges.
simple: He summed up the meeting in two sentences.

chinese: 鼓起 (勇氣) / 喚起 (記憶)
example: She had to summon up all her courage to walk onto the stage.
simple: The smell summoned up memories of my childhood.

example: My mother swears by this traditional remedy for a sore throat.
simple: He swears by his old car.

example: The new president will be sworn in during a ceremony tomorrow.
simple: The jury was sworn in by the judge.

example: After that terrible hangover, he decided to swear off alcohol for good.
simple: I've sworn off sweets.

chinese: 關掉 / 打開 (電器、開關)
example: Please switch off the computer and the lights before leaving the office.
simple: He switched on the TV.

example: I was quite taken aback by his sudden decision to quit his job.
pattern: S + be taken aback by + N
simple: She was taken aback by his rudeness.

example: I don't know why she suddenly took against him; they used to be friends.
simple: The dog took against the new mailman.

example: The mechanic had to take the engine apart to find the problem.
simple: He took the watch apart to fix it.

example: The manager took me aside to discuss the private matter.
simple: She took her daughter aside to talk.

chinese: 拿走 / 帶走 / 外帶
example: Waiters usually take away the dirty dishes as soon as you finish eating.
simple: I'll have two burgers to take away, please.

example: I take back what I said earlier; I was wrong about the plan.
simple: You should take that dress back to the shop.

example: Please take down my phone number in case you need to contact me later.
simple: They took down the Christmas decorations.

example: I’m sorry, I took you for your brother; you look so similar!
simple: Don't take my kindness for weakness.

example: What can we take from this experience to improve our future performance?
pattern: S + take + N + from + 來源
simple: His rude behavior takes from his professional image.

chinese: 吸收 / 理解 / 欺騙 / 領養
example: It was a very complex lecture, and I couldn't take in all the information.
simple: She was taken in by his smooth talk.

chinese: 拿出 / 帶某人出去 (約會) / 扣除
example: He took her out to a fancy restaurant for her birthday dinner.
simple: Take out the trash before you leave.

example: All students are encouraged to take part in extracurricular activities.
simple: He took part in the marathon last year.

chinese: 開始喜歡 / 養成...的習慣
example: The new puppy took to its owners almost immediately.
simple: He has taken to drinking tea in the morning.

example: I’d like to take you up on your offer to help me move house.
pattern: S + take + 人 + up + on + N
simple: Can I take you up on that drink later?

example: We need to talk about the budget for the upcoming project.
simple: What are you talking about?

chinese: 說服 / 避開重點談論
example: He eventually managed to talk his parents around to letting him go on the trip.
simple: They spent an hour talking around the main issue.

chinese: 對 (某人) 嘮叨 / 單向說話 (不聽回應)
example: I felt like she was just talking at me rather than having a real conversation.
simple: Stop talking at me and listen for a change!

chinese: 以居高臨下的語氣說話
example: Don't talk down to me just because I'm younger than you.
simple: He always talks down to his employees.

chinese: 說服去 / 說服不要去
example: My friends talked me into joining the hiking club last weekend.
simple: She talked him out of quitting his job.

example: We need to talk over the plan with the manager before we make a decision.
simple: Let's talk it over tonight.

chinese: 詳盡解釋 / 討論出結果
example: The teacher talked us through the complicated math problem step by step.
simple: We spent hours talking through our problems.

example: I need to talk to you about the incident that happened yesterday.
simple: Can I talk to you for a second?

example: You don't want to tangle with that gang; they are very dangerous.
simple: He got tangled with the law.

chinese: 利用 / 開發 (資源、情感)
example: The company is trying to tap into the growing market for organic food.
simple: The movie taps into the fears of teenagers.

example: We decided to team up with another local business for the charity event.
simple: The two companies teamed up to create a new app.

chinese: 拆散 / 撕碎 / 使心碎
example: The war tore many families apart and caused immense suffering.
simple: The bad news really tore her apart.

example: The little boy began to tear at the wrapping paper on his birthday gift.
simple: The sight of the starving children tore at her heart.

example: The old library was torn down to make room for a modern shopping mall.
simple: He spent his life tearing down his rivals.

chinese: 猛烈攻擊 / 狼吞虎嚥
example: The angry customer began to tear into the manager about the poor service.
simple: The hungry dog tore into the meat.

example: He tore off a piece of paper to write down his phone number.
simple: He tore off his wet clothes.

chinese: 撕碎 / 毀掉 (協議) / 傷心落淚
example: She tore up the old letters and threw them into the trash.
simple: He started to tear up during the sad movie.

example: The twins look so much alike that I can't tell them apart.
pattern: S + cannot tell + A and B + apart
simple: Can you tell the two brands apart?

example: It's difficult to tell the real diamond from a high-quality fake.
pattern: S + can/cannot tell + A + from + B
simple: How can you tell a butterfly from a moth?

example: The ancient poems tell of a legendary king who saved his people from war.
simple: The report tells of the extreme conditions in the arctic.

example: The teacher told the student off for being late for the third time this week.
simple: My mom told me off for not cleaning my room.

example: Don't tell on me! I'll never do it again, I promise.
simple: She told on her brother for stealing cookies.

chinese: (肌肉) 繃緊 / 緊張起來
example: He always tenses up whenever he has to speak in front of a large audience.
simple: Her shoulders tensed up during the interview.

example: The doctors are testing the patient for a rare type of virus.
simple: The soil was tested for pollution.

chinese: 試驗 / 測試 (主意、產品)
example: We need to test out the new software on different devices before the official release.
simple: They are testing out a new theory.

example: I would like to thank you for your generous help during my time of need.
simple: We thanked the hosts for the lovely dinner.

chinese: 未雨綢繆 / 預作打算
example: To be successful in business, you need to think ahead and predict future trends.
simple: We didn't think ahead and forgot to book a hotel.

example: When I think back on my school days, I realize how much I have changed.
simple: Think back to the first time we met.

chinese: 想起 / 考慮 / 認為
example: I can't think of a better way to celebrate your graduation than a party.
simple: What do you think of his latest movie?

chinese: 認真考慮 / 想出 (完整計畫)
example: The whole strategy was carefully thought out by the marketing team.
simple: It was a well-thought-out plan.

example: You should think the consequences through before you make such a major decision.
simple: I need time to think it through.

example: He’s always thinking up clever excuses for why he hasn't finished his work.
simple: Who thought up this brilliant idea?

chinese: 討論出結果 / 徹底解決
example: The two sides met to thrash out a new trade agreement.
simple: They thrashed out their differences over a cup of coffee.

example: The mirror threw the light back onto the wall.
simple: The crisis threw the country back into poverty.

example: If you buy the car today, the dealer will throw in a free car wash for a year.
simple: He threw in a few extra details.

chinese: 擺脫 / 趕走 / 使困惑
example: It took her a week to throw off the cold and return to her normal routine.
simple: His sudden question threw me off.

example: She threw on her coat and ran out to meet her friends.
simple: I'll just throw on a sweater and I'll be ready.

chinese: 扔掉 / 驅逐 / 駁回 (法律)
example: I really need to throw out these old newspapers to clear some space.
simple: The judge threw the case out due to lack of evidence.

chinese: 臨時湊成 / 草草組成
example: She managed to throw together a quick meal for the unexpected guests.
simple: The report was thrown together at the last minute.

chinese: 嘔吐 / 拋起 / 產生 (問題)
example: The baby threw up all over the new carpet.
simple: The investigation threw up some interesting facts.

chinese: 縛住 / 限制 / 牽制
example: Having a full-time job and a young family really ties him down.
simple: The soldiers tied down the enemy forces.

example: His version of the story doesn't tie in with what the other witnesses said.
simple: This promotion ties in with the movie release.

chinese: 繫緊 / 佔用 (時間金錢) / 圓滿結束
example: I'm sorry, I'm tied up in a meeting and can't talk right now.
simple: He tied up his shoes and ran out.

example: The government decided to tighten up security at all international airports.
simple: The rules need to be tightened up.

example: The coach called a time out to discuss the strategy with his players.
simple: The system timed out before I could save my work.

example: She quickly tired of her new toy and wanted something else to play with.
simple: He never tires of talking about his success.

example: The long hike through the mountains completely tired us out.
simple: Walking all day tired me out.

example: The plane is expected to touch down at Taipei Songshan Airport at 4 p.m.
simple: We touched down ten minutes early.

example: In his speech, the principal touched on several important issues regarding school safety.
simple: The report only touched on the economic side of the problem.

chinese: 潤色 / 修補 (畫作、妝容)
example: She needed to touch up her makeup before she went onto the stage.
simple: The artist touched up the painting with a bit of gold.

example: Historians can trace the roots of this tradition back to the 16th century.
pattern: S + trace + N + back to + 來源
simple: Her family can be traced back to the royal house.

example: The detective spent months trying to track down the missing witness.
simple: I finally tracked down a copy of the old book.

example: I decided to trade in my old car for a newer, more fuel-efficient model.
pattern: S + trade in + A + for + B
simple: You can trade in your old phone at this shop.

example: In business, you often have to trade off quality for a lower price.
pattern: S + trade off + A + against + B
simple: There is always a trade-off between speed and accuracy.

example: The athletes have been training hard for the national competition for months.
simple: He is training for a marathon.

example: She decided to transfer to the Tokyo office to gain international experience.
pattern: S + transfer to + 地方/部門
simple: The patient was transferred to another hospital.

example: The old factory was transformed into a modern art gallery last year.
simple: Caterpillars transform into butterflies.

example: His best-selling novel has been translated into more than thirty languages.
simple: Hard work doesn't always translate into success.

example: They spent the entire summer traveling around Europe by train.
simple: We are planning to travel to Japan next month.

example: I’m sorry, I didn’t mean to tread on your toes in the crowded bus.
simple: Be careful not to tread on the flowers.

example: You should treat others as you would like to be treated yourself.
simple: They treated the incident as a joke.

example: He was tricked into signing the contract without reading it carefully.
simple: The cat tricked me into giving it a second breakfast.

chinese: 被...絆倒 / 犯錯 / 絆倒某人
example: I tripped over a loose wire and fell down the stairs.
simple: The clever question tripped him up during the interview.

example: You need to trust in your own abilities if you want to succeed in life.
simple: We trust in the power of education.

example: The manager is busy right now; could you try back in an hour?
simple: I'll try back tomorrow.

example: After years of training, he decided to try for a place on the national team.
simple: She is trying for a world record.

example: We are trying out a new teaching method to see if it improves student engagement.
simple: He tried out for the basketball team.

example: The small restaurant is tucked away in a quiet alley behind the main street.
pattern: N + be tucked away in + 地方
simple: She has some money tucked away for emergencies.

chinese: 塞進 / 把 (小孩) 安頓入睡 / 開心地大吃
example: Please tuck your shirt in before you go onto the stage.
simple: Dinner is served; tuck in everyone!

chinese: 收聽 / 轉台 / 不理會 / 心不在焉
example: Millions of people tuned in to watch the live broadcast of the final match.
simple: He just tuned out during the boring speech.

example: The whole town turned against him after the scandal was revealed.
simple: His own family turned against him.

example: The new manager managed to turn around the failing company within a year.
simple: Turn around and let me see your back.

example: Thousands of fans were turned away because the stadium was already full.
pattern: S + be turned away from + 地方
simple: She turned away so I couldn't see her tears.

example: The weather was so bad that the hikers decided to turn back before reaching the summit.
simple: The guards turned back the crowd.

chinese: 繳交 / 上床睡覺 / 投案
example: Please turn in your homework at the end of the class.
simple: I think it's time to turn in for the night.

example: In the fairy tale, the frog turned into a handsome prince after the princess kissed him.
simple: The rain turned into snow.

chinese: 結果是 / 產出 / 熄滅 (燈)
example: It turned out that the rumor was completely false.
pattern: It + turn(ed) out that + S + V
simple: The party turned out to be a great success.

example: He turned over the letter to the police as evidence.
simple: Turn over to page 50.

chinese: 轉向 / 求助於 / 翻到
example: She always turns to her mother for advice when she is in trouble.
pattern: S + turn to + 人 + for + N
simple: The caterpillar turns into a butterfly.

example: Please type in your password carefully to access your account.
simple: I typed in the address into the search bar.

example: We’ve used up all the milk, so I’ll have to go to the grocery store.
simple: Playing games uses up a lot of battery.

example: The invention of the internet ushered in a new era of information technology.
simple: Spring ushers in warmer weather.

chinese: 為...擔保 / 擔保某人的人格
example: I can vouch for his honesty; I have known him for over ten years.
simple: Who can vouch for her story?

example: The waiter was busy waiting on a large table of customers.
simple: She is tired of waiting on her lazy brother.

chinese: 等候直到 (某事) 結束
example: We decided to stay in the cafe and wait out the storm.
simple: The soldiers waited out the winter in the mountains.

chinese: 等候 (某人) 回家而不睡覺
example: Don't wait up for me; I'll be working late tonight at the office.
simple: My parents always wait up for me.

chinese: 走開 / 擺脫 / (在事故中)毫髮無傷
example: He was lucky to walk away from the serious car accident without a single scratch.
simple: You can't just walk away from your responsibilities.

example: I'm sorry, I didn't mean to walk in on your private meeting.
simple: He walked in on his sister while she was studying.

chinese: 走進 / 無意中陷入 (困境)
example: We unknowingly walked into a trap set by our competitors.
simple: She walked into a job at the bank.

chinese: 走掉 / 透過散步消除 (情緒/體重)
example: He was so angry that he just walked off without saying a word.
simple: I need to walk off this big lunch.

chinese: 走出 / 罷工 / 遺棄 (某人)
example: The workers decided to walk out to protest against the poor working conditions.
simple: He walked out on his family ten years ago.

example: The little boy wandered off while his mother was talking on the phone.
simple: Don't wander off the point during the presentation.

chinese: 熱身 / (機器)預熱 / 變暖和
example: It's important to warm up before exercising to prevent muscle injuries.
simple: The party really warmed up after he arrived.

example: Doctors warn against eating too much processed food to maintain long-term health.
simple: The teacher warned us against cheating.

example: The weather forecast warned of heavy rain and possible flooding in low-lying areas.
simple: Scientists warned of the dangers of climate change.

example: The bridge was washed away by the powerful currents during the flood.
simple: The rain washed away the footprints.

chinese: 沖洗 (表面) / (吃藥時)順道喝水
example: He used a glass of orange juice to wash down the bitter medicine.
pattern: S + wash + N + down + with + 飲料
simple: The rain washed down the dusty streets.

example: You should wash off the mud from your boots before entering the house.
simple: This ink won't wash off easily.

chinese: 洗淨內部 / (雨天)使活動取消
example: The outdoor concert was washed out by a sudden thunderstorm.
simple: He washed out the dirty bucket.

chinese: 消瘦 / 虛弱 / 荒廢
example: The patient slowly wasted away because of the long illness.
simple: Don't waste your life away in front of the TV.

chinese: 等待 / 尋找 / 留意
example: Drivers must watch for cyclists when turning at the corner.
simple: The sailors watched for land.

example: The mother bird stayed near the nest to watch over her eggs.
simple: Angels watch over you.

chinese: 稀釋 / 削減 (強度/內容)
example: The politician decided to water down his proposal to get more support.
simple: The juice was watered down with too much ice.

example: The manager waved aside my concerns, saying there was nothing to worry about.
simple: She waved aside his apologies.

example: We managed to wave down a passing taxi near the station.
simple: The hitchhiker waved down the truck.

example: The steps of the ancient temple have been worn away by millions of visitors.
simple: The cliffs are being worn away by the sea.

chinese: 磨掉 / 使疲憊 / 使妥協
example: They eventually wore down the defense through constant pressure.
simple: The long argument wore him down.

example: The painkiller started to wear off after about four hours.
simple: The novelty of the new toy soon wore off.

chinese: 壓低 / 使沉重 / 使憂心忡忡
example: She was weighed down by heavy bags as she walked home from the store.
pattern: S + be weighed down by + N
simple: Responsibility weighed him down.

example: The guilt of keeping the secret began to weigh on his mind.
simple: Current problems are weighing on her.

example: You should carefully weigh up the pros and cons before making a decision.
simple: He's weighing up his chances of winning.

chinese: 歡迎來到 / 隨意使用
example: You are welcome to borrow my notes whenever you miss a class.
simple: Welcome to our home!

chinese: 迅速取出 / 匆忙做出 / 激起 (情感)
example: She managed to whip up a delicious meal in just thirty minutes.
simple: He whipped out his credit card to pay.

chinese: 放鬆 / 逐漸結束 (營運)
example: I like to listen to soft music to help me wind down after a long day at work.
simple: The company is winding down its overseas operations.

chinese: 結束 / 最終落得 / 惹惱 (某人)
example: They wound up the meeting around six o'clock as everyone was tired.
pattern: S + wind up + V-ing/介系詞片語
simple: If you keep stealing, you'll wind up in jail.

example: The little boy wiped away his tears and started to smile again.
simple: Wipe that smile off your face!

chinese: 徹底摧毀 / 消滅 / 使精疲力竭
example: The earthquake wiped out several small villages near the epicenter.
simple: The flu wiped me out for a week.

example: He didn't have much, but he couldn't wish for a better family.
simple: She closed her eyes and wished for a miracle.

example: The engineers had to find a way to work around the software bug.
simple: We can work around your schedule.

chinese: 透過體力消耗 (熱量/壓力/債務)
example: He went to the gym to work off some of the energy he had accumulated all day.
simple: I need to work off this huge pizza.

example: The scientists are currently working on a new treatment for cancer.
simple: He is working on his parents to let him go.

chinese: 克服 / 處理 (複雜問題)
example: The couple is going to therapy to work through their relationship problems.
simple: We need to work through this pile of paperwork.

example: The two countries are working toward a peaceful resolution to the conflict.
simple: I am working toward my degree.

chinese: 激發 (食慾/勇氣) / 逐漸發展
example: The long walk in the fresh air really worked up my appetite.
simple: She finally worked up the courage to say no.

example: Don't worry about the small mistakes; focus on the overall quality of your work.

simple: They divvied up the prize money.

chinese: 扣上 / 翻修 / 包裝
example: The old cottage was beautifully done up and sold at a very high price.
simple: Do your coat up; it's cold outside.

example: I am exhausted; I could really do with a long holiday right now.
simple: The incident had nothing to do with me.

example: She spent three hours dolling herself up for the party tonight.
simple: Everyone was dolled up for the gala.

example: I was so tired during the lecture that I kept dozing off.
simple: The cat dozed off in the sun.

example: Please don't drag me into your argument with your brother.
simple: The cat dragged a mouse in.

example: The meeting dragged on for three hours, and everyone became very bored.
simple: The court case dragged on for years.

example: There's no need to drag out the discussion any longer; we have made our decision.
simple: They dragged out the ceremony with long speeches.

chinese: 流失 / 漸漸消失 / 排乾
example: He felt all the energy drain away from his body after the long marathon.
simple: Drain off any excess water before serving.

chinese: 退縮 / 後退 / 取回
example: She drew back in fear when she saw the large spider on the wall.
simple: The government drew back from its earlier decision.

example: The writer drew on her childhood experiences to write the award-winning novel.
simple: He drew on his savings to pay for the car.

chinese: 拉長 (時間) / 使開口說話
example: The teacher tried to draw out the shy student during the class discussion.
simple: The days are drawing out now that it is spring.

example: Since she was a child, she has always dreamed of becoming a professional dancer.
simple: I dreamed about you last night.

example: The inventor dreamed up a new way to clean the ocean using robots.
simple: I don't know who dreamed up this plan.

example: The reporter managed to dredge up some old scandals from the politician's past.
simple: They are dredging up mud from the river.

example: We were drenched in sweat after playing basketball for two hours in the sun.
simple: The field was drenched in moonlight.

example: On Fridays, many companies allow their employees to dress down.
simple: The boss gave him a real dressing-down.

chinese: 反覆灌輸 / 鑽孔進入
example: Our coach drilled the importance of teamwork into us every single day.
simple: The carpenter drilled a hole into the wood.

example: They stood on the mountain peak, drinking in the breathtaking view of the valley.
simple: The tourists drank in the atmosphere of the market.

example: Drink up your orange juice so we can leave for school.
simple: Drink up! There's plenty more.

example: I’m not quite sure what you are driving at with those strange comments.
simple: I don't understand what he is driving at.

example: The loud noise of the alarm drove away the burglars.
simple: The soldiers managed to drive back the enemy.

example: The professor droned on for over an hour, and most of the students fell asleep.
simple: He droned on about his old car.

example: All the fashion fans were drooling over the new designer collection.
simple: He spent all afternoon drooling over sports cars.

example: The runner began to drop behind the rest of the group in the final stage of the race.
simple: Sales began to drop away last month.

example: The noise of the passing train drowned out our conversation.
simple: Her voice was drowned out by the wind.

example: My parents drummed the importance of honesty into me from a young age.
simple: The coach drummed discipline into the players.

chinese: 招徠 / 爭取 (支持/生意)
example: The company is launching a new advertising campaign to drum up more business.
simple: He is trying to drum up support for the new policy.

chinese: 擦乾 / 乾涸 / 枯萎
example: The small river completely dried up during the long summer drought.
simple: Use a towel to dry yourself off.

example: He tried to duck out of the meeting early, but his boss saw him leaving.
simple: You can't duck out of your responsibilities.

chinese: 老是想著 (不快的事) / 詳述
example: You shouldn't dwell on your past mistakes; focus on your future instead.
simple: He dwelt on the negative aspects of the plan.

chinese: 減輕 / 緩放 / 放鬆
example: The heavy rain finally began to ease off around midnight.
simple: Ease up on the accelerator!

chinese: 腐蝕 / 侵蝕 / 消耗
example: The acidic rain slowly eats away at the ancient stone statues in the park.
simple: Guilt began to eat away at his mind.

chinese: 消耗 (金錢、時間) / 侵蝕
example: The high cost of living in the city is starting to eat into our monthly savings.
simple: Responsibilities eat into my free time.

chinese: 充滿...的聲音 / 迴響著
example: The empty hallways echoed with the sound of the children's laughter.
simple: The stadium echoed with cheers.

chinese: 擠掉 / 以微弱優勢擊敗
example: The small local shop was eventually edged out by the large supermarket chain.
pattern: S + be edged out by + N
simple: He was edged out of the starting lineup.

chinese: 艱苦維持 / 勉強彌補 (不足)
example: The refugees managed to eke out a living in the desert despite the harsh conditions.
simple: They eke out a meager existence on a small farm.

example: Could you please elaborate on your proposal so we can understand the details?
simple: He refused to elaborate on his plans.

example: The team was eliminated from the competition after losing the first round.
simple: Try to eliminate fat from your diet.

chinese: 著手進行 / 開始 (新旅程或計畫)
example: The young entrepreneur is ready to embark on a new business venture.
simple: She embarked on a career in politics.

chinese: 從...中出現 / 擺脫 (困境)
example: The bear slowly emerged from its cave after a long winter of hibernation.
simple: The country is emerging from a recession.

example: The Mississippi River eventually empties into the Gulf of Mexico.
pattern: 河流 + empty(ies) into + 海/湖
simple: All these pipes empty into the main sewer.

example: The scholarship enabled her to study abroad without worrying about the cost.
simple: The new technology enables us to work faster.

example: Parents should encourage their children in their creative pursuits.
simple: She encouraged me in my ambition.

example: She was endowed with a beautiful singing voice and natural acting talent.
simple: Humans are endowed with reason.

example: The two countries decided to engage in diplomatic talks to resolve the conflict.
simple: He engages in regular exercise.

example: My grandmother always enquires after you whenever I visit her.
simple: He called to enquire after his sick friend.

example: A committee has been formed to enquire into the causes of the accident.
simple: The police are enquiring into the robbery.

example: She decided to enrol in an online photography course to improve her skills.
simple: They enrolled their child in a private school.

chinese: 因...而產生 / 隨之而來
example: The problems that ensued from the merger were more difficult than expected.
simple: Chaos ensued from the power failure.

chinese: 防範 / 確保不發生 (負面事物)
example: We need to update our security system to ensure against cyber attacks.
simple: The policy ensures against future losses.

example: The young athlete decided to enter for the marathon next spring.
simple: She entered for the entrance exam.

chinese: 進入 (討論) / 締結 (協議)
example: The two companies are planning to enter into a partnership next year.
simple: They entered into a long argument.

example: This membership card entitles you to a ten percent discount at all our stores.
simple: You are entitled to your opinion.

example: The manager entrusted the important task to his most reliable assistant.
simple: I entrusted my keys to the neighbor.

example: It is difficult to erase painful memories from one's mind completely.
simple: She erased the name from the list.

chinese: 由...演化而成 / 發展成為
example: What started as a simple idea eventually evolved into a global movement.
simple: Birds are thought to have evolved from dinosaurs.

example: She has always excelled in mathematics and science since she was a primary school student.
simple: He excels at playing the piano.

type: 片語動詞 / 形容詞片語
example: Low-income families are often exempt from paying certain local taxes.
simple: The charity is exempt from tax.

example: The speaker asked for more time to expand on the results of the research.
simple: Could you expand on that idea?

example: The manager expects a high level of dedication from all of his employees.
simple: I expect better of you.

chinese: 對...做實驗 / 嘗試 (新方法)
example: Scientists are currently experimenting with new materials to create lighter batteries.
simple: They experimented on lab mice.

example: The doctor spent a long time explaining the treatment to the concerned parents.
simple: Can you explain this rule to me?

chinese: 使暴露於... / 使接觸到
example: Traveling can expose you to different ways of thinking and living.
simple: The painting was exposed to direct sunlight.

chinese: 延伸到 / 給予 (邀請、感激)
example: The invitation was extended to all members of the local community.
simple: The deadline was extended to next week.

example: Scientists can extract DNA from very small samples of hair or skin.
pattern: S + extract + N + from + 來源
simple: They managed to extract a confession from him.

chinese: 勇敢地面對並壓倒 (對手)
example: The politician managed to face down his critics and win the vote.
simple: The protesters faced down the police.

example: His anger slowly faded away as he realized it was just a misunderstanding.
simple: The music faded away into the distance.

example: If you fail in your duties, the entire team will suffer the consequences.
simple: He failed in his attempt to break the record.

example: When his plan failed, he had no savings to fall back on.
pattern: S + have + N + to fall back on
simple: She can always fall back on her medical training.

chinese: 落空 / 失敗 (計畫、協議)
example: The deal fell through at the last minute because the two companies couldn't agree on the price.
simple: The trip fell through when she got sick.

example: You should familiarize yourself with the rules before you start the game.
simple: Please familiarize yourself with the safety exit.

chinese: 抵擋 / 避開 (問題、攻擊)
example: The manager had to fend off a series of difficult questions during the press conference.
simple: He used his umbrella to fend off the dog.

example: It's better to fess up now than to wait until they catch you.
simple: Come on, fess up! Who ate the cake?

example: The soldiers fought back bravely against the invaders.
simple: She tried to fight back her tears.

example: My body is trying to fight off a cold, so I feel a bit tired today.
simple: They fought off the attackers.

example: Did you figure in the cost of shipping when you set the price?
simple: The inflation rate must be figured in.

example: The company was forced to file for bankruptcy after failing to pay its debts.
simple: They filed for divorce after two years.

example: We need to stop at a gas station to fill up the tank before we head out.
simple: The stadium filled up quickly.

chinese: 過濾掉 / (消息)走漏 / 透過
example: The sunlight filtered through the dense leaves of the forest.
simple: The air filter filters out dust.

chinese: 完成 / 結束 / 吃完
example: Let's finish off the remaining work so we can go home early.
simple: They finished up the pizza.

example: If you have any questions about the project, just fire away!
simple: I'm ready to answer, so fire away.

chinese: 快速發出 (郵件、問題) / 砲轟
example: The angry customer fired off a series of emails to the company's head office.
simple: The reporter fired off three questions at once.

chinese: 啟動 (引擎、電腦) / 激起熱情
example: We need to fire up the old generator before the storm gets any worse.
simple: The coach's speech really fired up the players.

chinese: 使穩固 / 確定 (計畫、細節)
example: We need to meet tomorrow to firm up the details of our travel itinerary.
simple: The company is trying to firm up its position in the market.

chinese: 間接打聽 / 渴望獲得 (讚美、資訊)
example: She was obviously fishing for a compliment when she showed us her new painting.
simple: He was fishing for information about the secret plan.

chinese: 適應 / 融合 / 抽空安排
example: The new student struggled to fit in with her classmates at first.
simple: The doctor can fit you in at four o'clock.

chinese: 逐漸消失 / 虎頭蛇尾
example: The initial enthusiasm for the project slowly fizzled out as the weeks passed.
simple: Their relationship just fizzled out after they moved away.

example: He managed to flag down a passing police car after his car broke down on the highway.
simple: We flagged down a taxi near the station.

chinese: (火焰)突然燃起 / (情緒、病痛)突然爆發
example: Tensions between the two countries flared up again over the border dispute.
simple: His back pain flared up during the long flight.

example: The movie frequently flashes back to the main character's childhood in the countryside.
simple: My mind flashed back to the day of the accident.

chinese: 充實內容 / 使...具體化
example: You have a good idea, but you need to flesh it out with more specific details.
simple: The writer fleshed out the characters in the second chapter.

chinese: 快速翻閱 / 瀏覽 (電視頻道)
example: I was flicking through a magazine while waiting for my appointment at the clinic.
simple: He spent the evening flicking through TV channels.

chinese: 抓狂 / 發瘋 / 激動
example: My mother will flip out when she sees the mess we made in the kitchen.
simple: I nearly flipped out when I heard I won the lottery.

chinese: 調情 / 挑逗 / 考慮 (某主意) / 冒...風險
example: The company is flirting with the idea of moving its headquarters to Singapore.
simple: Stop flirting with my sister!

example: Applications began to flood in as soon as the job advertisement was posted online.
simple: Letters of support flooded in from all over the country.

example: The company's problems flow from a lack of effective leadership at the top.
simple: Wealth does not always flow from hard work.

example: He spent too much time playing games and eventually flunked out of university.
simple: He flunked out in his first semester.

example: The police used tear gas to flush the suspects out of the abandoned building.
simple: Drink plenty of water to flush out the toxins.

chinese: 貫徹始終 / 完成 (動作)
example: The government promised to reduce taxes, but they failed to follow through on their promise.
simple: You need to follow through with your training plan.

chinese: 後續跟進 / 進一步行動
example: The doctor will follow up with another appointment in two weeks to check your progress.
simple: I need to follow up on that lead.

chinese: 閒逛 / 胡鬧 / 浪費時間
example: Stop fooling around and start studying for your final exams!
simple: The kids were just fooling around in the pool.

example: In the winter, wild animals often have to forage for food under the snow.
simple: We spent the afternoon foraging for mushrooms.

chinese: 擊退 / 抑制 (眼淚、情緒)
example: The soldiers fought bravely to force back the invading army.
simple: She tried to force back her tears.

example: I need to delete some old photos to free up some space on my smartphone.
simple: The new law will free up more money for education.

example: The lake usually freezes over in January, allowing people to go ice skating.
simple: The river has frozen over.

example: My computer keeps freezing up whenever I try to open this large file.
simple: I froze up when the teacher called my name.

example: I need to freshen up before we head out for dinner; it's been a long flight.
simple: The room needs a coat of paint to freshen it up.

example: Don't fret about the results of the test; you've already done your best.
simple: There's no point fretting about things you can't change.

example: The barking of the dog frightened away the stranger at the gate.
simple: The high prices frightened off many customers.

example: Smoking in the office is strictly frowned upon by the company management.
simple: Public displays of anger are frowned on here.

example: We should stop and fuel up before we start our long drive across the desert.
simple: The plane is fueling up for the flight.

chinese: 過分關心 / 對...大驚小怪
example: My grandmother always fusses over me whenever I visit her, making sure I eat enough.
simple: She fusses over every small detail.

example: The young singer has gained in confidence since her first public performance.
simple: The house has gained in value.

chinese: (因賭博) 輸掉 / 揮霍掉
example: He sadly gambled away his entire inheritance in just a few months.
simple: Don't gamble your future away.

chinese: 收拾 / 收集 / 鼓起 (勇氣)
example: The children gathered up their toys and went inside for dinner.
simple: She gathered up her courage to speak.

example: They sat on the beach and gazed at the beautiful sunset over the ocean.
simple: He gazed at her in wonder.

chinese: 為...做好準備 / 蓄勢待發
example: The tech company is gearing up for the launch of its new smartphone next month.
simple: Schools are gearing up for the new semester.

chinese: 解釋清楚 / 使人理解
example: The teacher used simple diagrams to help get the complicated concept across.
simple: I failed to get my point across.

example: He worked late every night to get ahead of his colleagues in the department.
simple: The car speeded up to get ahead of the bus.

example: She played a prank on him just to get back at him for what he did earlier.
pattern: S + get back at + 人 (for + N)
simple: I'll get back at him one day.

chinese: 回覆 / 回到 (主題/工作)
example: I'm not sure of the answer right now, but I will get back to you by tomorrow.
simple: Let's get back to work.

example: If you don't submit your reports on time, you will quickly get behind with your work.
simple: The whole community got behind the new project.

example: He was determined to get even with his neighbor for cutting down the old tree.
simple: I'll get even with you for this!

chinese: 出發 / 開始 / 運作起來
example: It's getting late; we should really get going if we want to catch the last train.
simple: The project is finally getting going.

chinese: 與...相處融洽 / 繼續做 (某事)
example: I get on very well with my new classmates, and we often study together.
pattern: S + get on with + 人/物
simple: Stop talking and get on with your work.

chinese: 抽空去做 / 終於著手做 (延宕已久的事)
example: I haven't got round to reading that book yet, but I plan to do it during the holiday.
simple: I finally got round to fixing the faucet.

chinese: 讓路給 / 被...取代 / 屈服於
example: Drivers must give way to pedestrians at the zebra crossing.
simple: Traditional shops are giving way to modern malls.

example: She glanced through the menu quickly and ordered a salad.
simple: He glanced through the report before the meeting.

example: Her eyes began to glaze over as the professor continued his long and boring lecture.
simple: His eyes glazed over when I mentioned math.

chinese: 掩蓋 (錯誤、缺陷) / 帶過
example: The report glossed over the company's financial problems to make the situation look better.
simple: Don't try to gloss over your mistakes.

example: The secret of what she had done began to gnaw at her conscience.
simple: Fear gnawed at his heart.

chinese: 食言 / 違背 (諾言、決定)
example: You promised to help us, and it's too late to go back on your word now.
simple: He would never go back on a promise.

chinese: 破產 / 倒閉 / 沉沒 / 接受麻醉
example: Thousands of small businesses went under during the global economic recession.
simple: The ship went under in the heavy storm.

chinese: 做好準備 / 蓄勢待發
example: The factory is gearing up for a major increase in production next month.
simple: The team is gearing up for the finals.

example: The poet's latest work gives voice to the struggles of the working class.
simple: She gave voice to our fears.

chinese: 上升 / 增長 / (建築物)被興建
example: The price of gasoline is expected to go up again next week due to the global oil shortage.
simple: New apartment blocks are going up all over the city.

chinese: 沒有...也行 / 將就 / 忍受缺乏
example: We had to go without fresh water for two days after the main pipe burst.
simple: He can't go without coffee in the morning.

chinese: 狼吞虎嚥 / 迅速消耗
example: The hungry children gobbled up the entire pizza in less than ten minutes.
simple: High fuel prices are gobbling up our savings.

chinese: 狼吞虎嚥 / 塞滿 (食物)
example: It's easy to gorge on sweets and snacks while watching a long movie.
simple: The guests gorged on the expensive buffet.

example: She graduated from National Taiwan University with a degree in political science.
simple: He graduated from high school last June.

chinese: 扭打 / 努力解決 (難題)
example: The government is currently grappling with the problem of rising unemployment.
simple: She is grappling with a difficult decision.

chinese: 磨擦 / 使人煩躁 / 刺耳
example: His constant complaining began to grate on my nerves after a few hours.
simple: The high-pitched sound grates on my ears.

example: Younger voters tend to gravitate toward candidates who promise environmental reform.
simple: Tourists gravitate to the city center.

chinese: (牲畜)吃草 / 吃零食 (代正餐)
example: We saw a small group of cows grazing on the grass in the field.
simple: I prefer grazing on healthy snacks throughout the day.

chinese: 以...迎接 / 受到...的反應
example: The singer's performance was greeted with a standing ovation from the crowd.
simple: He greeted her with a warm smile.

chinese: 磨碎 / 壓垮 / 使精疲力竭
example: Years of hard physical labor eventually ground him down.
simple: The continuous stress is grinding her down.

chinese: 艱難地產出 / 粗製濫造
example: He had to grind out a 2,000-word essay by tomorrow morning.
simple: The factory grinds out cheap toys every day.

example: Stop griping about the weather and enjoy your holiday!
simple: Employees were griping about the low pay.

example: The table was groaning under the weight of all the delicious food.
simple: The country is groaning under heavy taxes.

chinese: 訓練 / 培養...接替職務
example: She is being groomed for the position of CEO when the current one retires.
simple: He was groomed for a career in law.

chinese: 摸索 / 搜尋 (字眼、解答)
example: He was so nervous during the interview that he had to grope for the right words.
simple: She groped for the light switch in the dark.

example: His theories are firmly grounded in scientific facts and evidence.
simple: The course provides students with a good grounding in math.

example: The two best friends slowly grew apart after they went to different universities.
simple: Couples often grow apart over the years.

chinese: 漸漸喜歡上 / 逐漸加深印象
example: I didn't like the song at first, but it has really started to grow on me.
simple: The city will grow on you once you stay longer.

chinese: 長大而穿不下 / 源於 / 戒除 (習慣)
example: The little boy has already grown out of the shoes we bought him last year.
simple: He eventually grew out of his childhood fears.

example: Some people just love to grumble about the weather no matter how nice it is.
simple: The passengers grumbled about the delay.

chinese: 防範 / 預防 (負面事物)
example: We must install security software to guard against viruses and hackers.
simple: Proper nutrition helps guard against illness.

example: Without accurate data, we can only guess at the real cause of the problem.
pattern: S + can only guess at + N
simple: He could only guess at what she was thinking.

example: The teacher's role is to guide students to find their own answers.
simple: The stars guided them to the shore.

chinese: 抑制 (眼淚) / 狼吞虎嚥地喝
example: She had to gulp back her tears and continue her speech.
simple: He gulped down a glass of cold water.

chinese: 使...出故障 / 搞亂 / 黏住
example: The dust in the machine will eventually gum up the entire system.
simple: Bureaucracy often gums up the legal process.

example: The suspect was gunned down by the police after he refused to surrender.
simple: Several people were gunned down in the street.

example: Cybercriminals tried to hack into the bank's database to steal personal information.
simple: He was arrested for hacking into government computers.

example: The famous scientist hails from a small, remote village in the mountains.
simple: The rare plant hails from the Amazon rainforest.

chinese: 磨合出 / 艱難達成 (協議)
example: The two countries finally managed to hammer out a new trade agreement.
simple: They spent days hammering out the details.

example: Could you please hand around these cookies to everyone in the room?
simple: He handed around a photo of his new car.

example: You should hang onto those old records; they might be worth a lot of money someday.
simple: Hang onto your hat, it's very windy!

chinese: 團結一致 / (說法)一致、站得住腳
example: The witness's story doesn't really hang together; there are too many contradictions.
simple: If we don't hang together, we will fail separately.

example: Even though he had everything, he still hankered after the simple life of his childhood.
