example: We spent the whole afternoon just hacking around in the park with our friends.
simple: They were just hacking around waiting for the show.

chinese: 砍掉 / 使人氣憤 (口語)
example: The gardener had to hack off the dead branches of the tree.
simple: His rude behavior really hacked me off.

example: The explorers used machetes to hack through the dense jungle vines.
simple: They hacked through the frozen ground.

chinese: 苦苦思索 / 不停地做
example: He has been hammering at this math problem all morning without success.
simple: Someone was hammering at the door.

chinese: 努力工作 / 持續攻擊 / 反覆強調
example: The journalist kept hammering away at the same question during the interview.
simple: They hammered away until they reached a deal.

example: Our coach tried to hammer the importance of teamwork into our heads every day.
simple: He hammered the nail in.

example: Once you finish reading the report, please hand it on to the next person.
simple: Customs were handed on from father to son.

example: A suspicious-looking man was seen hanging about near the school gate.
simple: Stop hanging about and do some work!

example: Everyone else rushed forward, but she hung back, unsure of what to do.
simple: He hung back while the others entered the room.

example: Her long black hair hung down almost to her waist.
simple: Curtains hung down from the windows.

chinese: 堅持住 / 挺住 (非正式)
example: I know the project is difficult, but hang in there! We are almost finished.
simple: He hung in until the crisis was over.

chinese: 懸而未決 / 威脅著 / 籠罩
example: The threat of redundancy hung over the factory workers for many months.
simple: A cloud of suspicion hangs over him.

chinese: 偶然出現 / 碰巧路過
example: I was looking for a taxi when an empty one happened along.
simple: A stranger happened along and helped us.

example: I happened on a very rare book while browsing in a second-hand bookstore.
simple: They happened on a beautiful beach during their hike.

chinese: 凝固成 / 變硬成為 / 變成 (固定模式)
example: The flowing lava slowly began to cool and harden into solid rock.
simple: Small disagreements hardened into deep hatred.

chinese: (使植物)適應室外環境 / 抗寒化
example: You should harden off the seedlings before transplanting them into the garden.
simple: The plants need to be hardened off gradually.

chinese: 詳談 / 重新討論 (已討論過的事)
example: There is no need to hash over the same old arguments again and again.
simple: They spent hours hashing over the project details.

chinese: 跳下車 / 跳上車 (大眾運輸)
example: You can hop on the free shuttle bus at the main entrance of the theme park.
pattern: S + hop on/off + 大眾運輸
simple: We hopped off the bus at the wrong stop.

example: He told the driver to stop for a moment so he could hop out and buy a newspaper.
simple: She hopped out of bed as soon as the alarm went off.

chinese: 跳過 / 順道拜訪 (非正式)
example: The little boy managed to hop over the small fence into the neighbor's garden.
simple: I'll hop over to your house this evening.

chinese: 在萬分渺茫中抱著一線希望
example: They were hoping against hope that the earthquake survivors would still be found alive.
pattern: S + be hoping against hope that + 子句
simple: She hoped against hope for a miracle.

example: The firefighters spent an hour hosing down the building to cool the hot walls.
simple: He hosed down the dusty driveway.

chinese: 驅逐 / 迫使 (某人) 離開 (職位或地方)
example: The official was hounded out of office after the details of the scandal were revealed.
pattern: S + be hounded out of + N
simple: The media hounded him out of his home.

example: The stray dogs were housed in a large temporary shelter near the city outskirts.
simple: The library collection is housed in a fireproof vault.

chinese: 盤旋 / 徘徊 / 守在旁邊
example: The waiter continued to hover around our table, waiting for us to finish our meal.
simple: A hawk was hovering over the field.

chinese: 吼叫使某人無法說話 / 喝倒采
example: The protesters tried to howl down the speaker, so no one could hear his speech.
simple: The crowd howled down the unpopular politician.

chinese: 擠在一起 / 縮成一團 / 緊挨著
example: The penguins huddled together against the freezing Antarctic winds to stay warm.
simple: The kittens were huddling up for warmth.

example: I spent the whole morning trying to hunt out my old high school yearbooks.
simple: We need to hunt up some more evidence.

example: The angry man hurled a stone through the window of the abandoned house.
simple: He hurled the ball into the net.

phrase: hurry away / back / in / into / off / on / through
chinese: 趕緊離開 / 趕回 / 匆忙進入
example: Don't hurry through your breakfast; you still have plenty of time before school.
simple: She hurried back home when she realized she forgot her keys.

chinese: 急需 / 缺乏 (通常用於否定)
example: That successful company is certainly not hurting for new investment opportunities.
simple: He is not hurting for money these days.

chinese: 疾馳而下 / 飛速穿過
example: The race car hurtled through the finish line at an incredible speed.
simple: A huge rock hurtled down the mountainside.

chinese: 催促進入 / 誘使 (某人) 匆忙做...
example: The suspicious salesman tried to hustle me into signing the contract without reading it.
simple: The guards hustled the prisoners into the van.

example: The lake usually ices over in January, allowing local residents to go skating.
simple: The car windshield iced up during the night.

example: Stop idling around and start focusing on your project; the deadline is tomorrow.
simple: They spent the afternoon idling about in the park.

chinese: 引發成為 / 點燃變成
example: The small disagreement suddenly ignited into a fierce argument between the two neighbors.
simple: The dry grass ignited into a major fire.

example: The president was impeached for abuse of power and obstruction of justice.
simple: The judge was impeached for bribery.

example: Lack of proper funding has impeded the scientists from continuing their research.
simple: The injury impeded him from running.

example: The prisoners implored for mercy, but the cruel dictator ignored their pleas.
simple: She implored her parents for a second chance.

example: The company imports high-tech components from Japan to assemble its products.
pattern: S + import + N + from + 來源
simple: Oil is imported into the country from abroad.

example: The teacher tried to impress on the students the importance of regular exercise.
simple: He impressed upon me the need for secrecy.

example: She impressed the interviewers with her extensive knowledge of the industry.
simple: The building impressed everyone with its size.

example: The revolutionary was imprisoned in a small, dark cell for many years.
simple: She felt imprisoned in an unhappy marriage.

chinese: 在...上即興創作 / 即興發揮
example: The jazz musician began to improvise on the main melody of the song.
simple: He had to improvise on his speech when he lost his notes.

chinese: 以...正式開展 / 以...舉行開幕式
example: The new museum was inaugurated with a special exhibition of ancient art.
simple: The bridge was inaugurated with a grand ceremony.

example: The politician was accused of trying to incite the crowd to violence.
simple: The speech incited the soldiers to rebellion.

example: I incline to the view that education is the key to solving most social problems.
simple: He inclines toward a more traditional style.

chinese: 併入 / 融入 / 納入
example: The designers decided to incorporate traditional elements into the modern building.
simple: The suggestion was incorporated into the final report.

example: The eggs are carefully incubated in a temperature-controlled environment for 21 days.
pattern: S + be incubated in + 地方
simple: The idea was incubated in the small research lab.

example: The manager has already indented for a new set of office furniture.
simple: We need to indent for more stationery.

chinese: 簽約成為...的學徒 / 束縛
example: In the past, many young boys were indentured to master craftsmen to learn a trade.
simple: He was indentured to a printer.

example: The scientist indicated to the committee that the initial results were promising.
simple: She indicated the direction to the tourist.

example: The former senator was officially indicted for tax fraud and conspiracy.
simple: The gang was indicted for murder.

chinese: 大批滋生 / 遍佈 (害蟲)
example: The old wooden house was found to be infested with termites and needed treatment.
simple: The kitchen was infested with cockroaches.

example: Spies managed to infiltrate into the enemy's headquarters during the night.
simple: New ideas slowly infiltrate into the community.

example: His speech was designed to inflame the crowd with anger and resentment.
simple: His eyes were inflamed with dust.

example: He used a small pump to inflate the basketball with air before the game.
simple: He was inflated with pride after winning.

chinese: 告發 / 舉報 (某人的罪行)
example: The criminal decided to inform on his partners to get a shorter sentence.
simple: She refused to inform against her friends.

example: She was infuriated with his constant lies and decided to break up with him.
simple: His arrogance infuriated me.

chinese: 注入 (品質、情感) / 泡 (茶)
example: The new manager tried to infuse some enthusiasm into the discouraged team.
simple: The herbs should be infused into hot water.

example: Please initial the bottom of each page with your pen to confirm you've read it.
simple: The changes were initialed with blue ink.

example: He was initiated into the secret society during a ceremony held last night.
simple: She initiated me into the mysteries of gardening.

chinese: 注射進 / 注入 (金錢、新活力)
example: The government decided to inject more money into the public school system.
simple: His presence injected some humor into the meeting.

example: The expensive jewelry box was beautifully inset with small diamonds.
simple: The table was inset with a marble pattern.

example: Parents should strive to instill a sense of honesty in their children.
simple: The coach instilled confidence in his players.

example: Parents should strive to instill a strong sense of responsibility into their children from a young age.
simple: Good teachers instill confidence into their students.

example: The company decided to institute a new policy with specific guidelines for remote work.
simple: The law was instituted with much public support.

example: The competition for the championship will likely intensify with the arrival of the new season.
simple: His headache intensified with the loud noise.

example: The lawyer decided to intercede for the young defendant, hoping for a lighter sentence.
simple: Friends interceded for him to get the job back.

chinese: 向 (某人) 求情 / 調解
example: I asked the manager to intercede with the board of directors on my behalf.
simple: He interceded with the authorities for the prisoner.

example: Intelligence agencies managed to intercept secret messages from the enemy group.
pattern: S + intercept + N + from + 來源
simple: The letter was intercepted from the mail.

example: In some contexts, the two technical terms can be interchanged with each other.
simple: Daylight interchanges with darkness.

example: The railway network is designed to interconnect with the major airports in the country.
simple: The systems are interconnected with high-speed cables.

chinese: 禁止 (某人) 做某事 / 禁制
example: The pilot was interdicted from flying for six months due to a safety violation.
simple: They were interdicted from entering the building.

type: 動詞 / 名詞 + 介系詞
example: Traveling can interest people in learning about diverse cultures and histories.
simple: She has an interest in ancient history.

example: He occasionally interjected comments into the discussion to express his doubts.
simple: He interjected a question into the speech.

example: The novel's plot is interlaced with several mysterious subplots.
simple: Fingers were interlaced with each other.

example: The book contains beautiful illustrations interleaved with the text.
simple: Lessons are interleaved with practical exercises.

example: Our individual successes are closely interlinked with the performance of the team.
simple: The history of the two nations is interlinked.

example: The pieces of the puzzle are designed to interlock with each other perfectly.
simple: The two systems interlock to provide security.

chinese: 闖入 / 干涉 (他人事務)
example: He was accused of trying to interlope in the company's internal affairs.
simple: Don't interlope in their argument.

example: Over many centuries, the different ethnic groups began to intermarry with each other.
simple: Royal families often intermarried with each other.

example: Fact and fiction often intermingle with each other in historical novels.
simple: The tourists intermingled with the locals.

example: The chemicals should not be allowed to intermix with water during the experiment.
simple: Oil does not intermix with water.

example: He spent the summer interning in the marketing department of a tech giant.
simple: She was interned in a camp during the war.

example: The small local business eventually internationalized into a famous global brand.
simple: The curriculum was internationalized into a diverse program.

example: The various elements of the culture interpenetrate with each other to form a unique society.
simple: Religious and political ideas often interpenetrate.

example: Some later editors chose to interpolate their own opinions into the original text.
simple: Data points were interpolated into the graph.

example: He tried to interpose himself between the two arguing neighbors to stop the fight.
simple: A barrier was interposed between the two groups.

example: Psychologists study how different emotions interrelate with each other in human behavior.
simple: Poverty and crime are often interrelated.

example: The police interrogated the suspect for hours about his whereabouts on the night of the crime.
simple: The witness was interrogated about her testimony.

example: The speaker was frequently interrupted with loud cheers and applause from the audience.
simple: She interrupted him with a question.

example: The new highway is planned to intersect with the existing railway line near the city.
simple: Where the two roads intersect, there is a gas station.

chinese: 散佈在...之間 / 以...點綴
example: Bright wildflowers were interspersed among the tall grasses in the field.
simple: Laughter was interspersed with tears during the speech.

example: The history of the two countries is closely intertwined with each other.
simple: Roots of the tree were intertwined with those of its neighbors.

example: Tradition and modernity are beautifully interwoven with each other in Japanese society.
simple: Several subplots are interwoven with the main story.

chinese: 向...暗示 / 透露 (正式用法)
example: The manager intimated to his staff that there might be some changes in the department.
pattern: S + intimate + (that) + 子句 / S + intimate + to + 人
simple: He intimated to me his intention to resign.

example: New technology was introduced into the manufacturing process to improve efficiency.
simple: Potatoes were introduced into Europe from America.

example: He didn't want to intrude into their private conversation, so he waited outside.
simple: The noise intruded on my peace of mind.

example: Doctors often become inured to the sight of suffering after years of practice.
simple: He was inured to the cold weather.

example: The small town was suddenly invaded with tourists during the spring festival.
simple: The country was invaded with an enemy army.

example: The politician continued to inveigh against the government's high taxes.
simple: He inveighed against the injustice of the situation.

example: The salesman tried to inveigle the elderly woman into buying the expensive product.
simple: She was inveigled into joining the club.

example: This new tool was specially invented for people with physical disabilities.
simple: The telescope was invented for astronomy.

chinese: 賦予 (權力、特質) / 以...投資
example: The new laws invest the local government with more power to control pollution.
simple: She was invested with the title of Queen.

type: 動詞 / 名詞 + 介系詞
example: The commission is conducting an investigation into the causes of the financial crisis.
simple: The police are investigating into the matter.

example: It started to rain, so she kindly decided to invite the neighbors in for a hot cup of tea.
simple: Please invite our guests in.

example: The ancient ritual was meant to invoke peace and prosperity in the community.
simple: The speech invoked a sense of nostalgia in the audience.

example: He didn't want to get involved with the ongoing legal dispute between his two business partners.
simple: She is deeply involved with the local charity.

example: A strange, eerie light began to issue forth from the windows of the abandoned mansion.
simple: Smoke issued forth from the chimney.

example: All the expenses for the business trip are clearly itemized in the final report.
simple: Please itemize your requirements in the email.

example: The software is programmed to iterate over the data set until it finds an accurate solution.
simple: The computer will iterate over the list of numbers.

example: The speaker took a quick jab at his opponent's failed economic policies during the debate.
pattern: S + take a jab at + 人/物
simple: He jabbed at the meat with his fork.

example: She was so excited about the trip that she wouldn't stop jabbering about it all morning.
simple: Stop jabbering about your problems!

chinese: 接入 / 放棄 (工作或計畫)
example: After working as a lawyer for ten years, he decided to jack it all in and become a farmer.
simple: Jack in your job if you really hate it.

chinese: 塞住 / 故障 / 堵塞
example: A small piece of paper somehow managed to jam up the entire office printer.
simple: The traffic was jammed up for miles.

chinese: 刺激 (神經) / 發出刺耳聲
example: The constant noise of the construction site began to jangle on her already frayed nerves.
simple: The telephone's ring jangled on in the quiet house.

chinese: 碰撞 / 與...衝突 / 不協調
example: Her modern, colorful furniture really jars with the traditional style of the ancient house.
simple: The loud music jarred against the peaceful atmosphere.

example: My manager always jaws at us about productivity whenever we take a short break.
simple: Stop jawing at me!

example: After several discussions, our vague ideas finally began to jell into a concrete plan.
simple: The team's strategy is starting to jell into a winner.

example: It's important to know which topics are sensitive and shouldn't be jested about in public.
simple: He was only jesting about leaving his job.

example: The cargo was jettisoned from the sinking ship to keep it afloat for as long as possible.
simple: They jettisoned outdated ideas from the project.

example: Many investors might jib at the high risk associated with this new technology.
simple: He jibbed at the idea of working on weekends.

example: The loose key continued to jiggle about in the lock but wouldn't turn.
simple: Stop jiggling around and sit still!

example: The poor man was jilted by his fiancée only two days before the wedding ceremony.
simple: He felt heartbroken after being jilted by his girlfriend.

chinese: 發出叮噹聲 / 充滿 (錢、情感)
example: His pockets jingle with coins every time he walks down the street.
simple: The keys jingled with a metallic sound.

example: To save on production costs, the company decided to job out the manufacturing to a smaller factory.
pattern: S + job out + N + to + 單位
simple: The construction work was jobbed out to various contractors.

example: The two ambitious politicians are currently jockeying for the position of party leader.
simple: Reporters were jockeying for a good spot to take photos.

chinese: 勉強維持 / 緩慢進展
example: Despite the lack of new funding, the small project managed to joggle along for another year.
simple: The old bus joggled along the uneven road.

example: The architect decided to join the new wing to the original building with a glass hallway.
simple: A small garage was joined on to the side of the house.

example: If we all join together, we can surely find a solution to this environmental crisis.
simple: The two parts were joined together with glue.

example: He decided to join up as soon as he finished high school because he wanted to serve his country.
simple: The two paths join up at the end of the forest.

chinese: 使震驚而進入 (狀態或行動)
example: The sudden loud noise jolted her into awareness, and she realized something was wrong.
simple: The news jolted the government into action.

chinese: 以...為樂 / 樂於 (正式用法)
example: She truly joys in the simple pleasures of gardening and spending time in nature.
simple: Parents joy in the success of their children.

chinese: 使更有活力 / 加強 / 改裝 (車)
example: We need to juice up the website with some colorful images and interactive features.
simple: The band juiced up the crowd with a fast song.

example: All the old toys were jumbled up in a large cardboard box in the attic.
simple: Don't jumble up the files; they are in alphabetical order.

example: The music festival will jump off with a performance by a famous local rock band.
simple: The divers jumped off the cliff into the sea.

chinese: 跳出來 / 顯眼 / 一眼看出
example: The spelling error in the headline jumped out at me immediately.
simple: A suspicious-looking man jumped out from behind the tree.

example: The horse skillfully managed to jump over the tall fence during the competition.
simple: The kids were jumping over the puddles.

chinese: 跳過 / 經受考驗 (辦理繁瑣手續)
example: I had to jump through many hoops to get a work visa for this country.
pattern: S + have to jump through + N
simple: The dog jumped through the open window.

example: He jumped up in surprise when the alarm clock went off suddenly.
simple: She jumped up to answer the doorbell.

chinese: 向...證明正當性 / 解釋
example: You don't need to justify your personal choices to anyone else.
simple: The manager had to justify the cost to the board.

example: The teacher had to keep after the students to ensure they finished their reports on time.
simple: The debt collector kept after him for months.

example: I like to keep a spare set of keys around in case I ever lose mine.
simple: She decided to keep him around for help.

example: I'll keep this special bottle of wine for our tenth wedding anniversary.
pattern: S + keep + N + for + 目的/人
simple: Please keep a seat for me.

chinese: 留在室內 / 扣留 / 忍住 (情緒)
example: It was so cold outside that the mother decided to keep the children in all day.
simple: She struggled to keep her anger in.

chinese: 堅持 / 遵守 / 不離開 (道路)
example: It is important to keep to the original plan if we want to finish the project on time.
simple: Keep to the right side of the road.

chinese: 保持團結 / 使不散開
example: The guide reminded the tourists to keep together while walking through the crowded market.
simple: These old papers are kept together with a clip.

chinese: 使與...相稱 / 鎖定 / (引申) 關鍵在於
example: Our production levels must be keyed to the actual demand from the market.
simple: Hard work is the key to success.

example: The engine finally kicked over after he tried to start the car several times.
simple: He accidentally kicked over the trash can.

example: Her eyes began to kindle with excitement as she talked about her future plans.
simple: The wood was kindled with a small match.

example: Be careful not to let the long hose kink up while you are watering the garden.
simple: Our travel plans kinked up due to the flight delay.

example: The mother gently tried to kiss away her child's tears after he fell down.
simple: He kissed away her worries.

chinese: 粗魯地拒絕 / 不予理會 (非正式)
example: She just gave him the kiss off and told him she never wanted to see him again.
pattern: S + give + 人 + the kiss off
simple: The manager kissed off my suggestion without reading it.

example: The chef began to knead the butter into the flour to make a smooth dough.
simple: The clay was kneaded into a small bowl.

example: The sharp prow of the boat managed to knife through the icy waters easily.
simple: The cold wind knifed through my thin jacket.

chinese: 閒逛 / 流浪 / 粗暴對待
example: He spent several years knocking about Europe before finally returning home.
simple: Stop knocking about and do some work!

example: He accidentally knocked his elbow against the sharp edge of the table.
simple: The boat knocked against the pier.

chinese: 大口喝下 / 拒絕 (提議) / 擊退
example: He knocked back the entire glass of water in one go after the long run.
simple: The company knocked back his request for a raise.

chinese: 撞上 / (比喻) 將知識灌輸給某人
example: I wasn't looking where I was going and knocked into an old friend on the street.
simple: The teacher tried to knock some sense into the students.

chinese: 倉促做成 / 湊合著做
example: We managed to knock together a quick meal from the leftovers in the fridge.
simple: He knocked a simple bookshelf together in an afternoon.

chinese: 趕製 / (英式口語) 敲門叫醒
example: Could you please knock me up at seven o'clock tomorrow morning?
simple: She knocked up a quick report for the meeting.

example: The two ends of the rope were knotted together to make a longer line.
simple: Their lives were knotted together by fate.

example: The twins look so similar that even their parents find it hard to know one from the other.
simple: I don't know a hawk from a handsaw.

example: I don't know him personally, but I certainly know of his great reputation as an architect.
simple: Do you know of any good restaurants near here?

example: The manager laced into the staff for their repeated failure to meet the deadline.
simple: She really laced into him after he lied to her.

example: The wealthy children never lacked for anything they wanted.
simple: The project did not lack for volunteers.

chinese: (用杓子) 分發 / 慷慨給予 (建議等)
example: He loves to ladle out advice to anyone who will listen, even if it's not asked for.
simple: She ladled out the soup for the guests.

chinese: 因...而嚴厲譴責 / 以...痛打
example: The politician was lambasted for his controversial remarks about the new tax policy.
simple: The media lambasted him with criticism.

example: The certificates were laminated with a thin layer of plastic to protect them from moisture.
simple: The table top was laminated with wood grain.

example: The butterfly gently landed on a bright red rose in the garden.
simple: The boss really landed on him for being late.

example: I was landed with all the housework after my roommate went away for the weekend.
simple: They landed me with the bill.

example: The sound of small waves lapping against the shore was very relaxing.
simple: The lake water lapped against the side of the boat.

chinese: 充滿 / 穿插 (通常指裝飾過度)
example: His speech was larded with technical terms that no one in the audience understood.
simple: The report was larded with statistics.

example: Stop larking about and focus on your homework!
simple: The children were larking about in the park.

chinese: (大雨) 傾盆而下 / 繫牢
example: The rain began to lash down as soon as we started our hike.
simple: Make sure all the equipment is lashed down.

example: The critics lashed into the movie for its weak plot and poor acting.
simple: He lashed into his opponents during the debate.

example: The meeting lasted for over three hours, much longer than expected.
simple: The storm lasted for two days.

example: We don't have much water, but I hope it will last out until we find a well.
simple: Will our supplies last out the winter?

chinese: 大發議論 / 著手開始 (大計畫)
example: After the long silence, he suddenly launched forth into a speech about social justice.
simple: The company decided to launch out on its own.

example: The criminals tried to launder their illegal profits through a series of fake companies.
simple: Money was laundered through the casino.

example: She always lavishes praise on her children, even for the smallest achievements.
simple: Huge sums were lavished on the wedding.

chinese: 四處亂打 / 懶散度日
example: He began to lay about him with a heavy stick to defend himself from the dogs.
simple: He spent his youth laying about doing nothing.

example: I asked the shopkeeper to lay away the coat for me until I could pay for it.
simple: They lay away a little money every month.

chinese: 提出...供 (某人/單位) 審理
example: The new evidence was laid before the committee for further investigation.
pattern: S + lay + N + before + 人/單位
simple: The proposal will be laid before the board next week.

example: We decided to lay in a large stock of food before the winter storm arrived.
simple: They laid in fuel for the generator.

example: The company laid on a special bus to take the employees to the picnic.
simple: Free drinks were laid on for the guests.

example: A severe cold laid him up for over a week during the winter.
pattern: S + be laid up with + N
simple: I've been laid up with a bad back.

example: They spent their entire vacation lazing around on the beach.
simple: Stop lazing about and do some work!

example: The innocent young man was led astray by bad company at the university.
pattern: S + be led astray by + 人
simple: Don't be led astray by false promises.

example: This quiet path will eventually lead you into the heart of the ancient forest.
pattern: S + lead + 人 + into + 地方
simple: The discussion led into a new topic.

example: The chairman will lead off the meeting with a brief opening speech.
simple: Who wants to lead off the discussion?

chinese: 誤導 / 給予假希望 (通常指感情)
example: She felt betrayed when she realized he was just leading her on and had no intention of marrying her.
simple: Don't lead him on if you don't love him.

example: He was leaning against the wall, waiting patiently for the bus to arrive.
simple: I leaned my bicycle against the fence.

example: He leaned back in his chair and sighed with relief after finishing the difficult task.
simple: Lean back and relax for a moment.

example: Don't lean out of the window; it's very dangerous!
simple: She leaned out to wave goodbye.

example: The teacher leaned over to look at the student's notebook.
simple: The tree leaned over the river.

example: I'm leaning toward the smaller model because it's more fuel-efficient.
simple: The committee leans toward accepting the proposal.

example: The media leaped on the scandal as soon as the news broke.
simple: He leaped on the chance to go abroad.

chinese: 使人一眼看出 / 引人注目
example: The spelling mistake on the front page really leaped out at me.
simple: A suspicious man leaped out from the shadows.

example: He learned the entire poem by heart for the school competition.
simple: We learn by doing, not just by reading.

example: I was deeply shocked to learn of his sudden passing last night.
simple: She only learned of the meeting this morning.

example: We need to leave for the airport at least three hours before our flight.
pattern: S + leave + 地方1 + for + 地方2
simple: He left home for work early today.

example: Don't leave your keys in the car; it's not safe.
pattern: S + leave + N + in + 地方
simple: She was left in charge of the department.

example: Don't get carried away by excitement and spend more money than you planned.
pattern: Don't get carried away by + N
simple: Don't get carried away by the moment.

chinese: 打電話給；召集；使回憶起
example: She called up her old classmates to organize a reunion after many years.
pattern: S + called up + 人 + to + V
simple: He called up his friends to tell them the news.

example: Students should make use of every opportunity to practise speaking English.
pattern: S + should + make use of + N + to + V
simple: We should make use of technology to improve our lives.

example: It took me a long time to make sense of the complicated instructions in the manual.
pattern: It took + 人 + 時間 + to make sense of + N
simple: It took her a while to make sense of the data.

example: Her eyes filled with tears when she heard the touching story about the little girl.
pattern: S + filled with + N + when + S + V
simple: The room filled with laughter when he told the joke.

example: We must stand up for those who cannot speak for themselves and protect their rights.
pattern: S + must stand up for + 人/N + and + V
simple: You should stand up for what you believe in.

example: After a long discussion, the team finally agreed to move forward with the new project.
pattern: S + agreed to move forward with + N
simple: Let's move forward with the plan.

example: The teacher always ends the class with a short review of the key points.
simple: She ended her speech with a quote.

example: A good friend will always stick up for you even when others disagree.
pattern: S + will stick up for + 人 + even when + S + V
simple: He always sticks up for his younger brother.

example: The teacher asked one of the students to read out the answers to the class.
pattern: S + asked + 人 + to read out + N
simple: Please read out your name when called.

example: Make sure you read through the contract carefully before signing your name.
pattern: Make sure S + read(s) through + N + before + V-ing
simple: Read through the report before submitting it.

example: We decided to stop for lunch at a small village we were passing through on our road trip.
pattern: S + pass(ed) through + 地方 + on the way to + 目的地
simple: We passed through several towns on our way to the coast.

example: Could you please clean off the whiteboard before the next class begins?
pattern: Could you clean off + N + before + N?
simple: Clean off the mud before you come inside.

example: It took her the whole afternoon to sort through the boxes of old photos in the attic.
pattern: It took + 人 + 時間 + to sort through + N
simple: I need to sort through my old clothes.

example: We used up all the ink in the printer right before the important presentation.
pattern: S + used up + N + right before + N
simple: Don't use up all the hot water.

example: Always check over your answers before handing in the exam paper.
pattern: Always check over + N + before + V-ing
simple: He checked over the report one more time.

example: The cashier counted out the change and handed it back to the customer.
pattern: S + counted out + N + and + V
simple: Count me out — I'm too tired to join.

chinese: 就…達成共識、對…意見一致
example: After hours of negotiation, both sides finally agreed on the terms of the contract.
pattern: S + agreed on + N + after + N
simple: Can we agree on a meeting time?

example: The manager agreed to the new proposal after reviewing all the supporting data.
pattern: S + agreed to + N + after + V-ing
simple: She agreed to the terms without reading them.

example: The hotel will add on a service charge of ten percent to your final bill.
pattern: S + add(s) on + N + to + N
simple: They added on extra fees without telling us.

example: The heavy rain only added to the confusion during the outdoor music festival.
simple: His complaints only added to the tension.

example: The company decided to pull back from the overseas market due to rising costs.
pattern: S + decided to pull back from + N + due to + N
simple: He pulled back at the last moment.

example: The students were asked to act out a short dialogue in front of the whole class.
pattern: S + were asked to act out + N + in front of + N
simple: The children acted out the story they had read.

example: He finally gathered enough courage to ask her out after months of hesitation.
pattern: S + asked + 人 + out after + N
simple: Are you going to ask her out?

example: The bully backed off as soon as the teacher walked into the classroom.
pattern: S + backed off as soon as + S + V
simple: Back off and give me some space.

chinese: 溫習、重新練習（已學過的技能或知識）
example: I need to brush up on my Spanish before the trip to Mexico next month.
pattern: S + need(s) to brush up on + N + before + N
simple: She brushed up on her math before the exam.

chinese: 內建、將…納入（計畫或設計中）
example: The project manager built in extra time for unexpected delays when creating the schedule.
pattern: S + built in + N + for + N + when + V-ing
simple: Safety features were built in from the start.

example: The spacecraft burns up in the atmosphere before it can reach the ground.
pattern: N + burns up in + N + before + S + can + V
simple: He was burning up with fever last night.

example: The police closed in on the suspect after tracking him for several hours.
pattern: S + closed in on + 人/N + after + V-ing
simple: Darkness was closing in fast.

example: Could you cover for me on Friday afternoon while I attend my sister's graduation?
pattern: Could you cover for + 人 + while + S + V?
simple: She covered for her colleague during the holidays.

example: The journalist spent weeks digging into the financial records of the corporation.
pattern: S + spent + 時間 + digging into + N
simple: Let's dig into the data and find the answer.

example: Rude customer service will drive away even the most loyal customers over time.
pattern: N + will drive away + N + over time
simple: He got in his car and drove away without a word.

example: Feel free to drop in whenever you are in the neighbourhood and have some free time.
pattern: Feel free to drop in whenever + S + V
simple: My old teacher dropped in on us yesterday.

example: My old colleague dropped by the office yesterday to say hello to everyone.
pattern: S + dropped by + 地方 + to + V
simple: Feel free to drop by anytime you're nearby.

example: I will email you back as soon as I have reviewed all the documents you sent.
pattern: S + will email + 人 + back as soon as + S + V
simple: She emailed him back within the hour.

example: The music slowly faded out at the end of the film, leaving the audience in silence.
pattern: N + slowly faded out at + N, leaving + O + in + N
simple: Their friendship faded out after they moved apart.

chinese: 感覺有能力或體力做某事
example: I don't feel up to going to the party tonight because I have a terrible headache.
pattern: S + don't feel up to + V-ing + because + S + V
simple: Do you feel up to taking on this challenge?

example: Spending too much time on social media can really get you down if you're not careful.
pattern: V-ing + can get + 人 + down if + S + V
simple: Let's get down to business and stop wasting time.

example: The teacher handed around copies of the worksheet so everyone could start at the same time.
pattern: S + handed around + N + so + S + could + V
simple: Could you hand around these leaflets for me?

example: Hang on a moment — I need to check the details before I give you a final answer.
pattern: Hang on + 時間 — S + need(s) to + V + before + S + V
simple: Just hang on a little longer — help is on the way.

chinese: 突然想到（好主意）；對…搭訕
example: During the brainstorming session, she hit on a brilliant idea that impressed everyone.
pattern: S + hit on + N + that + V
simple: He hit on a great solution to the problem.

example: Several employees were listening in on the executive meeting without permission.
pattern: S + were listening in on + N + without + N
simple: Mind if I listen in on your conversation?

chinese: 忍受、接受（不好但無法改變的事）；與…同住
example: It's not an ideal solution, but it's something we're going to have to live with for now.
pattern: S + will have to live with + N + for now
simple: You made the decision, so you'll have to live with it.

chinese: 詳細規劃、擬定（計畫或策略）
example: Before starting the business, they mapped out a detailed plan covering every possible risk.
pattern: S + mapped out + N + before + V-ing
simple: Let's map out our route before we leave.

example: After a serious argument, it took them several weeks to patch up their friendship.
pattern: It took + 人 + 時間 + to patch up + N
simple: They managed to patch up their differences in the end.

example: The government plans to phase out all coal-powered plants within the next decade.
pattern: S + plans to phase out + N + within + 時間
simple: Older models will be phased out next year.

example: Despite the bad weather, the hikers decided to press on and reach the summit before dark.
pattern: Despite + N, S + decided to press on and + V
simple: We must press on with the project despite the setbacks.

example: Some companies have profited greatly from the rapid growth of online shopping.
pattern: S + have profited greatly from + N
simple: You can profit from other people's mistakes.

example: The powerful flood swept away several bridges and damaged hundreds of homes overnight.
pattern: N + swept away + N + and + V + O
simple: Old traditions were swept away by modernization.

example: It is wrong to act against your own principles just to please other people.
pattern: It is wrong to act against + N + just to + V
simple: She refused to act against her beliefs.

example: All the small expenses you ignore every day can add up to a surprisingly large amount.
pattern: N + can add up to + N
simple: All these problems add up to a serious crisis.

example: The new safety regulations apply to all workers on the construction site without exception.
pattern: N + apply/applies to + N + without exception
simple: This rule applies to everyone in the company.

example: My younger brother is really into astronomy and spends hours reading about the universe.
pattern: S + is/are really into + N + and + V
simple: Are you into outdoor activities or indoor hobbies?

chinese: 離開、出發；休假；（食物）變質
example: I'm off to the airport now — my flight leaves in less than two hours.
pattern: S + be off to + 地方 — N + V + in + 時間
simple: The milk is off — don't drink it.

chinese: 在做（某事）；由…決定；達到（標準）
example: What have you been up to lately? I haven't heard from you in such a long time.
pattern: What have you been up to + 時間副詞?
simple: It's up to you to decide what we do next.

example: After living abroad for five years, she is now fully used to the local customs and lifestyle.
pattern: After + V-ing, S + be now used to + N/V-ing
simple: I'm not used to waking up this early.

chinese: 遮擋（光線）；排除（雜念）；預留（時間）
example: She put on heavy curtains to block out the sunlight and sleep in on her day off.
pattern: S + V + N + to block out + N
simple: Try to block out all negative thoughts before the exam.

chinese: 迅速花光（金錢）；快速通過
example: He blew through his entire month's salary in just one weekend of reckless spending.
pattern: S + blew through + N + in + 時間
simple: The team blew through the first stage in record time.

example: The popular restaurant is fully booked up for the entire month of December.
pattern: N + be fully booked up for + 時間
simple: I'm booked up all week — can we meet next Monday?

example: It takes great courage to break free from habits that have held you back for years.
pattern: It takes + N + to break free from + N
simple: She finally broke free from a toxic relationship.

chinese: （情緒）湧現、冒出；（液體）冒泡上升
example: Feelings of anxiety began to bubble up inside her as the exam date drew closer.
pattern: N + began to bubble up as + S + V
simple: New ideas started to bubble up during the discussion.

chinese: 以…為核心建立、圍繞…構建
example: The entire marketing strategy was built around the idea of making customers feel valued.
simple: Their business model is built around customer trust.

example: Instead of chasing after wealth and fame, she chose to pursue a life of meaning.
pattern: Instead of chasing after + N, S + chose to + V
simple: Stop chasing after perfection and start making progress.

chinese: 共同出資；插嘴、加入討論
example: Everyone in the office chipped in to buy a farewell gift for the retiring manager.
pattern: S + chipped in to + V + N + for + 人
simple: Would you like to chip in for the birthday cake?

example: Please clear away your books and notes before the exam papers are handed out.
pattern: Please clear away + N + before + S + V
simple: Let's clear away the dishes after dinner.

chinese: 從…彙整、整合（資料或資訊）
example: The report was compiled from data gathered across more than fifty schools nationwide.
pattern: N + was compiled from + N + across + N
simple: This dictionary was compiled from thousands of sources.

chinese: 排擠、取代（使無立足之地）
example: Large supermarket chains have gradually crowded out many small local grocery stores.
simple: Social media has crowded out traditional news sources.

example: The team leader divided up the tasks evenly so that everyone had an equal share of work.
pattern: S + divided up + N + so that + S + V
simple: Let's divide up the work and finish it faster.

example: The loud alarm successfully drove off the intruder before any damage could be done.
pattern: N + drove off + N + before + S + could + V
simple: He got in his car and drove off without a word.

chinese: 推高（價格或數字）；開車抵達
example: The sudden shortage of raw materials drove up the prices of everyday goods significantly.
simple: High demand is driving up property prices.

example: The lead runner dropped back in the final kilometre due to exhaustion and finished third.
pattern: S + dropped back due to + N + and + V
simple: He dropped back to let the others catch up.

example: The heavy rain began to ease off in the late afternoon, allowing the match to continue.
pattern: N + began to ease off, allowing + O + to + V
simple: The boss eased off once the deadline had passed.

chinese: 放鬆、減輕（壓力或努力程度）
example: The doctor advised him to ease up on work and take more time to rest each day.
pattern: S + advised + 人 + to ease up on + N
simple: You need to ease up — you've been working too hard.

chinese: 將…納入考量、把…計算在內
example: When planning your budget, make sure you factor in unexpected expenses like repairs.
pattern: Make sure S + factor(s) in + N + when + V-ing
simple: Did you factor in the cost of shipping?

example: The birds flew away the moment we stepped onto the grass, startled by the noise.
pattern: N + flew away the moment + S + V
simple: Time flies away when you're having fun.

chinese: 飛往（某地）出發；派人搭機前往
example: The company flew out its top engineers to fix the technical problems at the overseas factory.
pattern: S + flew out + 人 + to + V + N
simple: She flew out to Tokyo for a week-long conference.

chinese: 聚焦於、將注意力集中在
example: The documentary focuses in on the daily struggles of families living in extreme poverty.
simple: Let's focus in on the key issues one by one.

chinese: 跟著做、一起進行；看著（教材）跟上
example: Please open your textbooks to page twenty and follow along as I read the passage aloud.
pattern: Follow along as + S + V + O
simple: It's easy to follow along once you understand the pattern.

example: She decided to go in for the national essay competition after her teacher encouraged her.
pattern: S + decided to go in for + N + after + S + V
simple: Do you go in for outdoor sports or indoor activities?

example: Going through difficult experiences can harden you up and prepare you for future challenges.
pattern: V-ing + can harden + 人 + up and + V
simple: You need to harden up if you want to survive in this industry.

example: Please inform us of any changes to your schedule at least two days in advance.
pattern: Please inform + 人 + of + N + at least + 時間 + in advance
simple: We were not informed of the decision until it was too late.

chinese: 融入、整合到（某個群體或系統）
example: It took the new employee several months to fully integrate into the company culture.
pattern: It took + 人 + 時間 + to integrate into + N
simple: Technology has been integrated into every part of our daily lives.

chinese: 閒躺著、無所事事；（東西）散亂放置
example: Don't leave your valuables lying around in public places where anyone can take them.
pattern: Don't leave + N + lying around in + 地方
simple: He spent the whole day lying around doing nothing.

example: You need to log in with your student ID and password to access the online learning platform.
pattern: S + need(s) to log in with + N + to + V
simple: Please log in before you start the test.

example: Always remember to log out of your account when using a shared or public computer.
pattern: Always remember to log out of + N + when + V-ing
simple: Did you log out before you left the office?

chinese: 四處張望；到處看看、參觀
example: We arrived early at the museum and had plenty of time to look around every exhibit.
pattern: S + had + N + to look around + N
simple: Feel free to look around while I get the paperwork ready.

example: If you don't apply for the scholarship now, you may lose out on a great opportunity.
pattern: If + S + V, S + may lose out on + N
simple: Act quickly or you'll lose out on the early-bird discount.

example: We arranged to meet up for coffee after work to catch up on everything we had missed.
pattern: S + arranged to meet up + 時間/地點 + to + V
simple: Do you want to meet up this weekend?

example: The small regional airline merged with a larger carrier to expand its international routes.
pattern: S + merged with + N + to + V
simple: The two departments will merge with each other next quarter.

chinese: 展開、打開；（道路或空間）變寬闊
example: The narrow path opened out into a wide valley filled with wildflowers and tall trees.
simple: The map opens out to show the entire region.

example: Could you please pass along this message to the rest of the team before tomorrow's meeting?
pattern: Could you pass along + N + to + 人 + before + N?
simple: Please pass along my regards to your family.

chinese: 仔細挑選；逐一檢查（挑出最好的）
example: Shoppers at the market picked over the fruit carefully before deciding what to buy.
pattern: S + picked over + N + before + V-ing
simple: The critics picked over every detail of the new policy.

chinese: 將…放在；對…施加（責任、壓力）
example: Too much pressure placed on children at an early age can affect their long-term wellbeing.
pattern: N + placed on + 人 + can + V + N
simple: Please place your order on the counter.

example: It is wise to plan for the future while you are still young and full of energy.
pattern: It is + adj + to plan for + N + while + S + V
simple: Always plan for unexpected emergencies.

chinese: 強調、誇大；（機器）出問題；（小孩）搗蛋
example: The media tends to play up negative stories because they attract more attention from the public.
pattern: S + tends to play up + N + because + S + V
simple: My knee has been playing up since the marathon.

chinese: 贈送（某人某物）；使（某人）面臨（問題或挑戰）
example: The retiring principal was presented with a bouquet of flowers and a heartfelt card.
simple: This situation presents us with a unique opportunity.

example: After receiving approval from the board, the team was ready to proceed with the expansion plan.
pattern: After + V-ing, S + was ready to proceed with + N
simple: We will proceed with the meeting as planned.

example: He worked two jobs in order to provide for his family after losing his main source of income.
pattern: S + V + in order to provide for + N
simple: Parents have a duty to provide for their children.

example: She reached for her phone the moment she heard the notification sound go off.
pattern: S + reached for + N + the moment + S + V
simple: Always reach for your goals no matter how difficult they seem.

chinese: 使降低至；使淪落為；（使）感動落淚
example: The powerful speech reduced many members of the audience to tears within just a few minutes.
pattern: N + reduced + 人 + to + N + within + 時間
simple: The factory fire was reduced to ashes overnight.

example: Final responsibility for the project will remain with the project manager until completion.
pattern: N + will remain with + 人 + until + N
simple: The children will remain with their grandmother during the holidays.

chinese: 轉換（頻道、系統）；改換（到另一方）
example: Many businesses have switched over to cloud-based systems to improve efficiency and cut costs.
pattern: S + have switched over to + N + to + V
simple: Can you switch over to the other channel?

example: She tossed out all the old magazines that had been piling up in the corner for years.
pattern: S + tossed out + N + that had been + V-ing
simple: The committee tossed out the proposal without discussion.

example: The climate varies greatly from region to region, making it hard to predict the weather.
pattern: N + varies greatly from + N + to + N
simple: Results can vary from person to person.

example: The politician was accused of accepting bribes from several large construction companies.
pattern: S + was accused of + V-ing + N
simple: He was accused of cheating on the exam.

example: Don't forget to add in the service charge when you calculate the total cost of the meal.
pattern: Don't forget to add in + N + when + S + V
simple: She added in a few extra examples to make it clearer.

example: Many health problems can arise from a lack of sleep over a long period of time.
pattern: N + can arise from + N + over + 時間
simple: Conflicts often arise from misunderstandings between people.

chinese: 將…歸因於、認為…是由…所造成
example: Doctors attribute the rise in childhood obesity largely to poor diet and lack of exercise.
pattern: S + attribute(s) + N + largely to + N
simple: Her success is attributed to years of hard work and dedication.

chinese: 倒車進入；重新捲入（困境）
example: He carefully backed the car into the narrow parking space without hitting the walls.
pattern: S + backed + N + into + N + without + V-ing
simple: Don't back yourself into a corner during the negotiation.

chinese: 預料到、料想到（常用於否定）；議價
example: The project turned out to be far more complex than anyone had bargained for.
pattern: N + turned out to be far more + adj + than + S + had bargained for
simple: I got more than I bargained for when I took this job.

example: Students should be aware of the risks of sharing personal information on social media.
pattern: S + should be aware of + N/V-ing
simple: Are you aware of the changes to the schedule?

example: Many young graduates are faced with the challenge of finding stable employment in a tough market.
pattern: S + are faced with + N + of + V-ing
simple: When faced with failure, don't give up.

example: She has always been keen on photography and spends her weekends exploring the city with her camera.
pattern: S + has always been keen on + N + and + V
simple: He's not very keen on the idea of moving abroad.

example: This lightweight backpack is made of recycled plastic bottles, making it both eco-friendly and durable.
pattern: N + is made of + N, making it + adj + and + adj
simple: What is this table made of? It looks like solid oak.

example: After years of commuting two hours each day, he was tired of wasting so much time on the road.
pattern: After + 時間 + of + V-ing, S + was tired of + V-ing
simple: I'm tired of hearing the same excuses every time.

chinese: 精疲力竭；（物品）磨損破舊
example: By the end of the long hike, every member of the group was completely worn out.
pattern: By the end of + N, S + was completely worn out
simple: These old shoes are worn out — I need a new pair.

chinese: 突然出現、順道闖入；（風）吹入
example: An old friend blew in unexpectedly last night and we ended up talking until midnight.
pattern: S + blew in + 時間 + and S + ended up + V-ing
simple: A cold wind blew in from the north overnight.

chinese: （液體）沸騰溢出；（情緒）爆發失控
example: The soup boiled over while she was distracted by a phone call and made a mess of the stove.
pattern: N + boiled over while + S + V
simple: Tensions finally boiled over at the end of the heated debate.

example: Could you book me in for a dental check-up sometime next week if there is availability?
pattern: Could you book + 人 + in for + N + if + S + V?
simple: I need to book in for a service at the garage.

chinese: 隨意翻閱、瀏覽（書籍或網頁）
example: She spent an hour browsing through travel magazines before deciding on a destination.
pattern: S + spent + 時間 + browsing through + N + before + V-ing
simple: I like to browse through bookshops on weekends.

example: The airline bumped us up to business class because the economy section was overbooked.
pattern: S + bumped + 人 + up to + N + because + S + V
simple: They bumped up the price without any warning.

chinese: 呼籲、要求；正式邀請（某人發言或行動）
example: The chairperson called upon all members to submit their proposals before the end of the month.
pattern: S + called upon + 人 + to + V + N + before + N
simple: We are called upon to help those in need.

example: Please change into your gym clothes before the physical education class begins.
pattern: Please change into + N + before + N + V
simple: The caterpillar changes into a butterfly over time.

chinese: 對…發表評論、就…表達意見
example: The professor asked each student to comment on the strengths and weaknesses of the essay.
pattern: S + asked + 人 + to comment on + N
simple: The minister refused to comment on the allegations.

example: When you compare this year's results with last year's, the improvement is clearly significant.
pattern: When S + compare(s) + N + with + N, + N + be + clearly + adj
simple: How does this model compare with the previous version?

example: Small local businesses often struggle to compete with large international corporations on price.
pattern: S + struggle(s) to compete with + N + on + N
simple: It's hard to compete with someone who has so much more experience.

chinese: 與…建立聯繫；與…產生共鳴
example: A good teacher knows how to connect with students and make learning feel relevant to their lives.
pattern: S + knows how to connect with + N + and + V
simple: I really connected with the main character in that novel.

chinese: 轉換為、改信（某宗教或信念）
example: The old factory was converted to a modern art gallery that attracts thousands of visitors each year.
pattern: N + was converted to + N + that + V
simple: She converted to vegetarianism after watching a documentary.

example: Whether the outdoor event is held will depend upon the weather forecast for that day.
pattern: Whether + S + V + will depend upon + N
simple: You can depend upon her to keep her word.

chinese: 區分、辨別（A 與 B 的不同）
example: It can be difficult to distinguish genuine leather from high-quality synthetic materials.
pattern: It can be + adj + to distinguish + N + from + N
simple: Can you distinguish a crow from a raven?

chinese: 拉開距離；吸引離開；逐漸退開
example: The leading cyclist drew away from the rest of the group in the final stretch of the race.
pattern: S + drew away from + N + in + N
simple: She slowly drew away from her old social circle.

example: As a child, he used to dream of becoming an astronaut and exploring outer space.
pattern: S + used to dream of + V-ing + and + V-ing
simple: She dreams of opening her own bakery one day.

example: After graduation, many close friends drift apart as they move to different cities for work.
pattern: S + drift apart as + S + V
simple: We drifted apart after she moved abroad.

chinese: 開車進入；強迫陷入（困境）
example: The sudden economic crisis drove many small businesses into debt and eventually bankruptcy.
simple: Be careful not to drive into the oncoming lane.

example: Temperatures dropped down sharply overnight, catching many people off guard.
pattern: N + dropped down + adv, catching + 人 + off guard
simple: Just drop down here and do twenty push-ups.

chinese: 逐漸適應、緩緩進入（狀態）
example: It is important to ease into a new exercise routine gradually rather than pushing too hard at first.
pattern: It is + adj + to ease into + N + rather than + V-ing
simple: She eased into conversation with the new colleagues slowly.

chinese: 擴展至（新市場或新領域）
example: The tech company plans to expand into the Southeast Asian market within the next two years.
pattern: S + plans to expand into + N + within + 時間
simple: The brand expanded into clothing after its initial success in footwear.

example: Parents often expect too much from their children without understanding their individual abilities.
pattern: S + expect(s) too much from + N + without + V-ing
simple: What do you expect from this course?

example: The chef loves to experiment with unusual ingredients to create dishes that surprise his guests.
pattern: S + loves to experiment with + N + to + V
simple: Young artists should experiment with different styles.

example: He tried to explain away his long absence with a series of unconvincing excuses.
pattern: S + tried to explain away + N + with + N
simple: You can't explain away the evidence with a simple apology.

chinese: 將注意力集中於、專注於
example: The research team decided to focus upon the long-term effects of screen time on children.
pattern: S + decided to focus upon + N + of + N
simple: The new policy focuses upon reducing carbon emissions.

chinese: 由…得出結論；是…的必然結果
example: It follows from the data that a stricter policy on pollution is urgently needed.
pattern: It follows from + N + that + S + V
simple: This argument follows logically from the evidence presented.

chinese: 不贊成、對…皺眉頭（表示不認可）
example: Using a mobile phone during a meeting is generally frowned upon in professional settings.
pattern: V-ing + is generally frowned upon in + N
simple: Littering in public is frowned upon in most countries.

chinese: 發出（聲音、氣味、光）；發表（意見）
example: The old fireplace gave forth a warm glow that filled the entire room with a cosy feeling.
pattern: N + gave forth + N + that + V
simple: The roses gave forth a sweet fragrance in the morning air.

example: True kindness goes beyond simply following rules — it comes from genuine care for others.
pattern: N + goes beyond + V-ing — it comes from + N
simple: His ambitions go beyond what most people dare to dream.

chinese: 手牽手；相互關聯、相輔相成
example: Economic development and environmental protection must go hand in hand if we want a sustainable future.
pattern: N + and + N + must go hand in hand if + S + V
simple: The two children walked hand in hand across the playground.

example: While exploring the old town, she happened upon a tiny antique shop full of fascinating items.

simple: I happened upon an old letter while cleaning the attic.

example: Don't hesitate over small decisions — learn to trust your instincts and move forward.
pattern: Don't hesitate over + N — learn to + V
simple: She hesitated over whether to accept the job offer.

example: After years of hardship, the community hungers for stability, safety, and a better quality of life.
pattern: After + 時間 + of + N, S + hungers for + N

example: It is unfair to impose your personal beliefs on others who do not share the same views.
pattern: It is + adj + to impose + N + on + 人 + who + V
simple: I don't want to impose on your hospitality.

example: The number of international students enrolled at the university increased by thirty percent last year.
pattern: N + increased by + 數量 + 時間
simple: Our sales have increased by fifty percent this quarter.

example: After a long and tiring day, she lay back on the sofa and closed her eyes to rest.
pattern: After + N, S + lay back on + N + and + V
simple: Just lie back and let the music wash over you.

example: Scientists have found strong evidence linking prolonged stress to a higher risk of heart disease.
pattern: S + found evidence linking + N + to + N
simple: This button links to the homepage of the website.

example: The shy new student gradually merged into the class and made several close friends over time.
pattern: S + gradually merged into + N + and + V + over time
simple: The two lanes merge into one further ahead.

chinese: 加入攪拌、混入；融入（群體）
example: Mix in the flour slowly until the batter is smooth and there are no lumps remaining.

chinese: 繼續前進；（請人）移動、散開
example: The police officer asked the crowd to move along and not block the entrance to the building.
pattern: S + asked + 人 + to move along and not + V
simple: Let's move along — we still have a lot to cover today.

example: Many countries are now moving towards renewable energy to reduce their dependence on fossil fuels.

chinese: 冒充、假冒；（事件）順利進行

chinese: 存入、繳款至（帳戶或基金）

pattern: S + are required to pay into + N + every + 時間
simple: She pays into a savings account every month.

example: The new regulations will be phased in gradually over the next three years to give businesses time to adapt.
pattern: N + will be phased in + adv + over + 時間 + to + V
simple: The new system will be phased in starting next quarter.

chinese: 逐一批評、詳細分析（找出缺陷）
example: The professor picked apart every argument in the student's essay during the feedback session.
pattern: S + picked apart + N + during + N

chinese: 從…中獲益、藉由…得到好處

pattern: S + learns to profit by + N + rather than + V-ing

pattern: S + provides + 人 + with + N + at the start of + N

example: After a painful breakup, it took her months to put the difficult experience behind her.

chinese: 再次查閱、回頭參考；退回（給原單位）

pattern: If + S + V, please refer back to + N

example: Taking time to reflect upon your experiences helps you grow as a person and avoid past mistakes.
pattern: V-ing + to reflect upon + N + helps + 人 + V

chinese: 重新將注意力集中在、重新聚焦於
example: After a short break, the team refocused on the main objective and worked with renewed energy.
pattern: After + N, S + refocused on + N + and + V
simple: Let's refocus on what really matters here.

example: The suspect was ordered to remain on the property and not leave until further notice.
pattern: S + was ordered to remain on + N + until + N
simple: Please remain on the line while I transfer your call.

example: The journalist was sent overseas to report on the ongoing conflict and its impact on civilians.
pattern: S + was sent + 地點 + to report on + N
simple: Each student must report on a different country's culture.

chinese: 藉由…產生、透過…所導致的結果
example: The most lasting changes in society result through the dedicated efforts of ordinary people over time.
pattern: N + result(s) through + N + over time
simple: Progress results through consistent daily effort.

example: Due to budget cuts, the company was forced to scale back its plans for the new headquarters.
pattern: Due to + N, S + was forced to scale back + N

example: The startup is ready to scale up its production now that demand for its product has grown significantly.
pattern: S + is ready to scale up + N + now that + S + V

example: She searched through every drawer in the room but could not find the missing document.
pattern: S + searched through + N + but + S + could not + V
simple: I searched through my bag but couldn't find my keys.

example: The company is shifting from traditional retail to online sales in response to changing consumer habits.
pattern: S + is shifting from + N + to + N + in response to + N

example: Don't settle for average when you have the talent and ability to shoot for the top.
pattern: Don't settle for + N + when + S + can shoot for + N

example: It takes great courage to speak against injustice, especially when others are unwilling to do so.
pattern: It takes + N + to speak against + N, especially when + S + V
simple: She was the only one who dared to speak against the policy.

example: It is too early to speculate about the cause of the accident before a full investigation is completed.
pattern: It is too early to speculate about + N + before + S + V
simple: The media began to speculate about who would win the election.

example: Every citizen has a duty to stand against corruption and demand accountability from those in power.
pattern: S + has a duty to stand against + N + and + V
simple: They stood against the proposed changes to the law.

example: Parents should steer their children away from content that promotes violence or harmful behaviour.
pattern: S + should steer + 人 + away from + N + that + V

example: Great athletes constantly strive for improvement, never allowing themselves to become complacent.
pattern: S + constantly strive(s) for + N, never + V-ing

example: Skilled translators can switch between languages effortlessly without losing the original meaning.
pattern: S + can switch between + N + without + V-ing
simple: She switches between working from home and going to the office.

example: People who grew up in cities tend to feel uncomfortable when they are surrounded by silence.
pattern: S + who + V + tend(s) to + V + when + S + V
simple: Prices tend to rise during the holiday season.

example: The sudden resignation of the CEO threw the entire company into a state of confusion and uncertainty.
simple: She threw herself into the new project with great enthusiasm.

example: I understand from your message that the meeting has been moved to Thursday afternoon.
pattern: S + understand(s) from + N + that + S + V
simple: I understand from the report that sales have declined.

example: The two rival parties agreed to unite with each other in order to defeat their common opponent.
pattern: S + agreed to unite with + N + in order to + V
simple: Communities must unite with one another to overcome shared challenges.

example: A majority of council members voted against the proposed development project in the historic district.
pattern: A majority of + N + voted against + N
simple: Citizens voted against the new tax proposal.

example: I don't want to wait around all afternoon for a reply that may never come.
pattern: S + don't want to wait around + 時間 + for + N
simple: Stop waiting around and take action.

example: A lack of sleep and poor nutrition will work against your efforts to stay healthy and focused.

example: All members of the team are working towards the same goal of launching the app before the end of the year.

chinese: 融入、被吸收進（群體或系統）

example: The contractor will add a small storage room onto the back of the house during the renovation.
pattern: S + will add + N + onto + N + during + N

example: Both parties finally agreed upon a fair settlement after weeks of intense negotiation.

example: The company's new strategy aligns with its long-term vision of becoming a global leader in clean energy.

example: Children under the age of twelve are not allowed into the cinema without a parent or guardian.

chinese: 出現在…面前；（正式）出席（法庭或機構）
example: The company director was required to appear before the court to answer charges of financial fraud.

example: Apply the sunscreen evenly onto all exposed areas of skin at least twenty minutes before going outside.
pattern: Apply + N + evenly onto + N + before + V-ing

example: Most conflicts in the workplace arise out of poor communication and unspoken expectations.
pattern: N + arise(s) out of + N + and + N

example: The receptionist asked the applicants into the waiting room and offered them a glass of water.

example: The children were asked to assemble into groups of four before the outdoor activity began.
pattern: S + were asked to assemble into + N + before + N + V

chinese: 與…交往；將…與…聯想在一起

chinese: 依靠、退回到；（建築）背向、緊臨

example: All decisions made in this company must be based on solid evidence rather than personal opinions.

example: Once you are done with the assignment, please submit it through the online portal before midnight.

example: She was completely fed up with being overlooked for promotion despite her outstanding performance.

example: My grandfather has always been fond of classical music and plays the piano every morning.

example: The senior editor is in charge of reviewing all articles before they are published on the website.

example: Most of the committee members were in favor of extending the deadline by one additional week.
pattern: S + were in favor of + V-ing + N + by + 數量

example: We are completely out of coffee this morning, so could you pick some up on your way in?

example: The charity is short of volunteers this weekend and is urgently looking for people to help out.

example: The manager began to bear down on the team as the product launch deadline grew closer.

chinese: 拐彎抹角、迴避重點（常用 beat around the bush）

chinese: 快速通過；超越（目標或限制）

example: After years of success in food delivery, the company decided to branch into grocery retail.
pattern: After + 時間 + of + N, S + decided to branch into + N
simple: The clothing brand branched into home furnishings last year.

chinese: 傳達（訊息或想法）；帶過來
example: The documentary uses personal stories to bring across the devastating impact of poverty on families.

example: Every small habit you develop today is building towards the person you will become in the future.

example: She called across the crowded hall to get her friend's attention before the ceremony started.

example: The familiar melody carried her back to her childhood summers spent by the sea.

chinese: 理解、領悟；（趨勢）流行起來

example: Please check your answers against the answer key at the back of the book before submitting.

example: It was difficult to choose between the two job offers, as both came with excellent benefits.

example: You can claim back the tax you paid on business expenses at the end of the financial year.

example: Make sure the gate closes behind you every time you enter, as the dog must not escape.

example: Regular exercise, when combined with a balanced diet, can significantly reduce the risk of heart disease.

example: Despite starting with fewer resources, the smaller team came ahead in the final ranking.

example: The reform team came against strong resistance from those who benefited from the old system.

example: The judge was asked to comment upon the strengths of each performance before announcing the winner.
pattern: S + was asked to comment upon + N + before + V-ing

example: It is essential to communicate clearly with your team members to avoid misunderstandings.
pattern: It is + adj + to communicate + adv + with + N + to + V

example: Our school will compete against three other schools in the regional science competition next month.
pattern: S + will compete against + N + in + N + 時間

example: All businesses operating in this country are required by law to comply with local environmental regulations.
pattern: S + are required by law to comply with + N
simple: Failure to comply with safety rules may result in serious penalties.

example: Make sure your device is connected to a secure Wi-Fi network before downloading any sensitive files.
pattern: Make sure + N + be connected to + N + before + V-ing

chinese: 考慮（某人或某物）擔任或用於
example: Her outstanding performance this year means she will be seriously considered for a promotion.
pattern: N + means + S + will be considered for + N

example: The driver lost control on the icy road and crashed into the barrier on the side of the highway.

example: The artist created a stunning sculpture from pieces of scrap metal she found at a local junkyard.

chinese: 切除、割掉；剪掉多餘部分

chinese: 宣布支持、正式表態贊成

example: The number of traffic accidents in the city decreased by twenty percent after the new speed limits were introduced.

example: Any further delay in making a decision could result in the company losing the contract entirely.

chinese: 啟程、出發（某日或某旅程）

pattern: S + is scheduled to depart on + 日期 + for + N

example: Students who participate actively in class discussions derive far greater benefit from their education.
pattern: S + who + V + derive far greater benefit from + N

example: It is difficult to determine from the available data whether the treatment is truly effective.

example: The pilot was instructed never to deviate from the approved flight path under any circumstances.
pattern: S + was instructed never to deviate from + N + under any circumstances
simple: The report deviates significantly from the original brief.

example: The journalist spent weeks digging around for evidence of corruption within the local government.

example: The counsellor directed the student towards resources that could help improve his study skills.

chinese: 在（三者或以上）之間分配

chinese: 共用、疊用；彎身（大笑或疼痛）

example: The black limousine drew beside the kerb and a well-dressed woman stepped out calmly.

chinese: 不知不覺進入（某狀態）；漂入

example: While sorting through old boxes, he dropped upon a collection of rare stamps from the 1940s.

example: She earns a comfortable income from her freelance writing work for several online publications.

example: The country is slowly edging towards an agreement on trade that could benefit millions of people.

example: The professor asked the student to elaborate upon her research findings in greater detail during the presentation.

chinese: 開始、踏上（新計畫或旅程）

chinese: 進入、走進（某空間或狀態）

chinese: 鼓勵（某人）朝向、引導走向

chinese: 逃入；沉浸於（某事物）以逃避現實

chinese: 從…演變而來、由…發展而成
