pattern: N + has evolved from + N + into + N
simple: Our partnership evolved from a chance meeting into a successful business.

example: She has always excelled at languages and was fluent in four of them before graduating from university.
pattern: S + has always excelled at + N + and + V
simple: He excels at problem-solving under pressure.

example: The two schools regularly exchange students with each other to promote cultural understanding and friendship.
pattern: S + regularly exchange(s) + N + with + N + to + V
simple: They exchanged phone numbers with each other at the end of the conference.

example: The researcher was invited to expand upon her findings at the international conference next month.
pattern: S + was invited to expand upon + N + at + N
simple: Could you expand upon what you said earlier about the budget?

example: As the years passed, the painful memories slowly faded into the background of her daily life.
pattern: N + slowly faded into + N + as + S + V
simple: The old hero faded into obscurity after retiring from public life.

example: If you fall behind on your assignments, it becomes very difficult to catch up before the exam.
pattern: If + S + fall behind on + N, it becomes + adj + to + V
simple: He fell behind on his rent payments after losing his job.

chinese: 對…有某種感受、感情傾向
example: It took her a long time to understand what she truly felt towards him after all they had been through.
pattern: It took + 人 + 時間 + to understand what S + felt towards + 人
simple: I'm not sure how I feel towards the idea of moving abroad.

example: She figures among the most influential scientists of her generation, having published over a hundred papers.
pattern: S + figures among + N, having + p.p.
simple: This novel figures among the greatest works of the twentieth century.

chinese: 在…之中發現、於…之間找到
example: You can find among his early sketches several ideas that later became his most celebrated paintings.
pattern: S + can find among + N + N + that + V
simple: You will find among the guests several well-known authors.

example: The flexible working arrangement allows employees to fit their schedules around family responsibilities.
pattern: N + allows + 人 + to fit + N + around + N
simple: Can you fit the meeting around my afternoon appointments?

example: After the opening ceremony, the guests followed the host onto the stage for a group photograph.
pattern: After + N, S + followed + 人 + onto + N + for + N
simple: Follow onto the next page for further instructions.

chinese: 強迫進入；迫使（某人）做某事
example: No one should be forced into making a major life decision when they are under extreme pressure.
pattern: S + should not be forced into + V-ing + when + S + V
simple: The company was forced into bankruptcy by mounting debts.

example: Despite the initial setbacks, the development team forged ahead with their plan and met the deadline.
pattern: Despite + N, S + forged ahead with + N + and + V
simple: She decided to forge ahead with her plans despite the criticism.

example: The peace negotiations foundered on the issue of territorial boundaries, leaving both sides frustrated.
pattern: N + foundered on + N, leaving + 人 + adj
simple: The project foundered on a lack of funding at the final stage.

example: There is much to be gained from listening carefully to people whose experiences differ from your own.
pattern: There is much to be gained from + V-ing + N
simple: What do you hope to gain from this experience?

chinese: 找到時間做、終於著手（做某事）
example: I've been meaning to call her for weeks but I just haven't gotten around to it yet.
pattern: S + have been meaning to + V + but S + haven't gotten around to it yet
simple: I'll get around to fixing the leak when I have a free afternoon.

chinese: 重回（正軌）；重新上（交通工具）
example: After a difficult few months, she worked hard to get back on track with her studies.
pattern: After + N, S + worked hard to get back on + N
simple: It took time to get back on his feet after the surgery.

example: Neither side was willing to give ground during the negotiations, causing the talks to stall.
pattern: Neither + N + was willing to give ground, causing + N + to + V
simple: The government finally gave ground on the tax reform proposal.

chinese: 升級成為；逐漸進入（更高層次）
example: What started as a small hobby gradually graduated into a full-time business with fifteen employees.
pattern: What started as + N + gradually graduated into + N
simple: She graduated into a senior management role after five years.

chinese: 急忙抓取；拼命抓住（機會或希望）
example: Desperate for a solution, the committee began grasping at any idea that might solve the funding crisis.
pattern: Desperate for + N, S + began grasping at + N + that + V
simple: Don't just grasp at the first offer you receive.

chinese: 超越成長；超出（原有的限制或框架）
example: The startup has grown beyond its original vision and now operates in more than thirty countries.
pattern: N + has grown beyond + N + and now + V
simple: She has grown beyond the limitations she once placed on herself.

example: With guidance and support, young people can grow towards becoming responsible and confident adults.
pattern: With + N, S + can grow towards + V-ing
simple: The two countries are slowly growing towards a mutual understanding.

chinese: 引導完成、帶領通過（過程或困難）
example: The experienced counsellor guided the family through the difficult process of grief and recovery.
simple: Let me guide you through the steps of the application process.

example: She smiled warmly and handed the bouquet towards the guest of honour at the front of the room.
pattern: S + V + and handed + N + towards + 人 + at + 地點
simple: He handed the document towards her across the table.

chinese: 朝…前進；走向（某方向或結果）
example: After lunch, the group headed towards the old lighthouse at the far end of the coastal path.
pattern: After + N, S + headed towards + N + at + N
simple: The economy seems to be heading towards a recovery.

chinese: 懷抱希望朝向、期望達到
example: Despite the many setbacks, the community continues to hope towards a future of peace and stability.
pattern: Despite + N, S + continues to hope towards + N
simple: We hope towards a day when everyone has access to clean water.

example: The school includes community service among the requirements for graduation each year.
simple: She is included among the top ten finalists for the award.

example: Global temperatures are steadily increasing towards levels that scientists consider extremely dangerous.
pattern: N + are steadily increasing towards + N + that + S + V
simple: Support for the proposal is increasing towards a majority.

example: Several parents called the school to inquire about the new policy on mobile phone use in class.
pattern: S + called + 地點 + to inquire about + N
simple: I'd like to inquire about the availability of rooms next weekend.

example: The client insists upon receiving a detailed written report before approving any further payments.
pattern: S + insists upon + V-ing + before + V-ing
simple: She insists upon punctuality at every team meeting.

example: Most of the disputes in the office involve around miscommunication between departments.
pattern: Most of + N + involve around + N + between + N
simple: The course involves around three hours of study per week.

example: Keep along the river path for about two kilometres and you will reach the old stone bridge.
pattern: Keep along + N + for + 距離 + and + S + will + V
simple: Keep along the fence until you reach the main gate.

example: It is essential to keep spending within the approved budget to avoid running into financial difficulties.
pattern: It is + adj + to keep + N + within + N + to avoid + V-ing
simple: Please keep your comments within the time limit of two minutes.

example: After hours of brainstorming, the creative team finally landed upon a concept that excited everyone.
pattern: After + 時間 + of + N, S + finally landed upon + N + that + V
simple: The butterfly landed upon the flower and stayed perfectly still.

chinese: 猛烈攻擊、突然爆發（言語或行為）
example: When he is under extreme stress, he tends to lash out at those closest to him without meaning to.
pattern: When + S + V, S + tends to lash out at + 人 + without + V-ing
simple: She lashed out at the unfair criticism in front of the whole team.

chinese: 從…出發啟動；以…為基礎展開
example: The charity's annual campaign is launched from its headquarters and quickly spreads across the country.
pattern: N + is launched from + N + and + V
simple: The rocket was launched from the desert test facility at dawn.

example: During difficult times, it is natural to lean upon the people who know and care about you most.
pattern: It is + adj + to lean upon + 人 + who + V
simple: He leaned upon his years of experience to guide the new team.

chinese: 在…之間留下（空間或時間）
example: When scheduling appointments, always leave enough time between each one to avoid running late.
pattern: Always leave + N + between + N + to avoid + V-ing
simple: Leave some space between the furniture and the wall.

example: The responsibility for ensuring student safety lies upon every member of the teaching staff.
pattern: The responsibility for + V-ing + lies upon + 人
simple: A heavy burden lies upon those who lead others through crisis.

example: The crowd lifted the champion upon their shoulders and cheered loudly as he held up the trophy.
pattern: S + lifted + 人 + upon + N + and + V + as + S + V
simple: She lifted the child upon the counter so he could see better.

chinese: 超越表面去看；著眼於更遠的事物
example: A great leader knows how to look beyond short-term difficulties and focus on long-term goals.
pattern: S + knows how to look beyond + N + and + V
simple: Try to look beyond appearances and judge people by their character.

example: Many students look upon their time at university as the most transformative period of their lives.
pattern: S + look(s) upon + N + as + N
simple: She looks upon every failure as a lesson worth learning.

example: Taking care of your mental health matters just as much to your overall wellbeing as physical fitness.
pattern: V-ing + matters just as much to + N + as + N
simple: Your opinion matters to us — please share your feedback.

chinese: 與…相比較衡量；以…為標準評估
example: Student progress should not always be measured against standardised tests but also against personal growth.
pattern: N + should not be measured against + N + but also + N
simple: Each employee's performance is measured against clear targets.

chinese: 思念…的某方面；懷念…的點點滴滴
example: What she misses most about living abroad is the sense of independence and daily adventure.
pattern: What S + miss(es) most about + V-ing + is + N
simple: What do you miss most about your hometown?

example: She moves effortlessly between her roles as a mother, a teacher, and a community leader.
pattern: S + moves between + N, N, and + N
simple: He moves between London and Tokyo several times a year for work.

chinese: 朝向…開放；面向、開口朝向
example: The large glass doors open towards the garden, flooding the living room with natural light.
pattern: N + open(s) towards + N, flooding + N + with + N
simple: Try to stay open towards new ideas even when they challenge your beliefs.

chinese: 在…範圍內運作、在…框架下執行
example: All staff members are expected to operate within the ethical guidelines set by the organisation.
pattern: S + are expected to operate within + N + set by + N
simple: The charity must operate within a strict legal framework.

example: Many of the spices used in local cuisine originate from tropical regions in Southeast Asia.
simple: This tradition originates from a harvest festival celebrated centuries ago.

example: It is never easy to part from close friends when life takes you in completely different directions.
pattern: It is never + adj + to part from + N + when + S + V
simple: She wept as she parted from her family at the airport.

chinese: 在…之間傳遞；在…之間發生
example: A knowing glance passed between the two old friends as they listened to the familiar story.
pattern: N + passed between + N + as + S + V
simple: Very few words passed between them during the long drive home.

example: The forensic team spent the entire morning picking through the wreckage for clues about the cause.
pattern: S + spent + 時間 + picking through + N + for + N
simple: She picked through the pile of clothes looking for something to wear.

chinese: 將…列於其中；歸類於…之列
example: Critics have placed this novel among the finest works of contemporary fiction published this decade.
pattern: S + have placed + N + among + N + of + N
simple: Her achievement places her among the greatest athletes of all time.

example: The entire holiday itinerary was planned around the children's schedules and energy levels.
pattern: N + was planned around + N + and + N
simple: We plan our week around the children's school timetable.

chinese: 指向更遠處；暗示更深的含義
example: The author's choice of words points beyond the surface story to deeper themes of loss and redemption.
pattern: N + points beyond + N + to + N
simple: Her question points beyond the immediate problem to something more fundamental.

example: Coastal communities must prepare against rising sea levels by building stronger flood defences.
pattern: S + must prepare against + N + by + V-ing
simple: We need to prepare against the possibility of a power outage.

chinese: 向…施壓；強行給予；縈繞於心
example: The weight of the decision pressed heavily upon him as he sat alone in the quiet office.
pattern: N + pressed heavily upon + 人 + as + S + V
simple: She pressed upon him the importance of making the right choice.

example: All great achievements proceed from a combination of clear vision, hard work, and unwavering patience.
pattern: N + proceed(s) from + N, N, and + N

chinese: 防止、防護（某威脅或危害）

pattern: V-ing + helps protect + N + against + N + caused by + N

chinese: 把…推到一旁；忽視、壓抑（感受）
example: She pushed aside her personal feelings and focused entirely on completing the task professionally.
pattern: S + pushed aside + N + and + V + adv
simple: Don't push aside your doubts — they might be worth listening to.

example: The team leader encouraged everyone to push forward with the project despite the technical difficulties.
pattern: S + encouraged + 人 + to push forward with + N + despite + N
simple: We must push forward if we want to meet the deadline.

chinese: 強行通過；克服困難堅持到底
example: She pushed through the pain and exhaustion to finish the marathon and cross the finish line.
pattern: S + pushed through + N + and + N + to + V
simple: The legislation was pushed through parliament before the end of term.

example: The impact of her work reaches far beyond the scientific community and touches the lives of millions.
pattern: N + reaches far beyond + N + and + V
simple: We should set goals that reach beyond our comfort zone.

example: Every day she reaches towards her goal of becoming a published author by writing at least five hundred words.
pattern: S + reach(es) towards + N + by + V-ing
simple: The child reached towards the jar of cookies on the top shelf.

chinese: 逐步推理解決；理性分析完畢
example: Before making any decision, take the time to reason through all the possible outcomes carefully.
pattern: Take the time to reason through + N + before + V-ing
simple: She reasoned through the problem step by step until she found the answer.

example: The school received a generous donation from a former student who had gone on to become very successful.
pattern: S + received + N + from + 人 + who + V
simple: I received some very useful advice from my mentor last week.

example: After months of rehabilitation, the athlete fully recovered to his previous level of performance.
pattern: After + 時間 + of + N, S + fully recovered to + N
simple: The market recovered to its pre-crisis levels within two years.

example: Complex scientific theories should not always be reduced into oversimplified explanations for the public.
pattern: N + should not be reduced into + N
simple: The long report was reduced into a one-page summary for the board.

chinese: 回顧、反思過去；反射回來
example: When she reflects back on her early struggles, she feels grateful for every lesson they taught her.
pattern: When + S + reflect(s) back on + N, S + feel(s) + adj + for + N
simple: The mirror reflected the light back across the room.

example: Despite many distractions, the core of the discussion must remain about finding a practical solution.
pattern: Despite + N, N + must remain about + V-ing + N
simple: This policy debate must remain about what is best for the public.

example: What I remember most about that summer is the long walks we took along the quiet country roads.
pattern: What S + remember(s) most about + N + is + N
simple: Do you remember anything about where you left your keys?

chinese: 揭露關於…的事、透露有關…的資訊
example: What this data reveals about consumer behaviour has changed the way many companies approach marketing.
pattern: What + N + reveals about + N + has + V + N
simple: What does this study reveal about the link between sleep and memory?

chinese: 在…之間穿梭運行；奔走於…之間
example: A free shuttle bus runs between the hotel and the airport every thirty minutes throughout the day.
pattern: N + runs between + N + and + N + every + 時間
simple: She ran between the two offices all morning delivering documents.

example: They have been saving towards a deposit on their first home for the past three years.
pattern: S + have been saving towards + N + for the past + 時間
simple: I'm saving towards a trip to Japan next spring.

chinese: 尋求、追求（珍貴或難得之物）
example: True happiness is something that many people seek after their entire lives without ever truly finding it.
pattern: N + is something that S + seek after + 時間 + without + V-ing
simple: This rare mineral is highly sought after by collectors worldwide.

example: Guests are invited to select from a wide range of dishes prepared fresh by our award-winning chef.
pattern: S + are invited to select from + N + prepared by + N
simple: You can select from three different package options.

example: Public opinion has gradually shifted towards greater support for renewable energy policies.
pattern: N + has gradually shifted towards + N
simple: The team's strategy has shifted towards a more defensive approach.

example: A responsible leader must never shrink from making difficult decisions, even when they are unpopular.
pattern: S + must never shrink from + V-ing + N, even when + S + V
simple: Don't shrink from speaking the truth when it matters most.

chinese: 就…發言表態；對…議題表達立場
example: The panel was asked to speak towards the growing concerns about data privacy in modern technology.
pattern: S + was asked to speak towards + N + about + N
simple: She spoke towards the need for greater investment in public health.

chinese: 站在…上；堅持（原則或立場）
example: She stands firmly upon her principles and refuses to compromise no matter how great the pressure becomes.
pattern: S + stands upon + N + and + V + no matter how + adj + S + V

example: Every great journey starts from a single step taken with courage and a clear sense of direction.
pattern: N + starts from + N + taken with + N
simple: Let's start from the beginning and go through the plan again.

example: A few students were asked to stay behind after class to discuss their upcoming project presentations.
pattern: S + were asked to stay behind + 時間 + to + V
simple: I decided to stay behind while the others went on ahead.

chinese: 保持在…範圍內；留在…之內
example: All speakers are reminded to stay within their allotted time of ten minutes during the conference.
pattern: S + are reminded to stay within + N + of + 數量 + during + N

example: Good mentors steer their students towards opportunities that match their strengths and passions.
pattern: S + steer(s) + 人 + towards + N + that + V
simple: The therapist gently steered the conversation towards the real issue.

chinese: 跨越、超出（某界限或框架）
example: To grow as a person, you must be willing to step beyond your comfort zone and embrace uncertainty.
pattern: To + V, S + must be willing to step beyond + N + and + V
simple: She stepped beyond her usual role to help the struggling team.

example: Many households have switched to LED lighting to reduce their electricity bills and energy consumption.
pattern: S + have switched to + N + to + V
simple: Let's switch to a different topic before we run out of time.

example: Remember to take along a light jacket when you go hiking, as the mountain weather can change quickly.
pattern: Remember to take along + N + when + S + V, as + S + can + V
simple: She took her younger brother along to the concert.

example: Everyone in the village talks of the old lighthouse keeper and the mysterious stories surrounding him.
pattern: S + talks/talk of + 人 + and + N + surrounding + 人
simple: People have long talked of building a bridge across the bay.

example: She sat quietly by the window and thought upon everything that had happened during the long difficult year.
pattern: S + sat + adv + and thought upon + N + that + V
simple: Take a moment to think upon what truly matters to you in life.

example: The caravan slowly travelled towards the distant mountains as the golden sun began to set.
pattern: N + slowly travelled towards + N + as + S + V
simple: We travelled towards the coast, stopping at small towns along the way.

example: The outcome of the entire negotiation turns upon whether both sides can agree on the key financial terms.
pattern: N + turns upon + whether + S + can + V
simple: The frightened animal turned upon its pursuer without warning.

example: She volunteered for the community clean-up programme every weekend throughout the entire summer.
pattern: S + volunteered for + N + every + 時間 + throughout + N
simple: Would anyone like to volunteer for the role of team leader?

example: In the grand old hotel, dedicated staff wait upon guests from the moment they arrive until they depart.
pattern: S + wait upon + 人 + from the moment + S + V + until + S + V
simple: We are waiting upon the final approval before we can proceed.

chinese: 逐步說明、帶領了解；走過
example: The trainer walked the new employees through each step of the safety procedure before they started work.
pattern: S + walked + 人 + through + N + before + S + V
simple: Could you walk me through how the system works?

example: The speaker kept wandering from the main topic, making it difficult for the audience to follow.
pattern: S + kept wandering from + N, making it + adj + for + 人 + to + V
simple: Don't wander from the path or you might get lost in the forest.

example: The therapist worked upon the patient's anxiety gradually through a series of structured weekly sessions.
pattern: S + worked upon + N + gradually through + N
simple: She is working upon improving her public speaking skills.

example: You can account upon the support of your colleagues when times get tough in this organisation.
pattern: S + can account upon + N + when + S + V
simple: There are several factors we must account upon when planning the budget.

chinese: 繞過問題行事；在…周圍活動
example: The team found clever ways to act around the bureaucratic restrictions and still deliver results on time.
pattern: S + found ways to act around + N + and still + V
simple: Stop acting around the issue and address it directly.

chinese: 在…之前加入、在前面添加
example: Always add a brief introduction before the main content so readers know what to expect from the piece.
pattern: Always add + N + before + N + so + S + V
simple: Add a disclaimer before the terms and conditions section.

example: The company is adjusting its pricing strategy towards a more competitive and consumer-friendly model.
pattern: S + is adjusting + N + towards + N
simple: We need to adjust our approach towards a more collaborative style.

example: The rescue team advanced on the flooded village as soon as the storm began to ease off.
pattern: S + advanced on + N + as soon as + S + V
simple: The troops advanced on the enemy position at dawn.

example: The doctor strongly advised against eating processed foods and recommended a balanced natural diet instead.
pattern: S + strongly advised against + V-ing + and + V
simple: I would advise against making any hasty decisions right now.

example: The non-profit organisation has spent decades advocating for the rights of marginalised communities.
pattern: S + has spent + 時間 + advocating for + N
simple: She advocates for better mental health resources in schools.

example: The committee managed to agree around a set of core values that would guide the organisation forward.
pattern: S + managed to agree around + N + that + V
simple: Both teams agreed around a shared timeline for the project.

example: Regular physical exercise has been shown to aid in the prevention of many chronic health conditions.
pattern: N + has been shown to aid in + N + of + N
simple: Good nutrition aids in the recovery process after surgery.

example: The new education policy aims towards creating equal opportunities for students from all backgrounds.
pattern: N + aims towards + V-ing + N + for + 人
simple: Our campaign aims towards raising awareness of ocean pollution.

example: The security system alerts staff to any unusual activity detected inside or around the building.
pattern: N + alerts + 人 + to + N + detected + 地點
simple: Please alert us to any changes in your availability.

chinese: 聯合對抗、站在…的對立面
example: Several member states aligned against the proposed policy, forming a powerful bloc within the organisation.
pattern: S + aligned against + N, forming + N + within + N
simple: The opposition parties aligned against the ruling government's budget.

example: A significant portion of the annual budget has been allocated to improving the city's public transport network.
pattern: N + has been allocated to + V-ing + N
simple: Each team member has been allocated to a specific task.

chinese: 固定於、以…為依據；使扎根於
example: Strong family relationships anchor children to a sense of identity and belonging throughout their lives.
pattern: N + anchors + 人 + to + N + throughout + N
simple: The team's success was anchored to clear communication and mutual trust.

example: Every public official must be prepared to answer upon their decisions when called to account.
pattern: S + must be prepared to answer upon + N + when + V
simple: You will be asked to answer upon these choices at the review meeting.

example: The defendant's legal team plans to appeal against the verdict and seek a retrial in a higher court.
pattern: S + plans to appeal against + N + and + V
simple: She decided to appeal against the unfair dismissal from her job.

example: As the deadline approaches towards the end of the month, the team must work with increasing urgency.
pattern: As + N + approaches towards + N, S + must + V
simple: She approached towards the podium slowly and with great confidence.

example: Not all parents approve of the amount of time their children spend on video games and social media.
pattern: Not all + N + approve of + N + S + V
simple: Do you approve of the changes they made to the original design?

example: Several leading economists have argued strongly against the proposed tax increase, citing its impact on growth.
pattern: S + have argued against + N, citing + N
simple: Many residents argued against the plan to build a new motorway.

example: The two siblings argued over who should inherit the family home for months after their parents passed away.
pattern: S + argued over + N + for + 時間 + after + S + V
simple: They argued over every minor detail of the contract for hours.

chinese: 在…情況下出現、因…而產生
example: New challenges can arise upon the introduction of any major change within a large organisation.
pattern: N + can arise upon + N + within + N
simple: Opportunities arise upon those who are prepared and stay alert.

example: The event coordinator arranged for a professional photographer to capture every moment of the ceremony.
pattern: S + arranged for + N + to + V + N
simple: Can you arrange for a car to pick us up at seven?

example: The manager needed to assert authority over the disruptive situation before it escalated any further.
pattern: S + needed to assert + N + over + N + before + S + V
simple: He asserted control over the project after the previous manager resigned.

example: The volunteer team assisted with the distribution of emergency supplies to families affected by the flood.
pattern: S + assisted with + N + of + N + affected by + N
simple: Could you assist with setting up the equipment before the event?

chinese: 附上、依附於；對…產生依附感
example: Please attach to your application a copy of your most recent academic transcript and two references.
pattern: Please attach to + N + N + and + N
simple: Children often become deeply attached to comfort objects like stuffed animals.

example: He stood with his back against the wall and waited calmly as the crowd surged towards him.
pattern: S + stood with + N + back against + N + as + S + V
simple: She backed against the door to stop anyone from entering.

example: He decided to bail from the project at the last minute, leaving the rest of the team in a difficult position.
pattern: S + decided to bail from + N + at the last minute, leaving + 人 + in + N
simple: She bailed from the deal when she saw how risky it had become.

example: Finding a healthy balance between work and personal life is essential for long-term wellbeing.
pattern: V-ing + N + between + N + and + N + is + adj + for + N
simple: She tries to balance between her studies and her part-time job.

example: The two sides bargained over the terms of the contract for several weeks before reaching a final agreement.
pattern: S + bargained over + N + for + 時間 + before + V-ing
simple: Buyers and sellers often bargain over price at the local market.

example: She became acquainted with the local customs and traditions during her first year living abroad.
pattern: S + became acquainted with + N + during + N

pattern: As + S + V, it is + adj + for + 人 + to grow away from + 人

chinese: 硬撐過去、咬牙完成（艱難任務）
example: Despite feeling exhausted and ill, she gutted through the final week of rehearsals before opening night.
pattern: Despite + V-ing, S + gutted through + N + before + N
simple: You just have to gut through the difficult early stages and things get easier.

chinese: 拖拉進來；（警方）帶回審問
example: The fishing crew hauled in a surprisingly large catch despite the rough weather conditions that morning.
pattern: S + hauled in + N + despite + N + that + 時間
simple: The police hauled him in for questioning after new evidence emerged.

chinese: 強行帶走；（口語）用力揮打
example: Security guards hauled off the protester who had managed to get past the entrance barriers.
pattern: S + hauled off + 人 + who + V
simple: The broken sofa was hauled off to the dump by the removal team.

example: The temperature outside dropped sharply, so the children headed in from the garden before dinner.
pattern: N + dropped, so S + headed in from + N + before + N
simple: It's getting dark — let's head in and start making supper.

example: After weeks of broad research, the team finally honed in on the most promising solution to the problem.
pattern: After + 時間 + of + N, S + finally honed in on + N
simple: The detective honed in on the most suspicious detail in the witness statement.

chinese: 跳上、搭乘（交通工具）；迅速開始
example: She hopped on the first available bus and arrived at the venue just minutes before the show started.
pattern: S + hopped on + N + and + V + just before + N + V
simple: Just hop on the number five tram and get off at the central station.

chinese: 圍攏聚集在一起、擠在…周圍
example: The hikers huddled around the campfire to keep warm as the mountain temperature dropped after sunset.
pattern: S + huddled around + N + to + V + as + N + V
simple: The children huddled around the teacher to look at the illustrated book.

example: The tour guide hustled the group through the museum to make sure they caught their afternoon coach.
pattern: S + hustled + 人 + through + N + to + V
simple: She hustled through her morning routine to avoid being late again.

example: The editor inked out several sensitive paragraphs before the document was cleared for public release.
pattern: S + inked out + N + before + S + V
simple: He inked out the old address and wrote the correct one above it.

example: We spent the afternoon kicking around ideas for the new campaign before settling on one clear direction.
pattern: S + spent + 時間 + kicking around + N + before + V-ing
simple: Let's kick around a few options before we make any final decisions.

example: In the ceremony, each knight kneeled before the monarch to receive the honour of being knighted.
pattern: S + kneeled before + N + to + V
simple: He refused to kneel before anyone, no matter how powerful they were.

chinese: 緊緊捆綁在…上；猛力抓住
example: The sailors lashed the cargo onto the deck with thick ropes before the storm fully hit the vessel.
pattern: S + lashed + N + onto + N + with + N + before + S + V
simple: Lash the tent poles onto the backpack so they don't slide out.

example: The debate moderator was surprised when one candidate suddenly launched a sharp personal attack at the other.
pattern: S + was surprised when + S + launched + N + at + 人
simple: The dog launched itself at the intruder the moment it sensed danger.

example: Instead of avoiding the criticism, she leaned into it and used the feedback to improve her performance.
pattern: Instead of + V-ing, S + leaned into + N + and + V
simple: When the work got hard, he leaned into the challenge rather than stepping back.

example: She enrolled in an advanced course to level up her data analysis skills and increase her market value.
pattern: S + enrolled in + N + to level up + N + and + V
simple: If you want to level up in your career, continuous learning is essential.

example: While reviewing old research notes, the scientist lit on an overlooked variable that changed everything.
pattern: While + V-ing, S + lit on + N + that + V
simple: She lit on the perfect title for her novel while reading in the bath.

chinese: 裝載、大量載入；盡情取用
example: The volunteers loaded up the van with supplies before setting off for the flood-affected communities.
pattern: S + loaded up + N + with + N + before + V-ing
simple: Load up your plate — there is plenty of food for everyone.

example: A truly open-minded person can look past surface differences and appreciate the value in everyone.
pattern: S + can look past + N + and + V + N + in + 人
simple: Try to look past his awkward manner — he genuinely means well.

chinese: 引誘進入、誘騙（某人）去做
example: The fraudulent website lured unsuspecting users into entering their personal banking details.
pattern: N + lured + 人 + into + V-ing + N
simple: Don't be lured into making a hasty decision by a limited-time offer.

chinese: 規劃、逐步梳理（流程或路線）
example: The project manager mapped through every phase of the development process to identify potential bottlenecks.
pattern: S + mapped through + N + to + V + N
simple: Let's map through the user journey before we start designing the interface.

chinese: 達到極限、用盡（信用額度或能力）
example: He had maxed out three credit cards by the end of the year and was struggling to pay the minimum balances.
pattern: S + had maxed out + N + by + N + and + V-ing
simple: Don't max out your energy in the first half — save some for the finish.

example: After soaking in the warm bath for twenty minutes, all her tension and stress seemed to melt away.
pattern: After + V-ing + for + 時間, N + seemed to melt away
simple: With good music playing, the hours seem to melt away effortlessly.

example: Overwhelmed by the pressure of the final exams, the student completely melted down in the library.
pattern: Overwhelmed by + N, S + completely melted down + 地點
simple: The child melted down in the supermarket when he was told he couldn't have a toy.

chinese: 胡鬧、虛度時間；戲弄（某人）
example: Stop messing around and focus on finishing the report — the deadline is only two hours away.
pattern: Stop messing around and + V — N + is only + 時間 + away
simple: He was just messing around with a guitar when he accidentally wrote a hit song.

example: He completely messed up the presentation by forgetting to save the latest version of the slides.
pattern: S + completely messed up + N + by + V-ing + N
simple: Sorry I messed up — I'll make sure it doesn't happen again.

chinese: 錯過（機會或好事）、未能把握
example: By leaving the conference early, she missed on the chance to network with several key industry figures.
pattern: By + V-ing, S + missed on + N + to + V
simple: Don't miss on this limited opportunity to apply for the scholarship.

example: She spent the entire weekend mulling over the job offer before finally deciding to accept it.
pattern: S + spent + 時間 + mulling over + N + before + V-ing
simple: Take some time to mull over the proposal before giving your answer.

chinese: 靠意志力硬撐過去、強行完成
example: Despite the injury, the athlete muscled through the final round and secured a place on the podium.
pattern: Despite + N, S + muscled through + N + and + V
simple: We just have to muscle through the difficult part and things will get easier.

example: The reviewer noted upon several significant inconsistencies in the data that required further investigation.
pattern: S + noted upon + N + that + V
simple: The professor noted upon the student's exceptional use of evidence in the essay.

chinese: 輕輕推動朝向、引導走向
example: The counsellor gently nudged the student towards considering careers that matched her natural strengths.
pattern: S + gently nudged + 人 + towards + V-ing + N
simple: Good design can nudge people towards making healthier food choices.

example: The large French doors open onto a terrace with a breathtaking view of the valley below.
pattern: N + opens onto + N + with + N
simple: The kitchen opens onto a small courtyard where the family eats in summer.

example: Employees can opt into the company's flexible working scheme by submitting a written request to HR.
pattern: S + can opt into + N + by + V-ing + N
simple: Users must actively opt into receiving marketing emails.

chinese: 被…所淹沒、深受…所影響（情感）
example: When her name was announced as the winner, she was overcome with joy and burst into tears.
pattern: When + S + V, S + was overcome with + N + and + V
simple: He was overcome with grief when he heard the devastating news.

chinese: 充滿、洋溢著（情感或事物）
example: Her heart overflowed with gratitude as she accepted the award on behalf of her entire team.
pattern: N + overflowed with + N + as + S + V
simple: The venue was overflowing with excited fans long before the concert began.

chinese: 配對成雙、（讓人）兩兩分組
example: The dance instructor asked students to pair off with a partner before the lesson officially began.
pattern: S + asked + 人 + to pair off + before + S + V
simple: The students paired off to rehearse their dialogue for the oral exam.

example: The teacher encouraged shy students to pair up with more outgoing classmates for the group project.
pattern: S + encouraged + 人 + to pair up with + 人 + for + N
simple: Why don't we pair up and tackle this problem together?

chinese: （計畫或情況）發展順利、結果如何
example: The business partnership did not pan out as hoped, and both parties agreed to go their separate ways.
pattern: N + did not pan out as + p.p., and + S + V
simple: Nobody knew how the negotiations would pan out in the end.

chinese: 剝落、逐層揭去；脫離（群體）
example: Over time, the old paint began to peel away from the exterior walls of the historic building.
pattern: Over time, N + began to peel away from + N
simple: One by one, supporters began to peel away from the failing campaign.

chinese: 剝去（外層）；脫去（衣物）；分離
example: After the long run, she peeled off her sweaty clothes and stepped straight into a hot shower.
pattern: After + N, S + peeled off + N + and + V
simple: Peel off the protective film before using the screen for the first time.

example: The journalist peppered the minister with sharp questions about the handling of the public health crisis.
pattern: S + peppered + 人 + with + N + about + N
simple: Her speech was peppered with humorous anecdotes that kept the audience engaged.

example: The construction project will be phased through three distinct stages over the next two years.
pattern: N + will be phased through + N + over + 時間
simple: New staff are phased through an induction programme during their first month.

example: Everyone pitched in to help clean up the community park after the weekend festival had ended.
pattern: S + pitched in to + V + N + after + N + V
simple: If everyone pitches in, we can finish this in half the time.

example: The tense final minutes of the championship game played out in silence as the whole stadium held its breath.
pattern: N + played out in + N + as + S + V
simple: Nobody could predict how the dramatic events would play out in the end.

chinese: 突然出現；快速外出一趟
example: She popped out to the corner shop during her lunch break to grab a sandwich and some fruit.
pattern: S + popped out to + N + during + N + to + V
simple: Sorry I missed your call — I just popped out to get some fresh air.

example: It is considered rude to pry into someone's personal finances or family relationships uninvited.
pattern: It is + adj + to pry into + N + or + N
simple: The investigation team pried into the company's financial records for weeks.

example: The toddler pulled along her favourite toy elephant as she waddled across the living room floor.
pattern: S + pulled along + N + as + S + V
simple: She managed to pull the whole team along with her infectious enthusiasm.

example: Despite the exhausting terrain and fading light, the expedition team pushed on towards the summit.
pattern: Despite + N + and + N, S + pushed on towards + N
simple: It's getting late but we need to push on if we want to finish tonight.

chinese: 累積、大量積累（得分、債務等）
example: The startup racked up enormous debts in its first two years before finally turning a profit.
pattern: S + racked up + N + before + V-ing + N
simple: She racked up an impressive number of victories in her first season.

example: The blockbuster film raked in over two hundred million dollars in its opening weekend worldwide.
pattern: S + raked in + N + in + N
simple: That online course is raking in thousands of new subscribers every week.

example: The child rattled off the entire list of capital cities without pausing once, impressing her teacher.
pattern: S + rattled off + N + without + V-ing, V-ing + N
simple: She rattled off a long list of reasons why the plan would not work.

example: The salesperson reeled in three major clients in one week through a combination of charm and persistence.
pattern: S + reeled in + N + through + N + and + N
simple: The discount offer reeled in a huge number of new customers.

chinese: 滔滔不絕地說出、連續列舉
example: The expert reeled off a series of impressive statistics to support her argument during the debate.
pattern: S + reeled off + N + to + V + N + during + N
simple: He can reel off the names of every player in the tournament from memory.

example: Queen Victoria reigned over the British Empire for over sixty years, one of the longest in history.
pattern: S + reigned over + N + for + 時間, one of the + 最高級 + in + N
simple: The champion has reigned over the sport for nearly a decade.

example: The central bank moved to rein in inflation by raising interest rates for the third consecutive month.
pattern: S + moved to rein in + N + by + V-ing + N
simple: The manager tried to rein in spending after the budget overran significantly.

chinese: （習慣或特質）感染、影響到他人；擦去
example: Her positive attitude and boundless energy seemed to rub off on everyone around her over time.
pattern: N + seemed to rub off on + 人 + over time
simple: I hope some of his discipline and focus will rub off on the rest of the team.

example: The ancient king ruled over a vast territory that stretched from the mountains to the sea.
pattern: S + ruled over + N + that + V
simple: Fear should never be used to rule over others in a healthy organisation.

example: The software company rushed out a patch to fix the critical security vulnerability before more users were affected.
pattern: S + rushed out + N + to + V + before + S + V
simple: She rushed out of the meeting to take an urgent call from her client.

example: The family scraped together every spare coin they had to pay for their youngest daughter's school trip.
pattern: S + scraped together + N + to + V + N
simple: We barely scraped together enough money to cover the repair costs.

chinese: 敲詐勒索；徹底搜查；逐漸磨合
example: The corrupt officers were arrested after evidence emerged that they had been shaking down local business owners.
pattern: S + were arrested after + N + V + that + S + had been + V-ing + N
simple: Give the new system a week to shake down before making any major changes.

chinese: 一笑置之、不當一回事；擺脫
example: Despite the harsh public criticism, the director shrugged off the negative reviews and continued with her vision.
pattern: Despite + N, S + shrugged off + N + and + V
simple: He shrugged off the minor injury and carried on playing until the final whistle.

chinese: 迴避、巧妙繞過（問題或責任）
example: The politician cleverly sidestepped the journalist's question by redirecting the conversation to a different topic.
pattern: S + sidestepped + N + by + V-ing + N
simple: She always finds a way to sidestep responsibility when things go wrong.

chinese: 平靜下來、冷靜（激動情緒）
example: After a heated argument, both sides were asked to simmer down before continuing the discussion.
pattern: After + N, S + were asked to simmer down + before + V-ing
simple: Give him a few minutes to simmer down before you try talking to him again.

example: She skimmed over the lengthy report and picked out only the key figures she needed for the meeting.
pattern: S + skimmed over + N + and + V + N
simple: Don't just skim over the instructions — there are important details at the end.

example: The presenter skipped over the technical details to keep the audience engaged and focused on the main message.
pattern: S + skipped over + N + to + V + N + and + V
simple: Feel free to skip over any sections that are not relevant to your situation.

chinese: 突然振作起來、從（消極狀態）中甦醒
example: Her friends encouraged her to snap out of her sadness and take the first step towards rebuilding her life.
pattern: S + encouraged + 人 + to snap out of + N + and + V
simple: You need to snap out of this negativity and start focusing on solutions.

chinese: 大聲表達意見、暢所欲言（有時帶抱怨意味）
example: The public forum gave residents the chance to sound off about the proposed highway expansion in their area.
pattern: N + gave + 人 + the chance to sound off about + N
simple: He is always sounding off about how the company is being mismanaged.

chinese: 溢出；（問題或情緒）蔓延擴散
example: The conflict in the border region began to spill over into neighbouring countries, alarming world leaders.
pattern: N + began to spill over into + N, V-ing + N
simple: Stress from work can easily spill over into your personal relationships.

chinese: 衍生出；（公司）分拆成獨立部門
example: The popular television series spun off two new shows that attracted an entirely different demographic of viewers.
pattern: N + spun off + N + that + V + N
simple: The research led to a technology that was spun off into a successful new company.

chinese: 擠出時間安排；勉強擠入
example: The doctor managed to squeeze in one last appointment before the clinic closed for the evening.
pattern: S + managed to squeeze in + N + before + S + V
simple: Can you squeeze in a quick meeting between two and three this afternoon?

chinese: 讓步退下；（軍事）解除戒備；辭職
example: The chairman announced he would stand down from his position at the end of the financial year.
pattern: S + announced S + would stand down from + N + at the end of + N
simple: The troops were ordered to stand down after the ceasefire was declared.

example: A wave of excitement swept through the crowd when the legendary performer finally appeared on stage.
pattern: N + swept through + N + when + S + V
simple: A cold wind swept through the valley as the storm approached from the north.

example: I'll swing by the supermarket on my way home and pick up whatever we need for tonight's dinner.
pattern: S + will swing by + N + on the way + and + V
simple: Can you swing by my place and drop off those documents before noon?

example: Enthusiasm for the new product tailed off sharply after the initial wave of media coverage died down.
pattern: N + tailed off after + N + V
simple: His voice tailed off as he struggled to find the right words to continue.

example: The emergency fund was set up to tide small businesses over during periods of unexpected financial hardship.
pattern: N + was set up to tide + 人 + over during + N
simple: Could you lend me some money to tide me over until I get paid?

example: The editor asked the writer to tone down some of the more inflammatory language before the article was published.
pattern: S + asked + 人 + to tone down + N + before + S + V
simple: You might want to tone down your remarks before presenting to senior management.

chinese: 隨意討論（想法）；拋來拋去
example: The creative team spent an hour tossing around ideas for the new advertising campaign without reaching a conclusion.
pattern: S + spent + 時間 + tossing around + N + without + V-ing
simple: Let's toss around a few ideas before we commit to a final direction.

example: The keynote speaker touched upon several important issues but did not explore any of them in great depth.
pattern: S + touched upon + N + but + did not + V + in + N
simple: The report touches upon environmental concerns but focuses mainly on economic data.

chinese: 逐漸向下傳遞、滴流而下
example: Economists debate whether wealth created at the top of society truly trickles down to benefit those at the bottom.
pattern: S + debate whether + N + truly trickles down to + V + 人
simple: It takes time for new policies to trickle down to affect everyday life.

example: The accused maintained that the charges against him had been trumped up by his political rivals.
pattern: S + maintained that + N + had been trumped up by + N
simple: The whole scandal appeared to be trumped up to damage her reputation.

example: Three hours of swimming in the sea completely tuckered out the young children before lunchtime.
pattern: 時間 + of + V-ing + completely tuckered out + 人 + before + N
simple: All that gardening has tuckered me out — I need a rest.

example: The driver veered off the main road to avoid a large pothole and ended up in a ditch.
pattern: S + veered off + N + to + V + N + and + V
simple: The conversation veered off topic as soon as someone mentioned the merger.

chinese: 逐步削減、縮小（數量或範圍）
example: After three rounds of interviews, the panel had whittled down the list of candidates to just five finalists.
pattern: After + N + of + N, S + had whittled down + N + to + N
simple: We need to whittle down our options before the budget meeting on Friday.

example: Without regular investment and innovation, even the strongest industries can wither away over time.
pattern: Without + N, even + N + can wither away + over time
simple: Old traditions wither away when they are no longer passed on to younger generations.

example: After months of broad analysis, the researchers finally zeroed in on the specific gene responsible for the condition.
pattern: After + 時間 + of + N, S + finally zeroed in on + N
simple: Investigators zeroed in on the suspect after reviewing the security footage.

example: The legal team was highly confident that the evidence would be enough to win the case in court.
pattern: S + was highly confident that + S + would + V
simple: She was highly confident in her ability to handle the new responsibilities.

chinese: 沉穩自信、不動聲色地充滿信心
example: Despite the fierce competition, the young athlete appeared quietly confident as she waited for the race to begin.
pattern: Despite + N, S + appeared quietly confident + as + S + V
simple: He was quietly confident that his hard work would eventually be recognised.

example: The veteran negotiator walked into the room supremely confident, having prepared for every possible scenario.
pattern: S + walked into + N + supremely confident, having + p.p.
simple: She performed supremely confident on stage, as though she had done it a thousand times.

chinese: 相當有把握、有一定信心
example: We are reasonably confident that the project will be completed on time, provided no major problems arise.
pattern: S + are reasonably confident that + S + will + V, provided + S + V
simple: I'm reasonably confident I passed the exam, but I won't know for certain until next week.

chinese: 謹慎樂觀、小心翼翼地有信心
example: The doctors were cautiously confident about the patient's recovery after the first round of treatment showed positive results.
pattern: S + were cautiously confident about + N + after + N + V
simple: The team is cautiously confident going into the second half of the season.

example: She felt far more confident after practising her speech in front of a mirror several times.
pattern: S + felt far more confident after + V-ing + N
simple: You'll feel more confident once you've done it a few times.

example: Even if you are nervous, try to sound confident when you speak so the audience trusts what you say.
pattern: Even if + S + V, try to sound confident so + S + V
simple: She always sounds confident in meetings, even when she is unsure about the details.

example: Standing tall and making eye contact are two simple ways to look confident in any professional setting.
pattern: V-ing and V-ing are ways to look confident in + N
simple: Dress well and smile — it helps you look confident even when you don't feel it.

example: Despite a series of disappointing setbacks, the coach urged the players to remain confident in their abilities.
pattern: Despite + N, S + urged + 人 + to remain confident in + N
simple: It is important to remain confident even when things do not go as planned.

example: As the weeks went by, the new intern grew increasingly confident in handling client enquiries on her own.
pattern: As + S + V, S + grew increasingly confident in + V-ing
simple: Children grow more confident when they are praised for their efforts.

example: With the right support and encouragement, even the shyest students can become confident public speakers.
pattern: With + N + and + N, even + N + can become confident + N
simple: She became more confident after joining the drama club at school.

example: He seemed confident on the outside, but his close friends knew he was deeply worried about the outcome.
pattern: S + seemed confident on the outside, but + S + knew + S + V
simple: She seemed confident during the interview, but admitted afterwards she was terrified.

example: The board of directors was fully confident in the new CEO's ability to lead the company through the crisis.
pattern: S + was fully confident in + N + to + V
simple: You should feel confident in your own judgement after all the experience you have gained.

chinese: 對…感到有把握（事情或結果）
example: After months of preparation, the team felt genuinely confident about their chances of winning the regional championship.
pattern: After + 時間 + of + N, S + felt confident about + N
simple: Are you confident about the answers you gave in the written exam?

example: The prosecution team was confident of securing a conviction given the volume of evidence presented.
pattern: S + was confident of + V-ing + N + given + N
simple: We are confident of reaching our sales target by the end of the quarter.

chinese: 自信的、對自己有信心的
example: Growing up in a supportive environment helped her become a self-confident and independent young woman.
pattern: V-ing helped + 人 + become + adj + and + adj + N
simple: Being self-confident does not mean being arrogant — there is an important difference.

example: She walked into the interview room with a confident smile and immediately put the panel at ease.
pattern: S + walked into + N + with + N + and + V
simple: He gave a confident smile before stepping up to take the penalty kick.

chinese: 自信的舉止、自信的態度
example: The new manager addressed the team in a confident manner that quickly earned their respect and trust.
pattern: S + addressed + N + in a confident manner + that + V
simple: Presenting yourself in a confident manner can make a strong first impression.

chinese: 充滿自信的聲音、語氣自信
example: Speaking in a confident voice is just as important as having strong content when delivering a presentation.
pattern: V-ing in a confident voice is just as + adj + as + V-ing + N
simple: She answered every question in a confident voice, showing she truly knew the subject.

chinese: 有把握的預測、自信的預言
example: The analyst made a confident prediction that the company's shares would rise sharply by the end of the year.
pattern: S + made a confident prediction that + S + would + V
simple: It is hard to make confident predictions about how the economy will perform next year.

example: The experienced manager handled the tense situation calmly and prevented it from escalating further.
pattern: S + handled the situation + adv + and + V
simple: How you handle the situation will determine how others perceive your leadership.

chinese: 處理危機、應對突發事件
example: The government was widely criticised for its failure to handle the public health crisis swiftly and transparently.
pattern: S + was criticised for + N + to handle + N + adv
simple: A good leader must know how to handle a crisis without causing widespread panic.

example: Learning how to handle conflict constructively is one of the most valuable skills in any workplace.
pattern: V-ing how to handle conflict + adv + is + N
simple: HR managers are trained to handle conflict between employees fairly and professionally.

example: Staff are trained to handle customer complaints politely and to offer a solution within twenty-four hours.
pattern: S + are trained to handle + N + adv + and to + V + within + 時間
simple: The way a company handles a complaint can either damage or strengthen customer loyalty.

example: Different people handle stress in very different ways, and what works for one person may not work for another.
pattern: S + handle stress in + adj + ways, and what + V + may not + V
simple: Exercise is one of the most effective ways to handle stress in daily life.

chinese: 應付工作量、處理工作負擔
example: After two colleagues resigned, the remaining staff struggled to handle the increased workload on their own.
pattern: After + S + V, S + struggled to handle + N + on their own
simple: New software was introduced to help the team handle the workload more efficiently.

example: As she gained experience, she became capable of handling a much wider range of responsibilities independently.
pattern: As + S + V, S + became capable of handling + N + adv
simple: Promotion means you will need to handle more responsibilities than before.

example: The ability to handle criticism gracefully and learn from it is a sign of true professional maturity.
pattern: The ability to handle criticism + adv + is + N
simple: Not everyone finds it easy to handle criticism without becoming defensive.

example: How a person handles setbacks reveals far more about their character than how they handle success.
pattern: How S + handles setbacks reveals more about N than how S + handles + N
simple: Learning to handle setbacks with a positive mindset is essential for long-term success.

example: Organisations that handle change well tend to outperform those that resist it over the long term.
pattern: N + that handle change well tend to + V + those that + V
simple: Some people handle change better than others because of their mindset and experience.

chinese: 堅持立場、採取強硬態度；踩油門
example: When her teenage son kept staying out past midnight, she finally put her foot down and set a strict curfew.
pattern: When + S + kept + V-ing, S + finally put her foot down and + V
simple: Sometimes you just have to put your foot down and say no.

chinese: 說錯話、失言、無意中冒犯
example: He really put his foot in it when he asked his colleague when her baby was due — she wasn't pregnant.
pattern: S + put his/her foot in it when + S + V
simple: I really put my foot in it by mentioning his ex-girlfriend at the party.

example: It took her a few months to find her feet in the new city, but she eventually settled in and made good friends.
pattern: It took + 人 + 時間 + to find their feet, but S + eventually + V
simple: Give the new employee some time to find his feet before judging his performance.

example: The opposition party accused the government of dragging its feet on the long-promised education reforms.
pattern: S + accused + 人 + of dragging their feet on + N
simple: Stop dragging your feet — we need to submit the application by Friday.

phrase: stand on your own two feet
example: Moving abroad at the age of twenty-two forced her to stand on her own two feet for the very first time.
pattern: V-ing forced + 人 + to stand on their own two feet + for the first time
simple: Parents should encourage their children to stand on their own two feet from an early age.

chinese: 令人神魂顛倒、深深迷住某人
example: He swept her completely off her feet with his charm, humour, and unexpected romantic gestures.
pattern: S + swept + 人 + off their feet with + N, N, and + N
simple: She was swept off her feet by the stunning scenery when she first arrived in the Alps.

example: After the flood damage, the insurance company refused to foot the bill for the full cost of repairs.
pattern: S + refused to foot the bill for + N
simple: Who is going to foot the bill for the company's new office renovation?

phrase: get off on the right foot
chinese: 有個好的開始、開局順利
example: Arriving early and being well-prepared helped her get off on the right foot with the new team.
pattern: V-ing and V-ing helped + 人 + get off on the right foot with + N
simple: It is important to get off on the right foot when starting any new relationship.

phrase: get off on the wrong foot
chinese: 開局不順、一開始就出師不利
example: The new neighbours got off on the wrong foot when their loud party kept everyone awake on the first night.
pattern: S + got off on the wrong foot when + S + V
simple: They got off on the wrong foot at the first meeting and never quite recovered.

chinese: 全力以赴、盡力給人好印象
example: Whatever challenges you face, always put your best foot forward and give everything your full effort.
pattern: Whatever + N + S + V, always put your best foot forward and + V
simple: Make sure you put your best foot forward during the job interview tomorrow.

example: She had never set foot in a courtroom before and felt overwhelmed the moment she walked through the doors.
pattern: S + had never set foot in + N + before and + V + the moment + S + V
simple: He vowed never to set foot in that restaurant again after the terrible experience.

chinese: 笨手笨腳、毫無舞蹈天分
example: He laughed and admitted he had two left feet when his partner invited him to join the salsa class.
pattern: S + admitted S + had two left feet when + S + V
simple: Don't worry if you have two left feet — salsa dancing is all about practice.

example: After three weeks in hospital, she was finally back on her feet and ready to return to work.
pattern: After + 時間 + in + N, S + was finally back on her feet and + adj
simple: The business was back on its feet within a year of the economic downturn.

example: With the children home all day during the holidays, she found it hard to get anything done with them under her feet.
pattern: S + found it + adj + to + V + with + 人 + under her feet
simple: The puppy was always under her feet whenever she tried to cook dinner.

example: The small village nestled at the foot of the mountain had been home to the same families for generations.
pattern: N + nestled at the foot of + N + had been + N + for + N
simple: Please sign your name at the foot of the page before submitting the form.

chinese: 忘記初衷、失去目標；看不見
example: In the pursuit of profit, many companies lose sight of the values that made them successful in the first place.
pattern: In the pursuit of + N, S + lose sight of + N + that + V
simple: Don't lose sight of your original goal when things get difficult.

example: From a young age, she had set her sights on becoming the first person in her family to attend university.
pattern: From a young age, S + had set their sights on + V-ing
simple: He has set his sights on winning a gold medal at the next Olympic Games.

example: After hours of hiking through dense forest, the mountain peak finally came into sight and lifted everyone's spirits.
pattern: After + 時間 + of + V-ing, N + finally came into sight and + V
simple: The rescue ship came into sight just as the survivors had begun to lose hope.

chinese: 從視線中消失、不見蹤影
example: The small boat disappeared from sight within minutes as the thick sea fog rolled in from the coast.
pattern: N + disappeared from sight within + 時間 + as + S + V
simple: He walked away quickly and disappeared from sight around the corner.

chinese: 認得臉、見過面但不熟識
example: I know her by sight from the neighbourhood, but we have never actually been formally introduced.
pattern: S + know + 人 + by sight, but + S + have never + p.p.
simple: Most people in the village know each other by sight even if they don't speak regularly.

chinese: 在視線範圍內；即將到來
example: After years of negotiations, a peaceful resolution to the long-running dispute finally appeared to be in sight.
pattern: After + 時間 + of + N, N + finally appeared to be in sight
simple: There is no end in sight to the delays caused by the construction work.

example: At first sight, the problem seemed straightforward, but closer examination revealed several hidden complications.
pattern: At first sight, N + seemed + adj, but + V + N
simple: They fell in love at first sight and were married within the year.

example: The little girl burst into tears at the sight of the large dog blocking the entrance to the park.
pattern: S + V + at the sight of + N
simple: He fainted at the sight of blood and had to be helped out of the room.

chinese: 在…的視野範圍內；接近達成
example: The climbers were within sight of the summit when a sudden storm forced them to turn back.
pattern: S + were within sight of + N + when + S + V
simple: She was within sight of completing her degree when she had to take a year out.

chinese: 超出…的視線範圍、看不見…
example: The children were instructed to stay out of sight of the road whenever they were playing in the front garden.
pattern: S + were instructed to stay out of sight of + N + whenever + S + V
simple: Make sure the cables are kept out of sight of the main entrance.

example: The thousands of fireflies lighting up the forest at dusk was a truly breathtaking sight none of them would forget.
pattern: N + was a breathtaking sight + none of + 人 + would + V
simple: The aurora borealis filling the night sky was a breathtaking sight.

chinese: 罕見的景象、難得一見的景色
example: Snow in this part of the country is a rare sight, so the children ran outside excitedly the moment it began to fall.
pattern: N + is a rare sight, so + S + V + the moment + S + V
simple: Seeing all three generations of the family together was a rare sight indeed.

chinese: 熟悉的景象、常見的畫面
example: The old man feeding pigeons in the square every morning had become a familiar sight to all the locals.
pattern: N + had become a familiar sight to + 人
simple: Commuters in heavy rain gear have become a familiar sight during the monsoon season.

chinese: 可憐的景象、令人心酸的畫面
example: After the storm, the once beautiful garden was a sorry sight, with broken branches and uprooted flowers everywhere.
pattern: After + N, N + was a sorry sight, with + N + everywhere
simple: He was a sorry sight, soaking wet and shivering after being caught in the downpour.

chinese: 第六感、超自然預知能力
example: Villagers believed the old woman possessed second sight and would travel from far away to seek her advice.
pattern: S + believed + N + possessed second sight + and + would + V
simple: Some people claim to have second sight and can predict events before they happen.
